segments.s653-g937: کسی نیست بی‌آرزو درجهان is a compact existential clause. کسی نیست = 'there is no one' (کسی + نیست, negative existential), بی‌آرزو is an adjective phrase 'without desire' modifying the (implicit) person, and درجهان = 'in the world' (prepositional phrase). Word order places the negated existential before descriptive phrases, typical of Persian poetry.
segments.s653-g938: اگر آشکارست و‌گر در نهان is a conditional/contrastive clause clarifying the previous claim. اگر (if) + آشکارست ('is apparent' — آشکار + است fused into one word) gives 'if it is manifest', و‌گر = 'or if / but if' introduces the alternative, در نهان = 'in hiddenness / concealed'. Note the fused verb آشکارست uses the old poetic contraction of 'آشکار است'.
segments.s653-g939: همان آرزو را پدیدست راه shows poetic inversion. همان آرزو را = 'that very desire' with را marking it as definite/object-emphatic; پدیدست is an existential/appearance verb ('appears' / 'is revealed'), and راه is the predicate noun 'a way' — literally 'a way appears (is revealed): that desire'. In normal prose the order would be راه پدیدست همان آرزو را.
segments.s653-g940: که پیدا کند مرد را دستگاه is a relative clause (که = 'which/that') modifying راه. پیدا کند = پیدا (found/bring forth) + کند (do) — here 'پیدا کردن' = 'to obtain/secure' (پیدا used in the sense 'bring about/get'). مرد را is the affected person (را marks the definite object/beneficiary), and دستگاه = 'station/position/court' (an outcome obtained). The clause means 'that (way) which secures for a man his rank/station.'
segments.s653-g941: کدامین ره آید تو را سودمند is an interrogative clause. کدامین = 'which', ره = 'path/way', آید (present/impersonal 'comes') here functions like 'suits/becomes' or 'will be' in English; تو را uses را to mark the person concerned (dative-like emphasis), and سودمند = 'useful/beneficial' as predicate. Literally: 'Which way will come (be) useful to you?'
segments.s653-g942: کدامست با درد و رنج و گزند is a coordinating question that asks for the contrasting alternative. کدامست = 'which is it?' (fusion of کدام + است), با = 'with', followed by a series of nouns (درد, رنج, گزند) listing negative accompaniments: 'pain, suffering, and harm'. The structure asks 'which (path) is the one accompanied by pain, suffering, and harm?'
segments.s653-g943: چنین داد پاسخ که راه از دو سوست is the reporting clause and first part of the answer. چنین = 'thus/so', داد = past-tense 'gave' (here 'he gave'), پاسخ = 'answer', که introduces the content ('that'), راه = 'the way', از = 'of/from', دو سوست = 'is of two sides' (دو سو = 'two directions/sides' with the poetic ending -ست = 'is'). Note سوست is a fused poetic form meaning 'is two-sided'; the verb 'است' is attached.
segments.s653-g944: گذشتن تو را تا کدام آرزوست completes the answer and gives the condition. گذشتن is the infinitive/masdar 'to go/pass' used nominally ('which way one goes'), تو را marks the person concerned, تا here means 'depending on / as far as' (i.e., 'as far as which wish is concerned'), and کدام آرزوست = 'which desire it is' (آرزو + است fused). Together: 'which of the two you traverse depends on which desire you have.'
segments.s653-g433: چنین گفت is a formulaic phrase 'thus said (he)'. کان کو is a classical relative phrase 'he who' (کان + کو), and بود بردبار is a past-tense copula + predicate: بود ('was') + بردبار ('patient'). Together: 'Thus he said: he who was patient...'.
segments.s653-g434: به نزدیک is an adverbial phrase 'near/nearby'. اومرد here functions as a past action 'came/approached' (poetic/archaic verbal form), and بی‌شرم خوار are adjectival qualifiers 'shameless, despised' describing the person who arrived: 'a shameless, despised man came near.'
segments.s653-g435: دگر گفت 'again said'. کان کو as before is 'he who'. نجوید is present (classical) with negative prefix ن- + جوید 'seeks', meaning 'does not seek'; گزند is the object 'harm'. So: 'Again he said: he who does not seek harm...'.
segments.s653-g436: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. خوها is the plural 'habits/desires'. کدامش 'which one of them' is the interrogative subject, and بود سودمند uses بود (here past/modal copula) + سودمند 'beneficial/useful': 'which of his desires would be beneficial?'.
segments.s653-g437: بگفت is a narrative/perfect 'he said'. آنک is a relative 'he who/that which'. مغزش is مغز 'brain' + possessive suffix -ش 'his'. نجوشد is negative present/subjunctive form (ن- + جوشد) meaning 'does not boil/does not seethe', and زخشم reads as ز + خشم 'from anger'. So: 'he whose brain does not seethe from anger...'.
segments.s653-g438: بخوابد is a jussive/subjunctive 3sg 'may ... sleep / let ... be asleep'. بخشم here is written fused but functions adverbially as 'in/with anger' (poetic contraction of به خشم); از introduces the agent/source 'from', گنهکار 'sinner', and چشم 'eye' (i.e. 'in the sinner's eye'). The clause means roughly 'may wrath lie asleep in the sinner's eye.'
segments.s653-g439: دگر گفت as before 'again said'. کان is the classical particle used in relative clauses ('that/which' or as part of 'he who'); چیست‌ای is a fused form of چیست + ای (vocative) meaning 'what is, O...' and هوشمند addresses the addressee 'O intelligent one / O wise man'.
segments.s653-g440: که introduces the subordinate clause 'that/which'. آید is the present 3sg 'comes / is fitting'. خردمند را uses را as a dative/beneficiary marker 'to the wise (man)'. آن پسند is the predicate 'that which is pleasing' (آن = that, پسند = liking/approval). Literally: 'that which comes pleasing to the wise.'
segments.s653-g1177: 'برین داستان برسخن ساختم' is a compact prepositional + noun + prepositional phrase + verb. 'برین' is the fused preposition 'بر' + demonstrative 'این' ('on/onto this'), so 'برین داستان' = 'this tale.' 'برسخن' similarly fuses 'بر' + 'سخن' and here marks the medium 'in/into speech' (literally 'upon speech'). 'ساختم' is first person singular past of ساختن used in the sense 'I composed / I put (into a form)'. Word order is topical: the phrase specifying what was composed precedes the verb for metrical/poetic rhythm.
segments.s653-g1178: In 'به مهبود دستور پرداختم', 'به' functions as an agent/ablative marker meaning 'by/at the order of'; 'مهبود' is a proper/name-like epithet ('the moon-like / beloved one'). 'دستور' is the object 'order, command.' 'پرداختم' is first person singular past of پرداختن used here in the sense 'I attended to / carried out / executed.' The whole clause means 'I carried out (it) by Mahbud's order' — the word order is inverted for poetic effect.
segments.s653-g1179: 'میاسای ز آموختن یک زمان' begins with میاسای, an imperative/optative meaning 'be at rest / cease (for a while)'. It is followed by 'ز' + infinitive 'آموختن' meaning 'from learning/teaching' (here 'from learning'), and 'یک زمان' gives duration 'for a time / awhile.' The verb is addressed to the listener: 'Rest from learning for a while.'
segments.s653-g1180: 'ز دانش میفگن دل اندرگمان' literally 'from/ because of knowledge they cast the heart into doubt.' 'ز دانش' gives the cause/source ('from knowledge' or 'through knowledge'). 'میفگن' is an archaic/poetic third-person plural present of 'فکندن/انداختن' meaning 'they throw/cast/put.' 'دل اندرگمان' is the object phrase 'the heart into doubt.' The verb precedes its object here as is common in the poem's syntax.
segments.s653-g1181: 'چوگویی که' is a conditional/introductory phrase meaning 'if/when you say that...'. 'فام خرد' is a compound noun 'fām-e kherad' = 'the trace/hint (fām) of wisdom (خرد)'. 'توختم' is an archaic first-person past verb (lemma given as an archaic form) — here it functions as 'I have (verb)ed' tied to 'فام خرد' and must be read as 'I have (lost/been deprived of / changed) the trace of wisdom' (the precise nuance of the archaic verb is uncertain from the lemma, but it is first-person past and links the clause to the following contrast).
segments.s653-g1182: 'همه هرچ بایستم آموختم' = 'I learned all/whatever I had to.' 'همه هرچ' = 'all / whatever', a generalizing phrase. 'بایستم' here is an older/formal verbal form expressing obligation or necessity (contextually 'that I must / that I had to'); in classical usage it behaves like a modal/auxiliary attached to the speaker. 'آموختم' is the past 'I learned.' The clause states the speaker learned whatever was required of him.
segments.s653-g1183: 'یکی نغز بازی کند روزگار' uses 'یکی' as an adverbial/indefinite 'once / at times / a certain (time)'; 'نغز بازی' is a noun phrase 'a clever/subtle trick/play' ('نغز' = delicate, clever; 'بازی' = play/trick). 'کند' is the present tense third-person singular of کردن 'to do/make'; 'روزگار' is the subject 'Fate/time.' Word order places the object phrase before the verb for rhythm: 'Fate plays a clever trick.'
segments.s653-g1184: 'که بنشاندت پیش آموزگار' is a subordinate/result clause introduced by 'که' ('so that/that'). 'بنشاندت' is a causative/past form with the second-person object suffix '-ت' meaning 'he/she/it places you / seats you.' 'پیش آموزگار' means 'before/in the presence of the teacher.' Together: 'so that it puts you before a teacher' (i.e., fate places you in a position to be taught).
segments.s653-g449: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase 'gave such an answer / answered thus' (چنین 'thus' + داد 'gave' + پاسخ 'answer'). The clause-final که introduces reported speech and بر شهریار ('to/for the king') marks the addressee of the reported utterance; Persian often places the verb phrase 'داد پاسخ' before the subordinate clause introduced by که.
segments.s653-g450: خردمند گوید that sequence is subject + verb in the narrative present ('the wise man says/said'); گوید is the poetic/historical-present form used to report speech. The following که introduces the content clause; in that clause the noun آهو is followed by the numeral/adjective چهار as the predicate (Persian can omit the copula: 'آهو چهار' = 'there are four gazelles' or 'the gazelle is four [kinds]').
segments.s653-g451: یکی آنک ... is an identificational phrase 'one [is he] who...': یکی 'one (of them)' + آنک relative 'who/that'. ترسد is a present/habitual third-person singular 'fears'; ز دشمن is the source/object of fear ('of/from the enemy'), and به جنگ specifies the circumstance 'in/at battle' (به + noun = 'in/into [a fight]').
segments.s653-g452: و دیگر که دارد دل ... continues the list: و 'and', دیگر 'another'. که introduces the relative clause; دارد دل is a possessive construction 'has a heart' (دارد = 'has'). از بخش تنگ is an ablative/qualifying expression 'of/with respect to giving (بخش) narrow' — i.e. 'stingy'; note adjective تنگ follows the noun it qualifies.
segments.s653-g453: دگر آنک introduces another member of the list ('another who...'). The sequence رای خردمند مرد can be read as an apposition 'a man of wise counsel' or 'whose counsel is wise' (رای 'counsel/opinion', خردمند 'wise', مرد 'man'); together they characterize the person referenced by آنک.
segments.s653-g454: به یک‌سو نهد is a verbal phrase 'sets/puts to one side' (به + یک‌سو 'to one side' + نهد 'he places/sets'). روز ننگ و نبرد ('the day of shame and battle') is the direct object: روز here is 'day/occasion' and the two nouns ننگ and نبرد are linked by و as coordinated objects of نهد.
segments.s653-g455: که باشد سرش پرشتاب is a relative clause 'who is (that) his head is full of haste': که 'who/that', باشد here is the present/subjunctive/habitual copular verb 'is' used narratively, سرش 'his head' uses enclitic ش for possession, and پرشتاب is an adjective 'full of haste/impetuous' placed after the possessed noun.
segments.s653-g456: نجوید به کار اندر آرام و خواب uses a negative-present verb نجوید (ن‑ negative prefix + جوید 'seeks') meaning 'does not seek'. The prepositional phrase به کار اندر ('in/for his work') uses poetic اندر for 'in', and آرام و خواب are the things not sought ('calm and sleep'), coordinated by و.
segments.s653-g1049: دگر هفته = 'the next/another week' (دگر before a time word gives 'the next' or 'another'). چون هور بفراخت is a temporal subordinate clause introduced by چون ('as/when'); هور here is poetic for the rising splendour or light, and بفراخت is a past (=perfective) verb meaning 'broke forth/was kindled' or 'rose'. Word order is topic (time) + conjunction + subject + verb, typical of classical Persian.
segments.s653-g1050: بیامد نشست is two verbs in sequence: بیامد 'came' (perfective past) followed by نشست 'sat' — the crown is the understood subject that 'came and sat'. از بر تخت عاج is a prepositional phrase giving location: از بر (literally 'from/on') + تخت 'throne' + عاج 'ivory' (adjective after noun in classical word-order-like apposition).
segments.s653-g1051: ابا here functions as a poetic/archaic form equivalent to با 'with' (used to introduce company/list of persons). نامور modifies موبدان: 'the renowned mobeds'. و ردان continues the list ('and the rādān' — an old word for certain senior functionaries/elders). Word order is 'with' + modifiers + nouns.
segments.s653-g1052: This is a coordinated noun phrase: جهاندار 'the sovereign' (a title) و بیدار دل 'and (those) wakeful‑hearted' — دل is 'heart' and works as a noun modified by بیدار, together forming a compound epithet; بخردان is a plural adjective/epithet 'wise/prudent ones' placed after the group it qualifies. The whole group lists august, prudent persons present.
segments.s653-g1053: همی‌خواست is an imperfective/past habitual or 'wanted/asked' (classical narrative form); ز ایشان = 'from them' (ز = 'from' in poetic register); جهاندارشاه is a compound title 'world‑ruling shah/king' serving as the object of همی‌خواست. Word order: verb + prepositional object + object (title).
segments.s653-g1054: همان نیز here means 'the same/likewise also' (linking the next demand). فرخ is a proper name (here used as a name, though lexeme also means 'fortunate'); دبیر سپاه = 'secretary (scribe) of the army' is a noun phrase in apposition or identification. The sequence names another person requested.
segments.s653-g1055: هم از = 'also/from' (هم here adds 'also'); فیلسوفان = 'philosophers' (direct plural); وز مهتران is a poetic coordination equivalent to 'و مهتران' — 'and the elders/chiefs'. The segment lists learned and leading figures sought or assembled.
segments.s653-g1056: ز هر کشوری = 'from every country/realm' (ز = poetic 'from', هر = 'every', کشوری attributive). کار دیده سران is a noun phrase: کار دیده = 'experienced in affairs' (دیده as past participle 'seen/experienced'), and سران = 'leaders/heads' (the noun being qualified by the preceding phrase). Word order places the relative/source phrase before the qualified noun, typical in classical Persian.
segments.s653-g161: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. بشنید is simple past 'heard' (the subject is expressed after it). این گفته is the noun phrase 'this saying/statement' serving as the object. نوشین‌روان follows and supplies the subject (the sweet-souled one); Persian permits the sequence 'چو + past-verb + object + subject' in narrative style.
segments.s653-g162: شگفت آمدش is an idiomatic verbal phrase: آمدش ('came to her', with the clitic -ش referring back to نوشین‌روان) + شگفت ('wonder/astonishment') = 'she was astonished'. کار هر دو جوان is a genitive construction 'the affair/matter of the two youths' (کار = 'affair', هر دو جوان = 'both youths').
segments.s653-g163: برآشفت is a past, intransitive verb 'she grew angry/was enraged'. زان پس is an adverbial phrase 'thereupon/after that' (زان = 'from that', پس = 'then/after'). به دژخیم گفت = 'said to the torturer' (به + indirect object, گفت = past of 'to say'); the subject remains the same (she).
segments.s653-g164: که introduces reported speech or content ('that...'). این هر دو is 'these two' (subject). در خاک is a locative phrase 'in the earth/ground' and باید is the modal 'must/ought'. نهفت here functions as 'be hidden/buried' (past/participle-like form used in the periphrastic construction باید + نهفت = 'must be hidden/buried').
segments.s653-g165: کشنده is the agent noun 'the killer/executioner'. ببرد here functions as the narrative past 'took/carried' (classical/poetic usage). آن دو تن را is the definite object ('those two bodies') marked by را. دوان is a manner/adverbial element 'running/driving on', describing how he carried them.
segments.s653-g166: پس is a connective 'then/afterwards'. پرده شاه نوشین‌روان is a genitive phrase 'the curtain of King Nushin‑Ravan' (پرده = 'curtain', شاه = 'king', نوشین‌روان = name/epithet); this phrase continues the previous clause to indicate destination/location without repeating a verb.
segments.s653-g167: برآویختشان is a past verb with the clitic -شان 'them' attached: 'they were attacked/assailed' (or 'one sprang upon them'); درشبستان شاه is the locative 'in the king's night-chamber/bedchamber'. The verb is transitive here with the object incorporated as the clitic.
segments.s653-g168: This segment lists adjectival descriptions: نگونسار 'woeful/ill‑fated', پرخون 'full of blood/bloodied'. و links the phrases. تن پر گناه is a compact phrase 'a body full of sin' where پر functions as 'full (of)' and گناه is 'sin' — the adjective phrase follows the noun as is common in Persian poetic diction.
segments.s653-g441: چنین گفت کان کو بود پر خرد is a compact classical clause: چنین گفت = "thus he said" (چنین = thus; گفت here is the verb “said”). کان کو بود = a relative phrase meaning “who was” (کان is an archaic/formal relative particle and کو functions here as relative/interrogative 'who'; بود is the past form of 'to be'); پر خرد = “full of wisdom” (پر = full of; خرد = wisdom). Word order: comment/report verb (چنین گفت) precedes the relative description.
segments.s653-g442: ندارد غم آن کزو بگذرد is the predicate about that person: ندارد = “does not have” (present/stative denial of possession); غم = “grief/sorrow”; آن = a demonstrative/relative “that” (referring to a particular sorrow); کزو = “from which/from whom” (relative adverb linking to آن); بگذرد = present/subjunctive/indicative sense of ‘to pass/cease/leave’. Together: “he does not have that grief which would pass from him” (i.e. there is no sorrow that will pass him by).
segments.s653-g443: وگر ارجمندی سپارد به خاک = a conditional: وگر (و + گر) = 'and if' / 'but if'; ارجمندی = 'dignity / esteem' (the thing being given up); سپارد = 3rd-singular present/subjunctive of سپردن 'to entrust/hand over' (classical style); به خاک = 'to the earth/dust' (an idiom for consigning to oblivion). The clause sets up a hypothetical: if he consigns his dignity to the dust.
segments.s653-g444: نبندد دل اندر غم و درد پاک = main result clause: نبندد = negative present/subjunctive of بستن 'to bind/close', here 'will not bind/close'; دل = 'the heart'; اندر = 'in/within'; غم و درد = 'sorrow and pain' (paired emotional words); پاک at the end functions as an intensifier/adverb meaning 'completely/altogether' (here qualifying the negation: 'not at all'). Syntax: verb precedes its object (نبندد دل), and اندر + nouns marks the mental state the heart is not confined to.
segments.s653-g445: دگر کو ز نادیدنیها امید = opening phrase: دگر = 'another/again' (introducing another speaker or turn); کو is used here in a classical relative/interrogative sense ('who' / 'who is it that') — the supplied sense list lacks this use so treated as relative; ز = poetic 'from/of' or 'in regard to'; نادیدنیها = 'the unseen/that which is not seen' (plural/collective); امید = 'hope'. Literal sequence: 'Again—who, regarding the unseen, has hope'.
segments.s653-g446: چنان بگسلد دل چو از باد بید = result/simile clause: چنان = 'thus' / 'so'; بگسلد = 3rd-singular present/subjunctive of گسستن 'to break/rupture' (here 'breaks' or 'is broken'); دل = 'the heart'; چو = 'as/like'; از باد بید = 'from/by the wind (a) willow' → 'like a willow by the wind' (بید = willow). The simile modifies how the heart is broken: 'the heart breaks as a willow (is broken) by the wind.'
segments.s653-g447: دگر گفت بد چیست بر پادشای = 'Again he said: what bad/evil thing is (there) concerning the king?' Structure: دگر = again/another; گفت = 'said' (verb — sense list lacks the verbal sense); بد = 'bad/evil' (qualifying 'چیست'); چی ست (چی + ست) = 'what is' (interrogative + copula); بر = preposition 'on/upon/against/regarding'; پادشای = 'the king/sovereign'.
segments.s653-g448: کزو تیره گردد دل پارسای = subordinate relative clause: کزو = 'from which/from whom' (linking to 'بد' = 'that from which'); تیره گردد = 'becomes dark/gloomy' (تیره = dark/ominous; گردد = become/happen); دل پارسای = 'the heart of the devout/pious (man)' (دل = heart; پارسای = pious person). Word order: relative particle + verb + subject phrase — classical poetic ordering.
segments.s653-g1089: چو is the temporal/conditional conjunction 'when/if'. از نعمتش بهره یابی is a clause meaning 'you obtain a share of his blessing/bounty' with یابی as a present/infinitival verb 'you obtain/attain' (not an imperative here). بکوش is an imperative 'strive/endeavor' addressed to the listener and links this clause as the main exhortation.
segments.s653-g1090: که introduces a purposive/result clause 'so that / in order that'. داری is the present form 'you have/keep' (possession/state). همیشه is an adverb 'always'. به فرمانش گوش is a small prepositional phrase where به + فرمانش ('to his command') and گوش is used metonymically as 'attention / heeding'; together the clause says 'so that you always keep your ear to his command' (i.e. always obey him).
segments.s653-g1091: به introduces the object of the action 'toward / from' and اندیشه‌گر is a noun 'a thinker / a man of thought'. سربپیچی here denotes the act of turning away or evading (an archaic/poetic nominal/verbal expression meaning 'turning the head away' or 'rebellion'). ازوی ('از + وی') is the prepositional phrase 'from him'. The whole segment functions as a conditional protasis 'if you turn away from the thoughtful man'.
segments.s653-g1092: نبیند is the negative present/future third‑person form 'he/it will not see' (here impersonal: 'will not look'). به نیکی is an adverbial phrase 'with goodness / kindly'. تو را is the object/topic 'you' marked by را. بخت روی is a possessive phrase 'fortune's face' (idiomatically 'fortune's favor' or 'good luck looking upon you'); روی literally 'face' used figuratively. The clause predicts that fortune will not favor you.
segments.s653-g1093: چو is the temporal 'when/if'. نزدیک دارد literally 'he holds (you) near' (دارد = 'he has/keeps'). مشو is the negative imperative 'do not become' and combines with برمنش (on/against his disposition/noble character) to mean 'do not behave contrary to his good nature'—a caution against arrogance or mistreatment while favored.
segments.s653-g1094: وگر is the conditional 'but if / and if'. دور گردی here expresses 'if you go/become distant' with گردی functioning as a verbal element meaning 'wandering/going away'. مشو again is the negative imperative 'do not become' and بدکنش is a predicate meaning 'ill‑doing toward him' or 'ill‑disposed/unkind action' — so even if the ruler keeps you at a distance, do not treat him badly.
segments.s653-g1095: پرستنده‌گر is a compounded form where the -گر suffix works like an old conditional 'اگر' — here it introduces 'if a worshipper / devotee'. یابد is the verb 'finds/receives' (present/subjunctive meaning 'comes upon'). از شاه رنج is the prepositional object 'suffering from the king'. The segment is a conditional protasis: 'if a devotee finds suffering from the king'.
segments.s653-g1096: نگه کن is an imperative phrase 'keep watch / beware / take heed' formed from نگه (watch/keeping) + کن (do!). که introduces an explanatory clause 'for / because'. با رنج is 'with/by suffering'. نامست و گنج is an elliptical poetic statement meaning 'name (i.e. renown) and treasure' — the sense is that suffering brings neither renown nor wealth; the clause gives the reason to take heed.
segments.s653-g569: ازان پس is a temporal phrase 'after that' (ازان = 'from that', پس = 'then/after'). چو here is the subordinating conjunction 'when'. یارت is 'your companion' (possessive -t attached). بود is the past form 'was' describing the state. نیکساز is an adjectival predicate meaning 'well-disposed / proper / beneficial' describing یار.
segments.s653-g570: بروبر is an adverb meaning 'directly / straight / exactly' modifying the verb. به هنگامت is a locative/temporal phrase 'at your time/moment' (به + هنگام with the enclitic -ت = 'your'). آید is the present/future-oriented verb 'comes' (here rendered as a habitual/resultative). نیاز is the noun 'need / supplication' serving as the subject/object that 'comes'.
segments.s653-g571: چو functions as 'if/when'. کوشش is the abstract noun 'effort/endeavor'. نباشد here is a negative present/subjunctive form meaning 'there is not' or 'is lacking' (classical usage); its given lemma senses don't list this grammatical reading. تن زورمند is a nominal phrase 'a strong/powerful body/person' (تن = 'body/person', زورمند = 'mighty').
segments.s653-g572: نیارد is here an older/poetic negative verb meaning essentially 'does not bring / will not be brought / will not be obtained' (the morphological reality is archaic; the supplied senses do not match this verbal use). سر آرزوها is a genitive phrase 'the head/top of desires' (سر = 'head', آرزوها = 'desires/wishes'). ببند is an imperative 'close / shut / bind' directing the listener to restrain those wishes.
segments.s653-g573: چو = 'when'. کوشش = 'effort'. ز اندازه اندر گذشت is a prepositional-verb cluster meaning 'passed beyond measure' (ز = 'from/beyond' in poetic usage, اندازه = 'measure/limit', اندر = 'in/within' used here in the idiom, گذشت = past 'passed' or 'went beyond'). Together they mean 'when effort exceeds its proper limits.'
segments.s653-g574: چنان دان is an imperative-like counsel 'consider it thus' (چنان = 'so/thus', دان = 'know/consider' used as an instruction). که introduces a content clause 'that ...'. کوشنده is the agent noun 'the one who strives', نومید = 'hopeless' (adjective or noun), and گشت is the past form 'became'. The clause states the result: 'that the striver becomes hopeless.'
segments.s653-g575: خوی مرد دانا بگوییم پنج is an inverted order typical of Persian poetry: خوی 'disposition' + مرد دانا 'a wise man' is the phrase being defined; بگوییم is the first-person plural verb 'we call/say' (the supplied lexical senses are for گفت‌وگو and do not reflect this verbal meaning), and پنج is the numeral 'five' indicating the number of items attributed to that disposition.
segments.s653-g576: کزان = 'from which' (کَ + ز = 'that/from which'), linking back to the five things. عادت = 'habit / custom'. او خود = 'he himself' (او = 'he/she', خود = emphatic 'self/own'). نباشد is the negative present/subjunctive meaning 'is not / will not be' (here 'will not be subject to'); the supplied lemma senses do not list this reading. به رنج = 'to/with suffering/hardship' (به + رنج). The full clause means 'from which habit he himself will not be in (subject to) hardship.'
segments.s653-g225: جوان (the youth) is the subject; برزبان is a prepositional/adverbial phrase 'on/in (the) tongue/speech' placed after the subject (poetic order). پادشاهی qualifies برزبان (i.e. 'the king's speech' / 'the king's tongue'), and نمود is a past-tense verb 'appeared/acted' that takes the preceding noun phrase as what he appeared as — together meaning 'the young man appeared as/on the king's tongue' (i.e. acted as the king's spokesman).
segments.s653-g226: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'; گفتار او 'his speech' is the source phrase governed by ز; روشنایی is the abstract noun 'light/clarity' that is the theme; فزود is a past/inchoative verb 'increased/grew' (here meaning 'was increased' or 'grew'), so the clause reads 'from his speech, clarity increased.'
segments.s653-g227: بدو is the dative/indirect object 'to him'; گفت is the verb 'said' (here used idiomatically as a speech verb) and is followed by روشن‌روان 'the clear‑souled (one)' as the speaker; آنکسی is a demonstrative/pronominal introducer 'that one' which begins the content of the reported speech. The word order is emphatic/poetic: indirect object + verb + subject + demonstrative.
segments.s653-g228: که introduces a relative/content clause 'who/that'; کوتاه گوید is a verbless/elliptical description meaning 'speaks briefly' (کوتاه = 'brief' modifying گوید 'speaks'); به معنی uses the preposition به with معنی to mean 'in (its) meaning' or 'with much meaning'; بسی is an adverb 'enough/sufficiently' or 'much' here intensifying معنی. Overall: 'who speaks briefly but with ample meaning.'
segments.s653-g229: کسی را که is a relative construction 'someone whose/that'; را is the object/ topicalizing marker attached to کسی (poetic usage); مغزش بود 'his brain was' uses بود (past of بودن) to attribute the following adjective; پرشتاب is the adjective 'very swift/quick' describing مغز. The whole segment means 'someone whose brain was swift.'
segments.s653-g230: فراوان سخن باشد is a nominal-predicate phrase: فراوان modifies سخن ('much/abundant speech') and باشد is present/subjunctive-like 'there is / will be' describing that such a person 'has much to say' (i.e. is loquacious). و links to دیر یاب, where دیر ('long / slow') + یاب ('to be found/obtained') together form the adjectival phrase 'difficult/slow to be found' (i.e. 'hard to find' or 'rare').
segments.s653-g231: چو is the conjunction 'when'; گفتار بیهوده 'vain/pointless speech' is the subject phrase; بسیار گشت uses بسیار ('much/many') + گشت (past of گشتن 'became') to mean 'became abundant' or 'grew much' — together 'when speech became much and worthless/pointless.'
segments.s653-g232: سخن گوی is a compound 'speaker' (سخن = speech + گوی = one who utters); در مردمی 'among the people' (در = 'in/among', مردمی = 'of the people'); خوارگشت is a nominal predicate meaning 'became despised/humiliated' — so the clause says 'the speaker among the people became despised.'
segments.s653-g329: دگر هفته is a temporal adverbial = “another week” (دگر = another; هفته = week) placed at the front. روشن‌دل شهریار is a noun phrase with the epithet روشن‌دل (clear‑hearted) preceding the head noun شهریار (king). The whole segment sets the time and subject: “for another week, the clear‑hearted king…”.
segments.s653-g330: همی‌بود is the imperfective/past‑habitual copula meaning “continued to be” or “was continually”; it links the subject (the king) to the following predicative phrase. داننده را خواستار is the predicate: داننده (the knower) is marked by the object/definite marker را and خواستار is an adjectival predicate meaning “desirous of” or “seeking”. Literally: “(the king) was continually desirous of the knower.”
segments.s653-g331: دل is the patient/theme “heart”. از کار گیتی is a genitive/partitive phrase “from the affairs of the world” (کار = affairs/work; گیتی = world). به یکسو کشید is a verb phrase where کشید (past of کشیدن here = “drew/pulled”) takes به یکسو (“to one side”) as directional complement; together it means “he drew his heart aside (away from worldly affairs).”
segments.s653-g332: کجا خواست is a clause meaning “wherever/whenever he wished” (کجا = where/wherever; خواست = wanted/wished, past). گفتار دانا is the object phrase “the speech/words of the wise” (گفتار = speech; دانا = wise), and شنید is the past verb “he heard.” Word order places the adverbial clause first and the object phrase before the final verb: “wherever he wished, he heard the wise words.”
segments.s653-g333: کسی کو is an archaic relative phrasing equivalent to “one who / whoever” (کو functioning as a relative particle here). سرافراز درگاه بود is the predicate: سرافراز = “honored/triumphant” and درگاه = “(royal) court/royal presence”; بود is the past copula “was.” The clause reads “whoever was honored at the court…”.
segments.s653-g334: به دانندگی is an instrumental/adverbial phrase “by/through (his) knowledge.” درخور شاه is a predicative phrase meaning “worthy/fitting of a king” (درخور = worthy of; شاه = king). The final بود is the copula “was.” Together: “(he) was, by his knowledge, worthy of (a) king.”
segments.s653-g335: برفتند is past plural “they departed/rose up.” In this poetic order the verb comes before its subject phrase گویندگان سخن (“the speakers/narrators of speech”), so the clause means “the narrators/speakers set forth/left.”
segments.s653-g336: This is a coordinated noun phrase describing who the speakers were: جوان و جهاندیده مرد کهن can be read as “a young man and a worldly‑wise, old man.” جوان (young) is coordinated with جهاندیده (worldly‑wise), and مرد کهن (an aged man) supplies the noun modified by کهن (old/ancient); the phrase lists two persons/types: the young and the worldly‑wise elder.
segments.s653-g337: سرافراز is an adjective placed before the name for emphasis ('honored/proud'). بوزرجمهرجوان is the proper name plus the epithet جوان ('the young'); Persian often concatenates name + epithet without an extra particle. The whole phrase is the subject/agent of the following verb.
segments.s653-g338: بشد is the past 3rd‑person form of شدن ('became / came to be / rose'); here it means 'he rose / joined'. باحکیمان is 'با' + حکیمان = 'with the sages' (the preposition attached directly to the plural noun). روشن‌روان is a compound adjective 'bright‑souled / clear‑soul(ed)' describing those sages. Word order: verb then a with‑phrase marking accompaniment.
segments.s653-g339: حکیمان is the plural noun 'sages' (here the lexical senses list given is a mismatch, so the noun sense is not among the provided options). داننده and هوشمند are participial/adjectival modifiers ('knowing/knowledgeable' and 'intelligent'), coordinated with و ('and') to form a descriptive phrase modifying حکیمان.
segments.s653-g340: رسیدند is past 3rd‑person plural 'they arrived/approached'. نزدیک تخت بلند is a locative phrase 'near (the) high throne' (نزدیک before the noun acts adverbially). Word order: verb then adverbial/locative phrase.
segments.s653-g341: نهادند is the past 3rd‑person plural of نهادن 'to place/put/turn' used here idiomatically with رخ ('face') to mean 'they turned (their) faces'. رخ (object) is fronted, سوی + proper name marks the direction/target: 'toward Bozorgmehr.'
segments.s653-g342: که introduces the subordinate clause ('which / upon which / so that'). کسری is the king (Kisrā / Khosrow) and is the subject of the clause. همی is an aspectual/emphatic particle here ('indeed / even / would'), ز + و (زو) is the archaic compressed form of از او ('from him/because of him') and links causally to برافروخت ('kindled/brightened'); چهر is the object 'face'. The clause means roughly 'which made Khosrow's face lighten/flush because of him.'
segments.s653-g343: ازیشان (از ایشان) literally 'one from them / of them' places the speaker among the group. یکی 'one' is the indefinite/partitive 'one (of them)'. بود is the past copula 'was'. فرزانه‌تر is the comparative form of فرزانه ('wise'), the comparative suffix -تر giving 'more wise' or 'the wiser one.' The phrase means 'one of them was the wiser (person).'
segments.s653-g344: بپرسید is the past 3rd‑person form 'he asked'. ازو (از او) is the object 'of/from him' = 'asked him'. از introduces the topic 'از قضا و قدر' meaning 'concerning fate and destiny' (از here functions as 'about/concerning'). قضا and قدر are paired nouns meaning 'fate' and 'destiny' (often used together). و is the coordinating 'and'.
segments.s653-g465: This noun phrase lists two coordinated qualities as the grammatical subject: منش پستی و کام برپادشا = “a mean disposition and (a) craving for the king.” Note the poetic concatenation: و (part of وکام here) links the two nouns; برپادشا is a prepositional complement (بر + پادشا) modifying کام, indicating object/target of the craving.
segments.s653-g466: This is the predicate clause: به + بیهوده (adverb) + خستن (verbal infinitive/participial idea) + دل پارسا (object). Here خستن is used in a causative/result sense “to weary / to cause weariness,” so the whole clause means that the subject ‘caused the pious man’s heart to be wearied in vain.’ به introduces the manner/purpose ('in/with'), and دل پارسا is the possessed noun phrase 'the pious man's heart.'
segments.s653-g467: زبان راندن is a verbal phrase (literally 'to drive the tongue') used idiomatically as 'to lash out with the tongue / to use the tongue (speaking sharply)'; it is coordinated with دیده ... شرم (the eye lacking shame). The conjunction و links the two parts. بی‌آب here modifies شرم: a poetic compound meaning 'without the water (i.e. lacking) of shame' = shameless.
segments.s653-g468: گزیدن (to choose/select) takes خروش (a cry/uproar) as its object; اندر (in) + آواز نرم ('a soft/ gentle voice') gives the sphere/manner in which that cry occurs. The phrase therefore describes choosing/raising a clamor but doing so in a soft/gentle-sounding voice — a deliberate poetic contrast.
segments.s653-g469: خردمند مردم is a noun phrase 'a wise person of the people / among the people.' که دارد روا is a relative clause: که = 'who' and دارد (here acting like 'considers' / 'finds') + روا = 'proper / permissible.' Together they introduce the rhetorical question about who would consider something proper.
segments.s653-g470: خرد دور کردن is a light-verb construction: خرد (wisdom) + دور کردن ('to put away / to remove') = 'to cast wisdom aside.' ز بهر هوا is a poetic prepositional phrase meaning 'for the sake of desire' (ز/بهر = because of/for, هوا = passion/desire). The whole segment states the action the wise person would (not) find fitting.
segments.s653-g471: بپرسید is used as an injunction 'ask' (classical Persian form that can function as 2nd-person plural/polite imperative); دیگر یکی هوشمند = 'another / a certain intelligent one.' The verb + noun phrase invites the addressee to question an additional wise person.
segments.s653-g472: This is the content clause introduced by که: اندرجهان (in the world) sets the domain; چیست is the copula 'is what / is' and آن بی‌گزند is the noun phrase 'that unhurt/unharmed one.' Grammatically it is a question asking for the identity of the person described as بی‌گزند.
segments.s653-g241: This is a nominal phrase listing epithets: خردمند and ودانا are synonyms meaning 'wise/knowing' joined by the coordinating و, and روشن‌روان is another attributive compound 'bright‑souled'. Persian permits a series of adjectives/nouns without an explicit head noun; they function together as a description of a person.
segments.s653-g242: تنش is تن + enclitic possessive -ش 'his/her/its' = 'his body'. زین appears here as a fused prepositional element ('from/of this') used poetically before جهانست (جهان + است fused = 'is of the world'); the clause therefore reads 'his body is of this world'. وجان begins with the conjunction و 'and' plus جان 'soul', then زان ('from that') + جهان 'world' gives 'and his soul (is) from that world' — the copula is expressed by جهانست earlier and understood elliptically here.
segments.s653-g243: هران کس که is the poetic spelling of هر آن کس که 'whoever'. در کار 'in/with respect to a task' is a prepositional phrase. پیشی کند uses پیشی (the abstract noun 'precedence') with کردن (کند) to mean 'to take the lead/act before others' — کند is the present/subjunctive form used here as a general present.
segments.s653-g244: همه رای و آهنگ بیشی کند is the result clause: همه رای و آهنگ 'every counsel and intention' (رای here = counsel/plan; و‌آهنگ = 'and intention/aim') and بیشی کند 'makes greater/ increases' (بیشی as 'more/greater' with کردن). The verb کند again expresses a general/resultive present action.
segments.s653-g245: بنایافت is a past/passive verbal adjective 'was founded/established'. رنجه مکن is an imperative prohibition: رنجه (a noun/verbal sense 'suffering/distress') + مکن 'do not (make)'; خویشتن 'yourself' is the reflexive object. Together: 'Do not make yourself suffer (over it)'.
segments.s653-g246: که introduces the explanatory clause 'for/because'. تیمارجان 'tending/care of the soul' is a compound meaning 'care of the soul' and باشد (here an optative/existential 'is/let it be') links the idea; و رنج تن is elliptical ('and the body's suffering' — the copula is omitted). The whole gives the reason: 'for it is the soul's care and the body's suffering'.
segments.s653-g247: This is an inverted, poetic word order: ز نیرو ('from strength') + بود (past copula used as general truth 'is/comes') + مرد را راستی. را is the definite/object marker attached to مرد for emphasis, and راستی 'uprightness/truth' is the predicate. Read naturally: 'A man's honesty/uprightness comes from strength.'
segments.s653-g248: ز سستی ('from weakness') is the source phrase; دروغ آید 'falsehood comes' uses آید 'comes' as a general present; وکاستی 'and deficiency/defect' is appended with و to produce the pair 'falsehood and deficiency come from weakness.' The verb is general/axiomatic rather than a single past event.
segments.s653-g289: ازان (an archaic compound of از + آن) is a prepositional pointer meaning “from/at that” and introduces the source or occasion (here, Bozorgmehr's utterance). خوب‌گفتار is a compound adjective/epithet “good-spoken/eloquent” modifying بوزرجمهر, the proper name that follows in the normal Persian modifier→noun order.
segments.s653-g290: حکیمان همه تازه کردند چهر is a short clause with plural agent حکیمان (‘the sages’) + adverbial/quantifier همه (‘all’) followed by the past transitive کرد(ند) ‘made/refreshed’ and the object چهر ‘face/appearance’. تازه here functions as an adjective/adverb meaning “made fresh/renewed.” Word order is subject → (quantifier) → object → verb (typical older Persian allows flexible order; here the verb follows the object).
segments.s653-g291: یکی انجمن ماند اندر شگفت is literal: یکی (‘one’ / a certain) + انجمن (‘assembly’) as the subject, ماند (past of ماندن) ‘remained’ as the verb, and اندر شگفت (‘in wonder/astonishment’) as an adverbial phrase; اندر is the archaic form of امروز’s در meaning “in/within.” The clause means an assembly was left in amazement.
segments.s653-g292: که introduces a content clause ('that / because / namely that') explaining the cause of the astonishment. مرد جوان is a noun + adjective ‘young man’, آن بزرگی (‘that greatness’) is the object phrase and گرفت is the simple past of گرفتن ‘took/acquired’. The clause reads 'that the young man took/acquired that greatness.'
segments.s653-g293: جهاندار کسری is a title + name: 'the world-ruler Khosrow'. درو is an archaic contraction meaning در او ‘at him/toward him’ (so it marks the target of Khosrow's attention). خیره ماند is a compound of خیره (staring/astonished) + ماند (past of ماندن) meaning 'was left staring/astonished.' Word order places the prepositional contraction after the name, which is common in older Persian.
segments.s653-g294: سرافراز is an epithet 'triumphant/honored' and functions as an appositive or subject modifier. روزی here (in classical usage) is temporal/pointing ('at that moment' / 'then'); دهان را uses the object-marker را after دهان ‘mouth’ to indicate the thing affected, and بخواند is the past form meaning 'he opened/spoke/read/uttered' — here best rendered 'opened his mouth and spoke' (بخواند often means 'recited/said'). The clause describes him beginning to speak.
segments.s653-g295: بفرمود is a polite/honorific past verb 'he commanded/said' (classical register). تا introduces a purpose or result clause here ('so that' / 'in order that'), and نام او سر کنند is a semi-idiomatic sequence: نام او 'his name' + سر کردن where سر is 'head/top' and کردن is 'to make/place' — together 'place his name at the head' or 'record his name first.' کنند is the 3rd‑person plural subjunctive/imperative form used to express the ordered action.
segments.s653-g296: بدانگه is an adverbial phrase 'thereupon/at that moment/in that state' linking the command to what follows; که introduces the subordinate clause 'that they begin...', آغاز دفتر is 'the beginning of the book/record', and کنند again is the 3rd‑person plural subjunctive/imperative 'they do/begin'. The whole means they should thereupon commence the book (i.e., start the record with his name).
segments.s653-g673: نیازد is a verbal, third-person jussive/imperative-like form of نیازیدن used here as a prohibition ('do not seek/ask for'). The phrase به کاری places کاری (object noun, 'a task/work') after the preposition به ('for/for the purpose of'), and the relative clause که ناکردنیست ('which is impossible') modifies کاری; ناکردنیست is a compound adjective formed from ناکردنی + است meaning 'is not doable/ impossible'.
segments.s653-g674: نیازارد is a negative-form verb meaning 'do not trouble/harm' (an admonitory statement). آن را is the demonstrative plus the definite object marker (آن = 'that/the one', را marks it as definite object). The following relative clause که نازردنیست modifies آن; نازردنیست (poetic formation) describes someone/something that cannot be softened/pampered or affected, so the whole clause means 'do not trouble the one who cannot be affected.'
segments.s653-g675: نماند is a verb meaning 'do not remain' or here best read as a prohibitional/jussive notion 'do not allow/let it be that...'. The following clause is a small clause: که نیکی برو بگذرد — که introduces the content, نیکی ('goodness' or 'a good deed') is subject and برو بگذرد is a verbal phrase where بگذرد (subjunctive/presentive of گذشتن) means 'to pass'. The literal structure is 'do not let it be that a good deed passes.'
segments.s653-g676: پی روز is a prepositional/time phrase ('after/in respect of a day' or 'as for a day'); نا آمده is a compound negative + participle meaning 'not yet come' (نا = not, آمده = come/arrived). نشمرد is a negative jussive/imperative form of شمردن ('do not count/ reckon'). Together the clause warns against counting/expecting a day that has not arrived.
segments.s653-g677: بدشمن is a compound noun 'evil/hostile enemy' serving as subject. ز is the poetic preposition 'from' or 'than' in comparisons; نخچیر ('spoil, booty') is the thing being compared. آژیرتر is a comparative-form adjective derived from آژیر ('alarm/cry') meaning 'more alarming' — so the clause reads 'an evil enemy is more alarming than booty/loot.'
segments.s653-g678: This clause states a positive counterpart: برو (a literary/discourse particle here introducing the following noun phrase) + دوست ('friend') is the subject, همواره ('always/ever') is an adverb, and چون تیر و پر is a simile ('like arrow and feather') where تیر ('arrow') and پر ('feather', i.e. fletching) are paired to express close association. Word order is subject–adverb–predicate(simile).
segments.s653-g679: ز here introduces a causal clause ('because/of'), شادی is the noun 'joy', and که introduces the relative/clausal content. فرجام او is a possessive phrase ('its end/outcome'), غم بود is the verb phrase 'was sorrow' (بود = 'was'). The clause thus means 'because the joy's outcome was sorrow.' The verb بود is past copula.
segments.s653-g680: خردمند را uses را as a topical/definite marker on 'the wise man' (خردمند). ارز وی is 'its/that value to him' (ارز = worth/value, وی = to him/it); کم بود is the predication 'was little' (کم = small/insufficient, بود = was). The overall meaning: 'the wise valued it little' or 'to the wise its worth was small.'
segments.s653-g217: گر ای (گر‌ای) is the conditional particle 'if' combined with the vocative 'ای' addressing someone ('O ...'). دون is a vocative adjective 'low/base/ignoble one'. که introduces the content of the condition ('that / that you ...'). فرمان دهی is a second-person present/subjunctive/optative construction meaning 'you give an order'; بنده را is the definite direct object marked by را ('the servant').
segments.s653-g218: که begins a subordinate/result/purpose clause 'so that / that'. بگشاید is 3rd-person singular (classical/poetic) optative/subjunctive 'may open/release'; its understood subject is بنده (the servant) from the previous clause. از بند is a prepositional phrase 'from the bond(s)/shackle(s)' and گوینده را is the definite object 'the speaker' (را marks the object).
segments.s653-g219: بگویم is a first-person verbal form ('I say / I will speak') used here narratively. وگر is a concessive/conditional particle 'and if / though'. چند in this concessive use means 'somewhat / though' or 'however little', and بی‌مایه‌ام is an adjectival predicate with the 1st-person possessive suffix '-ام' meaning 'I am without substance/insignificant'.
segments.s653-g220: بدانش here functions as an imperative/notice 'know this' or 'be aware of this' (بدان + ش as an enclitic); در introduces the domain 'in' (in the matter of). از is the comparative/prepositional 'than / from' used in the sense of comparison here, کمترین is 'the least/lowest', and پایه‌ام ('my base/rank') is the noun پایه with the 1st-person suffix '-ام' meaning 'my station/basis'. The clause says roughly 'know that, in learning, I am above the lowest of ranks.'
segments.s653-g221: نکوهش نباشد is a fixed negative jussive/imperative expression 'let there be no reproach / there should be no blame'. که introduces the content clause 'that ...'. دانا is the agent 'the wise one' and زبان is used metonymically for 'speech' or 'utterance' (i.e., 'the wise man's speech').
segments.s653-g222: گشاده کند is a causative/performative phrase 'he opens (his) speech' or 'speaks frankly' — گشاده is an adjective 'open/frank' and کند is the 3rd-person singular auxiliary/verb 'does/makes'. نزد means 'in the presence of / before', and نوشین‌روان is an epithet 'sweet‑souled' (a pleasant‑minded person); together: 'speaks frankly before the sweet‑souled one.'
segments.s653-g223: نگه کرد is a two-part verbal expression: نگه (a noun/verb stem 'gaze, look') plus کرد 'did/made' yielding 'he looked/gazed'. کسری is the subject name 'Kisra' (Khosrow). بداننده is the indirect object 'to the knower' or 'to that man of knowledge', and گفت is the narrative verb 'said' introducing the following speech clause.
segments.s653-g224: که begins the reported-question clause 'that / why ...'. دانش is 'knowledge'. چرا is the interrogative 'why'. باید is the modal 'must / ought'. اندر is an archaic locative 'in / within'. نهفت is an adjective/participle meaning 'hidden, concealed'. Together they form Kisra's question: 'Why must knowledge be kept hidden?'
segments.s653-g865: چنین (an adverb) precedes the verb to mean 'thus' or 'so', and گفت is the past tense 'said' (classical narrative order places the verb close to the front). باشاه خورشید چهر is the noun phrase identifying the speaker: باشاه is an archaic/formal address for 'the king' and خورشید چهر is a compound epithet 'sun‑faced' (خورشید 'sun' + چهر 'face/appearance') describing the king.
segments.s653-g866: که introduces the content clause 'that': here it leads into an optative wish. بادا is the optative auxiliary 'may' (a classical subjunctive/wish marker). به کام تو literally 'to/for your desire' (به = 'to/for', کام = 'desire', تو = 'you'). روشن سپهر is the wished state: روشن 'bright' modifies سپهر 'sky/heaven'.
segments.s653-g867: چنان دان is an imperative/hortative clause meaning 'consider/know it so' (چنان = 'thus/so', دان here = 'know/consider'). که begins a relative/conditional clause 'that/who', هرکس که دارد خرد = 'whoever has wisdom' (هرکس 'whoever', که linking relative, دارد present tense 'has/possesses', خرد 'wisdom').
segments.s653-g868: بدانش is a fused prepositional/adverbial element here meaning 'by/with that' (بِ + دانش = 'with that knowledge'); روان را 'the soul' is marked by the definite/object marker را. همی‌پرورد is a classical present/habitual verb (همی- prefix signals continuous/presentive in poetic Persian) meaning 'nurtures' or 'fosters'.
segments.s653-g869: نکوهیده is an adjective/past participle 'censured/blamed' placed before the phrase it modifies. ده کار 'ten deeds' uses the numeral ده before the noun کار. بر here functions as a comparative/relational preposition ('among/over/against' in poetic usage) linking the deeds to ده گروه 'ten groups' (گروه = 'group/company'). The phrase is compact and idiomatic: 'a censured deed among ten groups…'.
segments.s653-g870: نکوهیده‌تر is the comparative form 'more censured/more blameworthy' (تر suffix makes comparative). نزد here means 'in the presence of / in the estimation of' (classical usage: 'in the eyes of'). دانش‌پژوه is the agent of judgment 'the scholar' or 'student of learning' — so the whole segment reads '…is judged more blameworthy in the scholar's eyes.'
segments.s653-g871: یکی آنک is an idiomatic opening 'one who/there is one who' (یکی = 'one, a certain', آنک = relative 'who/that'). حاکم بود is past description 'was ruler' (حاکم 'ruler', بود past copula 'was'). با دروغ is an instrumental phrase 'by/with falsehood' (با = 'with/by', دروغ = 'lie/falsehood') describing the manner of his rule.
segments.s653-g872: نگیرد is a present/subjunctive/negative form of گرفتن meaning 'does not take/seize' here (poetic negated present). بر مرد دانا is a prepositional phrase 'against/over the wise man' where مرد دانا = 'the wise man' (مرد 'man', دانا 'wise/learned'). فروغ 'radiance' is the thing governed by the verb and appears at the end as the semantic object being 'not taken' (i.e., 'he cannot deprive the wise man of his radiance').
segments.s653-g841: This is a compact nominal clause: ابرشاه (the great/supreme king) is the topical subject, followed by the predicative adjective phrase زشتست (زشت + است, 'is unbecoming/ugly') and then the verbal-noun خون ریختن ('to spill blood'). In classical Persian the verbal noun (خون ریختن) functions as the complement being judged by زشتست; the copula is fused as زشتست (poetic contraction).
segments.s653-g842: به اندک سخن is an adverbial phrase meaning 'by/with a few words' (به marking the circumstance), دل is the object/topic 'the heart', and برآهیختن is the verbal-noun/infinitive 'to rouse, to awaken'. Word order places the object (دل) before the verb phrase (برآهیختن), typical in older/poetic Persian: literally 'to rouse the heart by a small speech'.
segments.s653-g843: همان چون begins a conditional/temporal clause ('that/when/as'), followed by the adjective سبک‌سر and the copula بود; شهریار (the king) appears after the predicate in the inverted, poetic order: 'the king was rash' (همان چون ... بود شهریار = 'when the ruler was such (rash)').
segments.s653-g844: بداندیش ('ill‑minded' or 'malicious') is the actor; دست اندآرد is a fixed verbal phrase ('puts his hand / undertakes' — دست + اندآرد, classical present/political sense) with اندآرد meaning 'to put/put in' here; به کار is the prepositional complement 'to the work' — together meaning 'he takes up the work' or 'sets about action'.
segments.s653-g845: This clause places the topical demonstrative همان up front, then the prepositional phrase با خردمند ('with the wise'), followed by the verb گیرد ('takes/engages') and the noun ستیز ('strife/fight') as its object: literally 'that one with the wise takes strife', i.e. 'he quarrels with the wise'.
segments.s653-g846: کند is the light verb 'does/makes', with دل ('the heart') before it as the patient/theme and تیز as the resulting adjective; ز نادانی خویش is an ablative/genitive phrase ('from his own ignorance') where ز = 'from/because of' and خوی + ش = 'his disposition / his'. Together: 'he makes the heart keen/sharp from his own ignorance' (i.e. his ignorance quickens/agitates the heart).
segments.s653-g847: دل شاه گیتی is a genitive chain 'the heart of the king of the world' (noun + possessor + epithet). چو introduces a temporal clause 'when', and پر آز گشت is the predicate 'became full of greed' (پر = 'full (of)', آز = 'greed', گشت = 'became'). Word order is typical poetic inversion with the predicate following the qualifier.
segments.s653-g848: روان (the soul/spirit) followed by the cliticic/dir. particle ورا ('to/for him') is the subject/affected theme; دیو is the predicative noun 'demon/fiend' and انباز is an archaic/epithet modifying دیو; گشت is the past-of-becoming 'became'. Literally: 'his spirit became a devil/anbaz' — note the older adjectival epithet and the clitic placement (ورا) after the noun.
segments.s653-g457: بپرسید is an imperative/jussive here (poetic address) meaning “ask” or “inquire”; دیگـر modifies it as “again/another.” که introduces a relative/question clause “who/that,” and بی‌عیب کیست is the embedded question ‘who is without fault’ with کی (who) + ست (is) as the copula at the end, which is typical Persian word order for a nominal question.
segments.s653-g458: This clause is an object-question phrased after the previous sentence: نکوهیدن (the act of blaming) + آزادگان را (آزادگان marked by را as the definite/direct object) and بچیست as the interrogative-final predicate ‘what is it?’ (ب-چیست), so the literal order is ‘To blame the free, what is it?’ → ‘What is the reason for blaming the free?’
segments.s653-g459: چنین گفت is a formulaic ‘thus he said’ (چنین = thus, گفت = said). کین را is the object ‘this grudge/hatred’ marked by را, and ببخشیم is an optative/ hortative present-subjunctive (‘let us forgive’ / ‘we should forgive’) addressing the group; راست is an emphatic adverb (‘truly/indeed’) commenting on the following clause.
segments.s653-g460: که introduces an explanatory clause (‘for/because/that’). جان و خرد is a coordinated subject (‘life and wisdom’), درسخن is the locative phrase ‘in speech’ (در + سخن), and پادشاست is a poetic fused form meaning ‘is king/sovereign’ (پادشاه است), the predicate asserting that life and wisdom are sovereign in speech.
segments.s653-g461: This is a noun phrase that will be object/target of the following verb: گرانمایگان را—گرانمایگان (‘the illustrious/noble ones’) with را marking them as definite/affected; فسون و دروغ lists the agents/causes (‘enchantment and falsehood’) that act upon them.
segments.s653-g462: به before the nouns here means ‘by/through’ or ‘with’; کژی و بیداد are the means (‘crookedness and injustice’) joined by و; جستن is the infinitive ‘to seek’ or ‘they seek’ (infinitival/nominal verbal form used to express action), and فروغ is the object sought (‘radiance/splendor’), so the phrase means ‘they seek splendor by crookedness and injustice.’
segments.s653-g463: میانه بود (here split as میانه بو د in the manuscript) is the locative + past copula ‘in the middle was’; this poetic separation should be read as بود = ‘was’. مرد کنداوری is the predicate noun + proper name ‘the man Kandauri.’ The fronting of میانه marks the setting (‘in the midst/center’).
segments.s653-g464: This is a descriptive apposition: نکوهشگر ‘a reproacher/critic’ joined by و to a participial/adjectival phrase سر پر از داوری ‘a head full of judgment,’ where سر = ‘head/mind,’ پر از = ‘full of,’ and داوری = ‘judgment,’ characterizing the man.
segments.s653-g801: سرموبد موبدان اردشیر is a title plus apposition: سرموبد (head/chief mobad) + موبدان (the mobeds/priesthood, here a genitive group 'of the mobeds') with اردشیر in apposition as the proper name. Word order places the title before the genitive and the name follows as an identifying apposition.
segments.s653-g802: چو شاپور وچون یزدگرد دبیر lists examples introduced by چو ('like/as') and the compound conjunction وچون ('and as/like'); each name follows these particles and دبیر is a postposed nominal apposition meaning 'secretary' attached to یزدگرد. Here چو/وچون function as similes ('such as...').
segments.s653-g803: ستاره شناسان و جویندگان is a coordinated noun phrase: ستاره (star) + شناسان (agentive plural 'knowers/experts') = 'astrologers/astronomers', then و ('and') joining جویندگان ('seekers/searchers'). The compound is a list of learned types.
segments.s653-g804: خردمند و بیدار گویندگان places two adjectives (خردمند 'wise', بیدار 'awake/alert') before the noun گویندگان ('speakers/narrators'), so it reads 'wise and alert speakers'. The adjectives modify گویندگان and the conjunction و links them.
segments.s653-g805: سراینده بوزرجمهر جوان: سراینده ('poet/singer') is followed by the proper name بوزرجمهر with the adjective جوان ('young') postposed to the name—a common appositional pattern meaning 'Bozorgmehr, the young poet'.
segments.s653-g806: بیامد برشاه نوشین‌روان: بیامد is the past verb 'came' (3rd sg.), برشاه is the prepositional phrase 'to/upon the king' (بر + شاه), and نوشین‌روان is an epithet in apposition to شاه ('sweet‑souled' / pleasant‑minded). The clause states that he came to the king so‑described.
segments.s653-g807: بدانندگان گفت شاه جهان: بدانندگان ('those who know' = experts) is the subject. گفت here functions as the reporting verb 'said' (classical usage); شاه جهان is the addressee/vocative 'king of the world' placed after the verb without an overt preposition. (Note: the provided lemma senses for گفت do not list 'said', so that sense is not among the supplied options.)
segments.s653-g808: که باکیست این دانش اندر نهان is an indirect interrogative introduced by که ('who/that' marking the clause). باکیست is an old-style interrogative 'with whom/is whose' (با + کی + است → 'who has/with whom is'), این دانش 'this knowledge', and اندر نهان 'in secret/hidden'. The clause asks who secretly possesses this knowledge.
segments.s653-g625: همیشه is an adverb placed at the front meaning 'always'. دل شاه نوشین‌روان is a noun phrase where دل ('heart') is qualified by شاه نوشین‌روان ('the sweet‑souled king'); in poetry the ezāfe is not written but the relation is genitive/appositive: 'the heart of the sweet‑souled king.'
segments.s653-g626: مبادا introduces a negative wish/jussive ('may it not be, lest'); the clause that follows (ز آموختن ناتوان) predicates on the preceding subject (the king's heart). ز here marks relation/source with the verbal noun آموختن, so 'ناتوان' ('incapable') means 'incapable of learning'—the copula/verb is omitted because مبادا carries the jussive force.
segments.s653-g627: بپرسید is the past 3rd‑person form 'asked' (poetic past); پس = 'then' connects temporally; موبد تیز مغز is the noun phrase 'the mobed (Zoroastrian priest) of sharp mind/brain' with the compound adjective تیز مغز ('sharp‑brained') following the head noun.
segments.s653-g628: که introduces the reported question/clause ('that/what'); اندر جهان = 'in the world' (inward prepositional phrase). چیست is the copula + interrogative 'what is', and کردار نغز is the noun phrase 'a fine/delightful deed' with نغز qualifying کردار.
segments.s653-g629: کجا is the interrogative 'where'; مرد را uses را as the definite/object/topic marker 'to the man' (topicalizing the recipient). روشنایی is the noun 'light/illumination' and دهد is the present/jussive 3rd‑person form 'gives' (here part of a rhetorical question).
segments.s653-g630: ز marks source/origin ('from'); رنج زمانه is a genitive 'the suffering/hardship of the age'; رهایی is the abstract noun 'release/deliverance' and دهد repeats the verb 'gives'—the clause asks rhetorically where such deliverance can be provided.
segments.s653-g631: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed narrative phrase 'thus gave an answer' (چنین 'thus', داد past 'gave', پاسخ 'answer'). که begins a reported clause; هر کو is the archaic indefinite 'whoever' (هر + کو), and خرد is the thing 'wisdom' that is found in the following verb phrase.
segments.s653-g632: بیابد is the present/subjunctive 3rd‑person form 'finds/attains' (from یابیدن). ز هر دو جهان = 'from both worlds' (literally 'from each of the two worlds'), and بر خورد is the verbal compound (بر + خورد ~ برخوردن) meaning 'befalls/comes to' or 'is bestowed upon'—here 'benefit/accrual will come to him from both worlds.'
segments.s653-g889: همان بیخرد کو نیابد خرد — 'همان' (preceding the noun) is 'that/the same' pointing to a previously mentioned person; 'بیخرد' is used as a noun 'fool'. 'کو' is an archaic relative/interrogative particle here linking to the verb clause (not literally 'where'), and 'نیابد' is third-person singular present meaning 'does not find/obtain' (classical present form). 'خرد' is the direct object 'wisdom.'
segments.s653-g890: پشیمان شود هم ز گفتار بد — 'پشیمان شود' is the verb phrase 'becomes regretful' with 'شود' as the third-person singular copula/verb 'become.' 'هم' means 'also/too' (emphatic) and precedes the cause phrase. 'ز' is the classical/prepositional form of 'از' meaning 'because of/from,' linking to 'گفتار بد' ('evil/bad speech') as the cause.
segments.s653-g891: دل مردم بیخرد به آرزوی — 'دل مردم' is a genitive-like juxtaposition 'the heart of the people' (here 'people' is qualified by 'بیخرد' = 'foolish people'). 'به آرزوی' is a prepositional phrase 'to/onto desire' where 'به' marks the target and 'آرزوی' is the noun 'desire/wish.'
segments.s653-g892: برین گونه آویزد ای نیک‌خوی — 'برین' is the fused poetic form of 'بر این' meaning 'upon this' or 'in this way' (بر + این); 'گونه' means 'manner/way.' 'آویزد' is third-person singular present ('hangs/clings') describing what the heart does. The vocative 'ای نیک‌خوی' addresses someone ('O kind‑natured one').
segments.s653-g893: چوآتش که گوگرد یابد خورش — 'چو' here introduces a comparison/conditional 'when/as.' 'آتش' is the subject 'fire' and 'که' is the relative/connector 'that/which.' 'گوگرد یابد' is 'finds/comes upon sulphur' (یابد = third‑person present of 'to find'). 'خورش' in context refers to the fire's burning/flare (an older/poetic usage), functioning as the subject's effect.
segments.s653-g894: گرش درنیستان بود پرورش — 'گرش' here denotes something like the fire's turn/force or activity (a noun of motion/force). 'درنیستان' is a locative compound 'in the reed‑bed' (در + نیستان). 'بود' is the past of 'to be' ('was' — classical narrative usage). 'پرورش' is a noun 'nurture/feeding' used figuratively: 'its force was nurtured/fostered in the reed‑bed.'
segments.s653-g895: دل شاه نوشین‌روان زنده‌باد — a short optative/exclamatory clause. 'دل شاه نوشین‌روان' = 'the king's sweet‑souled heart' (noun phrase with attributive 'نوشین‌روان'). 'زنده‌باد' is an optative formula 'Long live!' placed after the NP to wish vitality/long life to it.
segments.s653-g896: سران جهان پیش او بنده باد — another optative clause: 'سران جهان' = 'the leaders/heads of the world'; 'پیش او' means 'in his presence / before him.' 'بنده' is predicate 'servant' and 'باد' is the optative particle 'may' (wishing), so the whole line means 'May the rulers of the world be servants before him.'
segments.s653-g505: This is an inverted, compressed classical phrase: وز here is an archaic/poetic preposition meaning 'from' or 'by' (contracted in older texts), followed by آواز 'voice' and the pronoun او 'his'. بدهراسان is a fused poetic adjective (بِ + هراسان) meaning 'causing terror/terrifying' and بود is the past copula 'was'. Word order (prep/agent + noun + pronoun + adjective + بود) is typical of Persian poetry where the copula comes at the end.
segments.s653-g506: This clause states a state of the earth. زمین 'the earth' is the subject, زیر 'beneath' modifies تختش 'his throne' (تخت + possessive ـش), and then تن آسان بود is a verbless nominal predicate meaning 'the body/thing was easy/at rest' (تن here used poetically of the land being 'in repose'). بود is the past copula placed at the end as usual.
segments.s653-g507: دگر 'again' (adverb) begins the clause; گفت here functions as the verb 'said' (but the provided sense list did not include the verbal sense); مردم 'the people' is the subject; توانگر 'wealthy' is the predicate noun and بچیست (ب + چیست) is an interrogative formula meaning 'who/what is (he)?' or 'who is it?'. The clause is a reported question introduced by 'said'.
segments.s653-g508: This is a second rhetorical question. به گیتی 'in/within the world' (به used poetically where Modern Persian more often uses در), پر از رنج 'full of suffering' modifies گیتی, و درویش کی ست is a short question 'and who is the poor (one)?' with کی (who) + ست (is). Word order keeps the copula ست at the end, as in older Persian.
segments.s653-g509: چنین 'thus' + گفت 'spoke/said' (verbal; provided senses did not include the past-verb meaning) introduces آنکس 'that person' followed by a relative clause که هستش بسند: که (who) + هستش (he is / it exists) and بسند is an old/poetic form meaning 'is enough' (بس است). The clause means 'the one for whom that is enough spoke thus.'
segments.s653-g510: This is an elliptic vocative/imprecatory sentence. ببخش is an imperative 'grant' or 'forgive' (here 'grant' fits the requestive sense), followed by the addressee خداوند چرخ بلند 'Lord of the high wheel/sky' (an honorific for God). The verb imperative appears before the vocative for forceful prayer-like address.
segments.s653-g511: This is a rhetorical negative question. کسی 'anyone' (here in the indefinite/anyone sense) + را is an object/topic marker, کجا (literally 'where') is used rhetorically together with نیست 'is not' to mean 'is there anyone...?' بخت 'fortune' is the noun being discussed. انباز is an archaic/uncertain word in the manuscript (sense not clear from the provided list), so its precise meaning is obscure here; syntactically it modifies بخت. The clause ends with نیست (is not) forming the negative existential.
segments.s653-g512: This is a plain copular sentence: بدی 'evil/harm' + در جهان 'in the world' is the topic, followed by بتر از آز نیست 'is not worse than greed' — بتر (comparative 'worse') + از (than) + آز (greed). The final نیست negates any greater evil: 'there is no evil worse than greed.'
segments.s653-g553: مکن is the archaic negative imperative 'do not'. نیک مردی here is a compound noun meaning 'noble/generous conduct' (literally 'good manliness'). به جای کسی is a prepositional phrase 'in the place of / on behalf of someone' (به + جای = 'instead of / for the sake of'); the whole segment gives an imperative prohibition: 'Do not (perform) noble conduct for someone...'.
segments.s653-g554: که introduces a relative/subordinate clause 'who/that'. پاداش نیکی is the object 'the reward of the good deed'. نیابی is the negative present/optative form of یافتن (here functionally 'you will not get/receive/find'), and بسی (= بس) means 'enough'/'sufficient'. Together: '...who you will not obtain sufficient reward from.'
segments.s653-g555: گشاده دلانرا is a marked definite/particular phrase: گشاده (generous/open) + دلان (the hearts/people with heart) + را (object/definiteness marker) — 'the open‑hearted'. بود is past 'was' and بخت یار is the predicate nominal 'fortune (was) a friend' i.e. 'fortune favored'.
segments.s653-g556: This is an elliptical relative clause: انوشه is a proper name; کسی 'someone' (indefinite) + کو is an archaic relative/particle here meaning 'who' (word order is inverted), بود is past 'was', and بردبار is the predicate adjective 'patient/forbearing' — 'Anoushe was someone who was patient.'
segments.s653-g557: هران کس (هر آن کس) is an archaic/poetic compound meaning 'every person who / whoever'. که introduces the relative clause. جوید همی is the verbal phrase 'seeks' with the enclitic/old-fashioned prefix همی marking continuous or habitual action (here 'seeks/aims for'). برتری is the direct object 'superiority / preeminence.' The order is subject ('whoever') + relative + verb + object, as common in classical Persian.
segments.s653-g558: هنرها 'the arts/skills' is the topic/object being sought after. بباید is the modal 'must/ought' (literary form), and بدین داوری is a prepositional phrase 'by/with this judgment' (بدین = به + این 'by/with this', داوری = 'judgment/test'). Together the segment states the obligation/necessity: 'the arts (or those seeking superiority in them) must be dealt with by this judgment.'
segments.s653-g559: "یکی" here introduces the first item in an enumeration ('one/first'). "رای و فرهنگ" is a coordinated noun phrase ('counsel/opinion and learning/erudition') that serves as the semantic subject, and "باید" is a modal verb ('must/ought') asserting necessity. "نخست" is an adverb/ordinal marker ('first') that locates this requirement as the first point; the word order is poetic but means 'first: counsel and learning are necessary'.
segments.s653-g560: "دوم" marks the second item ('second'). "آزمایش" (test/trial) is the noun under discussion, and "بباید" is a poetic/metrical form of the modal 'must' indicating obligation. "درست" is an adjective/adverb modifying how the test must be conducted ('properly/correctly'); the clause reads 'second, the test must be proper'.
segments.s653-g121: بفرمای is an imperative/hortative verb (polite command) meaning 'bid/let (it be so)'; تا introduces a subordinate clause of purpose/result ('so that' / 'let them...'); پیش تو places the action 'in your presence' (prepositional phrase 'before you'); بگذرند is present-subjunctive/imperative-plural 'they pass' (used here as the action commanded). Together: 'Command that they pass before you.'
segments.s653-g122: پی خویشتن is a prepositional/adverbial phrase literally 'following themselves' meaning 'of their own accord' or 'on their own'; بر زمین is the location 'upon the ground' (بر = on/upon + زمین = ground); بسپرند is a subjunctive/imperative-plural of سپردن used idiomatically here as 'to cast/throw/commit (themselves)' — so the clause means 'and let them, on their own, cast themselves down on the ground.'
segments.s653-g123: بپرسیم is the 1st-person-plural hortatory/subjunctive 'let us ask' (we should ask); زان = 'from that / of that' marks the person from whom the question is to be asked (object of asking); ناسزای دلیر is an epithet: ناسزای 'ill-deserving / disgraceful' modifies دلیر 'brave one' — a paradoxical negative epithet applied to a hero.
segments.s653-g124: که introduces a subordinate (indirect question/explanatory) clause 'about how/that'; چون here functions as a temporal/comparative 'as/when' (classical usage = 'when'); اندر آمد is a past-tense verbal phrase 'he came in/approached' (اندر = 'in, into' + آمد = 'came'); به بالین شیر is a locative/goal phrase 'to the lion’s bedside/pillow' (به = to/towards, بالین = pillow/bedside, شیر = lion).
segments.s653-g125: ز here marks agency/instrumentality ('by/with/from'); بیگانه is 'the stranger/foreigners' (agent or source of the work); ایوانش is 'his iwan/porch' (ایوان + -ش possessive suffix); پردخت کرد is a verbal phrase where پردخت (classical form of پرداخت) means 'finished/embellished' and کرد is the auxiliary 'did/caused' — 'he caused the iwan to be finished/ornamented (by foreigners).'
segments.s653-g126: درکاخ is a locative phrase 'in the palace' (در + کاخ); شاهنشهی is the adjective 'imperial' qualifying کاخ; سخت here functions adverbially 'firmly/strongly'; کرد is the past-tense verb 'made/did' — 'he firmly established/made strong the imperial palace.'
segments.s653-g127: بتان is the plural noun 'idols/beautiful ones' used here as 'the beauties' (the beloveds/figures of beauty); شبستان 'bedchamber/inner chamber' is the locus; آن شهریار 'that sovereign/king' is the possessor — the word order (بتان شبستان آن شهریار) is possessive: 'the beauties of that king's chamber.'
segments.s653-g128: برفتند is the simple past plural 'they went forth/departed' (بر- + رفتند); پر is an adjectival particle 'full (of)'; بوی, رنگ و نگار are a triad of nouns 'scent, color, and ornament/beauty' that describe them — 'they went forth, full of fragrance, color, and beauty.'
segments.s653-g617: سخنگوی is an agent noun 'the speaker' (subject). چو here introduces a temporal/clausal clause 'when/once'. بر گشاید is a verb phrase with the separable prefix بر + گشاید (classical present form of گشودن 'to open, begin'); سخن is the object 'speech' and follows the verb as is common in Persian poetic word order (S + temporal + V + O).
segments.s653-g618: بمان is a second-person singular imperative 'stay/remain' addressing the listener. تا introduces the subordinate clause of time/purpose 'until' and governs بگوید, a third-person present/subjunctive form 'he may say / he says' (classical usage after تا). تو تندی مکن is an explicit subject 'you' + predicate: تندی 'haste' as direct object/complaint and مکن the archaic negative imperative 'do not do' (نـ + کن), so 'do not be hasty.'
segments.s653-g619: زبان را uses را as a topical/definite marker attached to 'zabān' (speech/tongue) — the phrase is presented as the theme. چو ... بود forms a conditional/temporal clause 'if/when ... was'; با دل 'with the heart' is an adjunct to بود, and راستی is the predicative noun 'truthfulness' describing the state. The clause literally: 'If the tongue was truthful with the heart.'
segments.s653-g620: ببندد is a third-person present/subjunctive-ish form 'it will be closed / it will shut' (classical narrative mood); ز هر سو is a prepositional phrase 'from every side' and درکاستی is a noun/adverbial 'for lack/shortcoming.' Together they express result: 'shortcomings will be closed off from every side.' Word order places the verb before the source phrase for emphasis (poetic inversion).
segments.s653-g621: ز marks source 'from idle speakers' (بیکار گویان is a participial noun phrase 'those who speak idly'). تو دانا شوی is the main clause 'you become wise' with شوی (classical present form of شدن) as second-person singular 'you become.' The construction expresses that contact with such talkers can nevertheless make one knowledgeable.
segments.s653-g622: نگویی here functions as an admonitory negative ('do not say' / 'do not speak') — the negative prefix نـ appears on the verb. ازان سان 'in that way/from that sort' is an adverbial phrase, followed by a relative/adverbial clause containing کزو ('from which / from whom') + بشنوی ('you hear' — second person present/subjunctive), so the clause means 'do not say it in the way which you heard (from them).' The relative link is compressed (typical poetic ellipsis).
segments.s653-g623: ز دانش marks 'from knowledge' (the source). دربی‌نیازی is a compound meaning 'to be free from need / to have no want'; مجوی is the negative prohibitive 'do not seek' (archaic-courteous imperative addressed to second person). The clause literally instructs: 'Do not seek (to gain) exemption from need through knowledge.'
segments.s653-g624: وگر چند is a concessive 'and if, however, though some...' where چند here means 'some/a little.' ازو 'from it' (knowledge) marks source; سختی 'hardship' is the subject of آید 'may come / comes' (third-person present), and بروی (lit. 'go upon you') indicates that the hardship befalls you — classical idiom meaning 'come upon you.' The whole clause concedes that knowledge may bring some hardship to you.
segments.s653-g1057: همان is a demonstrative meaning 'that/the same', placed before the following nouns to pick them out; here it introduces two items joined by و. ساوه and یزدگرد are proper names, and دبیر (placed after یزدگرد) is an appositive title 'the secretary', modifying یزدگرد.
segments.s653-g1058: به پیش اندرون is a locative phrase: به + پیش 'to/in the presence of' plus اندرون 'the inner quarters/inner chamber'; there is no explicit verb, so the predication 'stood/came' is understood. بهمن تیزویر is the addressee (proper name with epithet).
segments.s653-g1059: به marks the indirect object 'to Bozorgmehr'; آن زمان is an adverbial phrase 'at that time' modifying the verb; گفت is the past verb 'said' and شاه (the subject) follows for emphasis — typical inverted order in classical Persian.
segments.s653-g1060: که introduces the reported content (here an imperative/command). دل را is the direct object with the definite marker را. بیارای is an imperative/optative form of 'آراستن' ('adorn/beautify'); بنمای is the imperative of 'نمودن' ('show, reveal'), and راه is its object 'the way/path' (metaphorical).
segments.s653-g1061: ز + من (زمن) functions as 'from me' = 'to me' in classical usage; راستی is an adverb 'honestly/straightforwardly'; هرچ دانی is a relative phrase 'whatever you know' (دانی = 'you know'); بگوی is an imperative 'say' (classical imperative form, here addressing the addressee).
segments.s653-g1062: مجو is the negative imperative 'do not seek'; به کژی expresses the means/instrument 'by crookedness' (به used adverbially/instrumentally); ازجهان آبروی = 'the world's honour/reputation' (آبروی governed by ازجهان).
