The whole clause warns against seeking worldly honour by crooked means.
segments.s653-g1063: پرستش is the subject 'worship', چگونه 'how/what manner', and است is the copula 'is'; فرمان من is the predicate 'my command' (فرمان qualified by possessive من). The clause sets up an explicative definition: 'Worship is my command...'.
segments.s653-g1064: نگه‌داشتن is a verbal noun/infinitive 'to keep/observe' and functions as the content of فرمان ('my command is to...'). رای و پیمان من lists the objects: 'my counsel/opinion/plan and my covenant' — both governed by نگه‌داشتن.
segments.s653-g1121: سخن is the noun 'speech/words' (subject). کان is an archaic/relative particle here introducing the type of person ('one who...'); it does not match the modern pronouns exactly. نه is negation applying to the following predicate. اندر خورد با خرد is a poetic predicate meaning 'fits/accords with wisdom' (اندر = 'in/with', خورد here functions as the verbal/adjectival element 'befits/comes to', and با خرد = 'with wisdom'). Word order is topic (سخن)... then relative/qualifier then predicate.
segments.s653-g1122: بکوشد is a third-person present/poetic form 'he (or one) strives/tries'. که introduces a subordinate clause ('that/so that'). بر پادشا نشمرد is a verb phrase where بر = 'toward/against' and پادشا = 'the king'; نشمرد = negative present 'does not count/does not reckon' (here 'not hold it against him' or 'not reckon it to the king'). The subject of بکوشد is the person introduced earlier; the clause says what he strives for.
segments.s653-g1123: فزونست is a poetic present 'is more/there is more'. زان دانش = 'of that knowledge' or 'than that knowledge' (زان is an archaic pointer 'of/than that'). اندر جهان is the locative 'in the world'. Together this clause sets up a comparative/existential: more of that knowledge exists in the world.
segments.s653-g1124: که introduces the content clause 'which/that'. بشنید here is best taken as passive/impersonal 'is heard' (or 'one can hear'); گوش = 'ear' (the faculty), آشکار و نهان = 'open and hidden' (a pair 'whether public or secret'). The clause means 'that which the ear hears, whether openly or secretly.'
segments.s653-g1125: کسی را is a marked object/topic 'the person whom...'; را is the definite object marker. که introduces the relative clause 'who/that'. شاهِ جهان = 'the king of the world' (compound title). خوار کرد is a causative/perfective phrase 'made (him) despised/humbled' (خوار as adjective 'despised', کرد 'made/did'). The whole relative clause describes 'the person whom the king humbled.'
segments.s653-g1126: بماند is present/poetic 'remains' (third person). همیشه = 'always/ever'. روان = 'soul/spirit'. پر ز = 'full of' (پر as 'full (of)') and ز is the poetic preposition 'from/because of'. درد = 'pain/sorrow'. The clause states the resulting state: his soul remains always full of pain/sorrow.
segments.s653-g1127: همان ... آن بود is a cleft-like construction: همان = 'that/that very one', در جهان 'in the world', ارجمند 'esteemed/valuable', آن بود = 'that was (the one)'. Word order is emphatic/poetic: it identifies who was esteemed in the world.
segments.s653-g1128: که introduces the relative clause explaining 'that one': با او 'with him' (i.e., toward him), لب شاه خندان بود literally 'the king's lip was smiling' — a Persian idiom meaning 'the king smiled on him' or 'the king was pleased with him'. خندان is an adjectival/participial form 'smiling' and بود is the copula 'was'.
segments.s653-g265: هران کس که ... کند is a relative/free conditional clause meaning “whoever …”. هران ( contraction of هر آن) + کس = “whoever, any person who”; که introduces the clause. دانش فرامش کند is a verbal phrase where فرامش (an older/poetic form for فراموش) ‘forget(fulness)’ is used with the auxiliary/finite کند to form ‘forgets (his) knowledge’. The verb کند is present/habitual here, so the whole clause reads “whoever forgets knowledge.”
segments.s653-g266: زبان را marks the definite/affected object (‘the tongue’) with را placed after the noun. به گفتار is a prepositional phrase meaning “in/when speaking” (به = in, گفتار = speech). خامش (poetic variant of خاموش) ‘silent’ is predicated of the tongue and is followed by کند, the same present/habitual verb as above, producing “he makes the tongue silent / keeps the tongue silent.” The object-before-verb order and repetition of کند give a parallel construction with the previous clause.
segments.s653-g267: چوداری here functions as a substantive/adjective ‘a generous one’ (چودار = generous). به دست اندرون خواسته is an idiomatic sequence: به دست = ‘at hand/within reach’, اندرون = ‘the inner part/household’, and خواسته (past participle/adjectival use) = ‘desired’ or ‘wished for’; together they mean ‘wishes to have at hand inside (his house)’. Word order places the descriptive locative/possessive phrase after the head before the predicate adjective.
segments.s653-g268: This is a simple noun list (زر و سیم و اسبان = ‘gold and silver and horses’) followed by the postpositive adjective/predicate آراسته ‘adorned/arrayed’. In Persian poetry the adjective often follows the things described; آراسته here qualifies the listed riches as prepared or ornamented for showing/use.
segments.s653-g269: هزینه چنان کن is an imperative: هزینه ‘expenditure’ + چنان ‘thus/so’ + کن (imperative of کردن) = ‘make/arrange your spending thus’. که introduces a purpose/result clause: بایدت کرد literally ‘that it do what is necessary for you’ — بایدت (باید + ـت = ‘it is necessary for you’) + کرد (do/make) gives the sense ‘so that it befit/serve you’. The verb کرد is used in the result clause rather than a separate verbal construction.
segments.s653-g270: نشاید is an archaic/poetic impersonal negative meaning ‘it is not fitting/should not be’. گشاد ‘loose/wasteful’ is the predicate: ‘it is not fitting that it be loose’. The conjunction و links the second clause نباید فشرد where نباید is impersonal prohibition ‘one must not’ and فشرد (from فشردن) ‘to squeeze/press’ expresses the opposite extreme (i.e. do not be stingy). The pair forms a balanced antithesis: neither too loose nor too tight.
segments.s653-g271: خردمند ‘wise (man)’ is the head; کز is the classical contraction of که + از and here introduces a relative clause meaning ‘who, from (the) enemies …’. دشمنان دور گشت is a verbal phrase: دشمنان = ‘enemies’, دور = ‘distant’, and گشت (become) gives ‘became distant/keeps himself far from enemies’. The verb is perfective/resultative ‘became/was made distant’.
segments.s653-g272: تن دشمن او را places تن ‘person/body/force’ with the possessor دشمن او ‘his enemy’ and the object marker را (emphatic/definite). چو مزدور گشت is a simile/result: چو = ‘as/like’ and مزدور گشت = ‘became a mercenary/paid follower’. Together the clause says the enemy’s person/body becomes (to him) like a hired man — i.e. the enemy’s力量 serves him. Note the result-state verb گشت (became) and the simile introduced by چو.
segments.s653-g169: گزارنده خواب را: گزارشنده (a person who reports or interprets dreams) is the direct object marked by the enclitic object marker را. Here the phrase means “the dream‑reporter / dream‑interpreter.” بدره داد: بدره is a poetic noun (literally ‘full moon’ used metaphorically as a bright or splendid gift), and داد is the simple past ‘gave.’ Together the clause says he gave the dream‑reporter (a) badreh (a brilliant/gorgeous gift).
segments.s653-g170: ز اسب وز پوشیدنی بهره داد: ز is the classical/prepositional 'from'; the phrase lists what he gave ‘from’ (i.e. he allotted out of) the stock of horses and clothing. وز here represents an archaic contraction of و ز (‘and from’) joining the two items. پوشیدنی is ‘garment/clothing,’ بهره is ‘a share’ or portion, and داد is the same past verb ‘gave’ applying to these objects.
segments.s653-g171: فرومانده از دانش او شگفت: فرومانده is a past/predicative adjective meaning ‘overcome, abashed, overwhelmed.’ از + دانش او gives the cause: ‘by/at his learning’ (او here is the genitive 'his'). شگفت is an adjective ‘astonished’ used as a supplement: the whole phrase means he was overwhelmed by the man’s learning and amazed.
segments.s653-g172: ز گفتارش اندازه‌ها برگرفت: ز = ‘from’ (classical/prepositional). گفتارش is گفتار + ـش (his speech / his words). اندازه‌ها is plural ‘measures/standards/limits.’ برگرفت is the simple past of برداشتن used here in the sense ‘took up / adopted / derived.’ Together: he derived or adopted measures/standards from what he said.
segments.s653-g173: نوشتند نامش به دیوان شاه: نوشتند is third‑person plural past ‘they wrote/inscribed.’ نامش = نام + ـش ‘his name’ is the object. به دیوان شاه uses به to indicate destination/entry into the register (دیوان as the royal register/office) belonging to the king (شاه). The clause states that officials entered his name in the king’s rolls.
segments.s653-g174: بر موبدان نماینده راه: بر here functions as a prepositional element meaning ‘over / as (appointed over).’ موبدان is the plural of موبد (mōbed, Zoroastrian priest). نماینده is ‘representative’ and راه (‘way’) functions idiomatically with نماینده to mean ‘representative and guide’ or ‘one charged with showing the way’ to the priests. The phrase completes the sense: they entered his name as representative/guide over the mobeds.
segments.s653-g175: فروزنده شد نام بوزرجمهر: فروزنده is an adjective/epithet ‘radiant / illuminating.’ شد is the past of شدن ‘became.’ نام بوزرجمهر is the subject phrase ‘the name of Bozorgmehr.’ Word order is slightly topicalized (predicate before subject): it says that Bozorgmehr’s name became radiant (i.e. rose to fame).
segments.s653-g176: بدو روی بنمود گردان سپهر: بدو = به + او ‘to him / unto him.’ روی = ‘face’ (here ‘the face’ of the heavens). بنمود (بینمود) is past ‘showed / revealed.’ گردان سپهر is a poetic noun phrase ‘the turning/revolving sky’ (the heavens). The clause literally: ‘the turning heavens showed their face to him,’ i.e. heaven turned toward him.
segments.s653-g769: چوباشد is a poetic contraction of چو باشد, a conditional 'if/when there is' placed before the subject. جهانجوی is the subject 'world-seeker/ambitious person', and با فر و هوش is a prepositional complement 'with glory and intelligence' that describes the subject; word order is conditional marker + subject + circumstantial complement.
segments.s653-g770: نباید is an impersonal negative modal 'one must not/should not'; که introduces the subordinate clause. دارد is present 'has' (possession) and combines with the idiom داشتن به ... گوش (to lend an ear to). به marks the target of attention, بدگوی is 'slanderer/one who speaks ill', and گوش here means 'ear/attention' (lend an ear).
segments.s653-g771: ز introduces a causal/ablative relation 'from/because of'. دستور بد here means 'bad counsel/evil guidance' and گوهر is used poetically as 'essence/quality' (so: bad counsel and evil utterance). The nouns are joined by و 'and' to list causes ('bad counsel and bad speech').
segments.s653-g772: تباهی is the subject 'ruin/destruction'. به marks the direction/target and combines with دیهیم شاهی: a poetic phrase meaning the kingship becoming 'rustic/peasant-like' or degraded; رسد is the present/poetic 'befalls/comes to' (i.e. 'ruin befalls the kingship').
segments.s653-g773: نباید is impersonal 'one should not'; it governs the infinitive/masdar شنیدن 'to hear/to heed'. ز نادان سخن is the source phrase 'the speech of the ignorant' (ز = 'from/of'), so the whole segment means 'one must not listen to the words of the ignorant.'
segments.s653-g774: چو here is conditional/time 'when'. بد گوید is a present-tense clause 'he speaks ill' (گوید = 'says/speaks'). از داد means 'about/of justice' (از = concerning), and فرمان مکن is a negative imperative 'do not issue a decree' (مکن = 'do not'). The line warns: if he speaks badly about justice, do not follow his counsel with a command.
segments.s653-g775: همه modifies راستی: 'all/entire uprightness/truth'. باید + infinitive (آراستن) is the necessity construction 'must/ought to adorn/preserve'. In context this means 'all truth/rectitude must be honored or upheld.'
segments.s653-g776: نباید که is the impersonal negative 'it must not be that...' followed by the clause دیو آورد کاستن. دیو is literal 'demon' (used figuratively for destructive forces), آورد here means 'bring about/cause', and کاستن is the infinitive 'to lessen/diminish'. The clause cautions against allowing a demon (or ruin) to cause diminution.
segments.s653-g321: سرتاجور (saratājūr) is a compound noun 'head‑crown' placed before فر to name the object on the head; فر یزدان is a genitival phrase 'the farr (royal glory) of God.' بود is the past copula linking the whole noun phrase: 'the crown ... was the divine farr (from) God.'
segments.s653-g322: خردمند here functions as a nominal subject 'the wise man'. ازو ('az-ū') is an ablative/causal 'because of him / from him.' شاد و خندان are two coordinated predicative adjectives 'glad and smiling'; بود is the past copula 'was.' The clause reads: 'The wise man was glad and smiling because of him.'
segments.s653-g323: ازآهرمنست is a fused predicate meaning 'is of Ahriman / is demonic' (از + آهرمن + است). آن introduces 'that one' (demonstrative). کزو is a relative/ablative 'from whom' and شاد نیست negates having joy: 'one from whom there is no joy' (نیست = 'is not').
segments.s653-g324: دل و مغزش is a coordinated possessive phrase 'his heart and his brain/mind.' از before دانش gives a source/contents relation 'of/from knowledge,' آباد functions as a predicative adjective 'inhabited/filled/prosperous (with),' and نیست is the negative copula: 'his heart and brain are not inhabited by knowledge' (i.e. knowledge does not dwell in them).
segments.s653-g325: شنیدند is 3rd‑person plural past 'they heard.' گفتار مرد جوان is the object phrase 'the speech/words of the young man' (noun + genitive modifier). Word order is Verb + object here: 'they heard the young man's words.'
segments.s653-g326: فروبست is past of فروبستن 'shut/closed' with فرتوت را as the definite object 'the old man' (را marks the object). ز + و (here functioning together as زو = 'from him / by him') plus زبان ('tongue' = 'speech') expresses the instrumental/agent: 'with his speech he silenced/closed the old man's mouth.'
segments.s653-g327: پراگنده is an adjective 'scattered/widely dispersed' placed before the verb. گشتند is past plural 'they became' (so 'they became scattered' = 'they dispersed'). زان انجمن is an ablative phrase 'from that assembly' indicating where they dispersed from.
segments.s653-g328: پر از آفرین is a predicative phrase 'full of praise' (پر = 'full (of)'; از introduces the content). روز و شبشان is a time phrase 'day and night' with a possessive clitic implying 'their day and night' and dهن 'mouth' is the nominative/possessed focus: the whole means 'their mouths were full of praise day and night.'
segments.s653-g897: The phrase is a compact, somewhat poetic word-group: برین = بر + این ('بر این' = 'after this / upon this / besides'); نیزبگذشت is a fused form of نیز + گذشت (گذشت is past tense 'passed', with the adverbial نیز 'also' attached before it). یک هفته ماه places the time expression after the verb in a poetic order: 'a week's (lit. one week of the moon/month)' — here ماه functions as the temporal noun (the moon/month) associated with the week and will serve as the logical subject in the next clause.
segments.s653-g898: نشست is used as the past action 'sat' / 'took a seat' (verb meaning 'to sit, settle'). از بر تخت is a poetic locative construction meaning 'upon the throne' (بر تخت = 'on the throne', with از used here as part of the verbal phrase indicating movement/position), and پیروز شاه is a noun phrase 'the victorious king' modifying تخت; word order is verb + locative + modified noun phrase.
segments.s653-g899: به یک دست here means 'in/at one hand' (a prepositional phrase marking location/possession). موبد is the noun 'mōbed' (Zoroastrian priest). که بودش وزیر is a relative clause: که = 'who/that', بودش is the past of بودن with the enclitic -ش ('it/was to him' — i.e. 'who was for him' = 'who served as his'), and وزیر 'vizier' is the predicate of the clause. The whole segment describes an item/person held in one hand and qualifies him with a relative clause.
segments.s653-g900: به دست دگر mirrors the previous prepositional phrase: 'in the other hand' (دگر = 'other'). یزدگرد is the proper name 'Yazdegerd' and دبیر 'dabbir' is 'secretary/scribe' describing his role. The verbless construction (no main verb here) is typical: two parallel prepositional phrases list the people/items at each hand of the king.
segments.s653-g901: همان is the demonstrative 'that/same' and begins the phrase; گرد بر گرد او is a doubled adverbial/frame meaning 'round/about him' (گرد = 'around', بر گرد = a poetic prepositional cluster reinforcing 'around'). موبدان is the plural noun 'mobeds' and is the main noun of the segment. Word order is emphatic/demonstrative fronting followed by the circumstantial phrase and the subject noun.
segments.s653-g902: سخن‌گو qualifies the mobeds: here it means 'speakers' or 'eloquent' (i.e., skilled in speech). چو = 'like' (a simile particle). بوزرجمهر جوان is a simile target: the name Bozorgmehr with the adjective جوان 'young' — together 'like the young Bozorgmehr.' The segment thus likens these eloquent priests to the young Bozorgmehr.
segments.s653-g903: به بوزرجمهر 'to Bozorgmehr' is the indirect object fronted for emphasis; آن زمان 'at that time' gives the temporal setting (آن = 'that/then', زمان = 'time'). گفت شاه places the verb 'said' (گفت) before the subject 'شاه' — a common epic inversion: 'the king said.' The sequence literally reads 'to Bozorgmehr at that time said the king.'
segments.s653-g904: که‌ای here is the vocative/exclamatory address (classically 'که ای' = 'O!') followed by مرد 'man' as the addressee. پر + دانش is a compound meaning 'full of knowledge' (پر = 'full (of)', دانش = 'knowledge'). و نیک‌خواه links with 'and' to give 'and well-wishing' — i.e. 'O man, full of knowledge and a well-wisher' — a direct address to Bozorgmehr. Note that که‌ای is an older/poetic pattern combining a particle and the vocative 'ای'.
segments.s653-g561: سیوم is an ordinal 'third' placed before the clause; یار is the noun 'companion' (vocative/subject). باید is the modal 'must/ought' expressing necessity. بهنگام کار is an adverbial phrase 'at the opportune time of action' (بهنگام = timely / at the right moment; کار = action/work) and modifies باید.
segments.s653-g562: ز is the preposition 'from' (here introducing the domain to be assessed); نیک and بد are the contrasted nouns 'good' and 'bad'; برگرفتن is a verbal infinitive 'to take up / to take' and here governs شمار, meaning 'to take/count/assess'; شمار is the object meaning 'count/tally' — together = 'to take account of good and bad.' Note: وز in the line appears as a poetic/orthographic form and does not serve its listed literal senses here.
segments.s653-g563: چهارم is the ordinal 'fourth' introducing the next rule. که here introduces the following clause ('that' / 'so that'); مانی is a vocative/name addressed ('O Mani'). بجا modifies کام and means 'in the proper/right place'; را is the definite/object marker attached to کام, marking it as the thing under consideration.
segments.s653-g564: ببینی is a present/subjunctive 2nd-person singular form 'you should see / you must see' (the b- prefix is the classical present/subjunctive marker). ز آغاز is a prepositional phrase 'from the beginning' (ز = from, آغاز = beginning). فرجام را is the object phrase 'the outcome/end' with را marking it; the clause thus means 'see the outcome from the beginning.'
segments.s653-g565: به پنجم functions adverbially as 'fifthly / to the fifth point' introducing the item. اگر is the conditional 'if'. زورمندی is an abstract noun 'strength / might'. بود is the past/copular 'was/were' used here to state the conditional premise ('if there is/was strength').
segments.s653-g566: به تن is an adverbial phrase 'to/for the person' or 'by the self' (تن here meaning the person/self, so 'by one's own body/effort'). کوشش is the noun 'effort/endeavour' that is the means; آری is an emphatic adverb 'indeed' (reinforcing the assertion). بلندی is the predicate 'elevation/highness' and بود is the copula 'was/will be' attaching to it — overall: 'by one's own effort there is indeed elevation.'
segments.s653-g567: وزین is an adjective meaning 'weighty / dignified' qualifying the following noun phrase. هر is the distributive 'each / every'. دری in this poetic context functions as a noun ('matter' / 'gate/subject') — its exact sense is idiomatic/poetic (not given in the provided sense list). جفت گردد is a verbal phrase 'becomes paired' (جفت = pair, گردد = becomes); سخن is the noun 'speech/words' and is the subject/thing that 'becomes paired' — literally 'from each weighty matter words become paired.'
segments.s653-g568: هنرخیره is a compound meaning 'dazzling/striking art or skill' (the object of the prohibition). بی‌آزمایش is an adjective 'untested / without trial'. مکن is the negative imperative 'do not' — together: 'do not attempt/show dazzling skill without testing.'
segments.s653-g481: نگه‌داشتن is the infinitive/gerund 'to keep/preserve' used as a verbal noun here; it governs the following object phrase. مردم خویش is a noun phrase 'his own people' where خویش is split into خوی + ش in the lexical breakdown: خوی carries the kinship/’own’ sense and ش is the enclitic possessive 'his/her'. را is the definite/object marker placing emphasis on that object.
segments.s653-g482: گسستن is an infinitive meaning 'to separate/loose' and here describes freeing or releasing; تن 'body' is its direct object (followed by از indicating separation 'from'). از رنج درویش is a prepositional phrase 'from the suffering of the dervish/poor man'; را again marks/emphasizes the (definite) object phrase being mentioned.
segments.s653-g483: سپردن is the infinitive 'to entrust/hand over' used as a verbal noun. به + noun phrase ('به فرهنگ فرزند خرد') means 'to/into the care of the culture/education of a child'; فرهنگ here denotes upbringing/education rather than 'dictionary'. فرزند خرد literally 'a small/young child' (خرد used here with the sense 'small').
segments.s653-g484: که introduces the explanatory clause 'for/that ...'. گیتی 'the world' is the object of the implicit verb; بنادان functions as a dative/target phrase 'to the ignorant' (بنادان = 'to/for the ignorant'). نشاید is an impersonal classical form 'it is not fitting/should not', and سپرد is the infinitive 'to be entrusted' — together meaning 'the world should not be entrusted (to the ignorant)'.
segments.s653-g485: چو (here encoded as چوفرمان) is the conditional 'when/if'. فرمان پذیرنده is a compound idea 'command-receiving' = 'obedient' (پذیرنده 'one who receives/accepts' modifies فرمان), and باشد is the subjunctive/correlative copula 'is/would be' placed before the noun that follows; the clause thus reads 'if the son is obedient' with پسر finally naming who is obedient.
segments.s653-g486: نوازنده is a predicate meaning 'one who soothes/caresses (or comforts)' rather than merely 'musician' in this moral/ethical context. باید is the modal 'must/ought', که introduces the subordinate clause, and باشد is the copula; پدر at the end is the noun being predicated — 'the father ought to be (that) one who soothes/nurtures.'
segments.s653-g487: بپرسید here is a past narration form 'he asked' (classical/simple past). دیگر functions as 'again/further' modifying the asking. که introduces the content/question clause 'which/that', and فرزند راست is 'the rightful/true son' (راست = true/right).
segments.s653-g488: به نزد پدر is a locative phrase 'in the presence of/with the father' (نزد = near/in the presence of). جایگاهش is 'his place/status' (the possessive -ش attached to جایگاه), کجا is the interrogative 'where', and ست is the classical copula 'is' (here completing the question 'where is ...').
segments.s653-g305: چنین گفت is a simple statement 'thus (he) said' with the adverb چنین preceding the past verb گفت; کز is the archaic/poetic fused form of که + ز meaning 'that' or 'that from', introducing the subordinate clause; خسرو دادگر places the ruler (خسرو) followed by the adjective/epithet دادگر 'just' in attributive position.
segments.s653-g306: نپیچید is a negative verb form (ن + پیچید) used here as a prohibitive/negative infinitive/finite idea 'not to turn/deviate'; باید is the impersonal modal 'it is necessary / one must'; به اندیشه سر is a prepositional phrase where به + اندیشه = 'in/to thought' and سر here is used idiomatically for 'mind/inner attention' (i.e. 'in one's thoughts'). Word order is poetic (verb before باید).
segments.s653-g307: کجا here has a rhetorical/exclamatory force ('when/where/how'), followed by چون 'as/like'; شبانست is the fused verb 'is a shepherd' (شبان + است), and ما گوسفند is the predication 'we are sheep' with subject ما and predicate گوسفند (the copula is understood).
segments.s653-g308: و‌گر (= و گر) is the conditional 'and if'; the clause ma زمین او سپهر بلند contains inverted/elliptical word order: ما (we) as one term is equated to زمین (the earth) while او (he) is predicated as سپهر بلند 'the high/lofty sky' — بلند is the adjective 'high/lofty' qualifying سپهر.
segments.s653-g309: نشاید is an impersonal present/fixed expression 'it is not fitting/it must not'; گذشتن is the infinitive 'to pass/overstep' and with ز + پیمان اوی means 'to go beyond / break his covenant' — ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of', and اوی is the older possessive/pronoun form meaning 'his'.
segments.s653-g310: نه here functions as 'nor' (continuing the prohibition); پیچیدن is the infinitive 'to turn / to deviate' used metaphorically; از رای و فرمان اوی literally 'from his counsel and command' (از = from, رای = plan/counsel, فرمان = command), with اوی again the possessive 'his'.
segments.s653-g311: بشادیش is a prepositional/construct form meaning 'for his joy' (ب + شاد + ایش), followed by باید 'it is necessary'; که introduces the subordinate clause 'that' and باشیم شاد is the present-subjunctive formulation 'that we be happy' (باشیم = 'we be', شاد = 'happy').
segments.s653-g312: چو here = 'when'; داد زمانه can be read as 'when the time/age gives' (داد as verb 'gave/gives' with زمانه as subject) and بخواهیم داد puts بخواهیم (present-subjunctive or jussive 'let us desire / we should want') with داد as its object meaning 'justice' — so the clause urges: 'when the age grants, let us seek justice.'
segments.s653-g1073: هرآنکس که برپادشا دشمنست is a relative clause introduced by هرآنکس 'whoever'. برپادشا is an older/compounded prepositional phrase meaning 'against/upon the king' (بر + پادشاه contracted). دشمنست is the copula + predicate 'is an enemy' (stressed by the suffix -ست, an archaic poetic form of است), so the whole segment = 'whoever is an enemy to the king'.
segments.s653-g1074: روانش پرستار آهرمنست: روانش is روان + enclitic possessive -ش 'his soul'. پرستار here is a predicative noun meaning 'worshipper/adorer' (not merely 'caretaker' in this context). آهرمنست is the identifier 'is of Ahriman' (آهرمن + -ست), so the clause means 'his soul is a worshipper/servant of Ahriman' (i.e. devoted to the demon).
segments.s653-g1075: دلی کو ندارد تن شاه دوست: دلی 'a heart' (poetic noun) is followed by کو, an archaic relative/particle here functioning like 'that/which' (classical usage). ندارد is present negative 'does not have/hold'. تن شاه دوست is a compact phrase: تن (body/person) + شاه (the king) + دوست (friend); the sense is 'that does not make the king's person its friend.' Word order is typical classical Persian (relative + verb + object cluster).
segments.s653-g1076: نباید که باشد ورا مغز و پوست: نباید is an impersonal modal 'it should not / must not'. که links the clause ('that'). باشد is the present-subjunctive/correlative form of 'to be' ('be' / 'exist as'). ورا = و + را (here = 'to him' / object-direction), and مغز و پوست 'brain and skin' are the body-parts enumerated; the phrase as a whole means 'he should not be left even with brain and skin' (i.e. should not be spared).
segments.s653-g1077: چنان دان که آرام گیتیست شاه: چنان دان is an imperative or hortative 'consider/know thus' (چنان = 'thus', دان = 'know/consider' in an imperative sense). که introduces the explanatory clause. آرام گیتیست شاه literally orders as 'king is the world's calm/peace' (گیتیست = 'is the world' / a fused predicate meaning 'of the world'); the sense: 'the king is the world's peace/settlement'.
segments.s653-g1078: چونیکی کنیم او دهد دستگاه: چونیکی is a fused adverb/noun meaning 'if/when one does a single good deed' (چون = 'when/as', یکی = 'one'); کنيم is 1pl present/subjunctive 'we do' (used generically 'one does'). او is 'him' (the king), دهد is 3rd-person present/subjunctive 'gives/rewards'. دستگاه here means 'a post/court office' or 'establishment', so the clause = 'when one does him one good turn, he grants a post.'
segments.s653-g1079: به نیک و بد او را بود دست رس: به نیک و بد 'as/to good and bad' introduces the sphere; او را with the object-marker را points back to 'him' (the king or the person in question). بود دست رس is an idiomatic inversion: 'there was (بود) hand (دست) reach (رس)' — i.e. 'was within hand's reach' or 'was at his disposal'. Together: 'Good and bad were at his disposal.'
segments.s653-g1080: نیازد به کین و به آزرم کس: نیازد here functions as 'has no need / does not need' (from نیازیدن meaning 'to need' in older usage). به کین و به آزرم کس lists what is not needed: 'vengeance (کین) and آزرم (an older word for respect/honor/esteem) of any person (کس)'. So the clause says he does not need vengeance nor the honor/respect of anyone. Note: آزرم is somewhat rare/archaic here and means 'honor/respect' rather than a proper name.
segments.s653-g881: همان سفله = 'that wretch/lowly fellow' (demonstrative + pejorative noun). کز is the archaic/poetic relative 'که + ز' meaning 'who/from whom'; it introduces a relative clause. هر کس آرام و خواب is the internal object of that relative clause: هر کس (each/any person) + آرام (peace, calm) و خواب (sleep). The word order is demonstrative + noun, then the relative clause introduced by کز, ending with the coordinated nouns آرام و خواب which are things withheld from the person.
segments.s653-g882: ز دریا دریغ آیدش روشن آب = 'from the sea the clear water is withheld from him.' ز (poetic 'from') marks source (دریا = sea). دریغ (here 'withholding/reluctance') is the nominal predicate; آیدش is a verb form plus enclitic pronoun -ش ('it comes to him' literally, idiomatically 'isfalling to/affecting him' → 'is denied him'). روشن آب (lit. 'clear water') is the thing withheld; adjective precedes noun in this poetic order. The clause completes the relative begun by کز in the previous segment.
segments.s653-g883: وگرباد = the fused poetic conjunction + conditional 'و گر باد' meaning 'and if (a) breeze...'. نوشین is an adjective 'pleasant/delightful' modifying an implied باد (breeze); بتو = prepositional phrase 'to/for you'. برجهد is a present/subjunctive/optative-style verb form meaning 'were to rise/blow toward' (poetic diction). The clause sets up a hypothetical conditional.
segments.s653-g884: سپاسی ازان برسرت برنهد = 'it would place upon your head a thanks from that (breeze).' سپاسی (a noun) = 'a thanks/token of gratitude'; ازان = 'from that/it' (referring back to the breeze). برسرت is the prepositional phrase 'upon your head' (بر + سر + -ت enclitic 'your'). برنهد is a present/future/optative poetic verb 'to place on' (برنهد completes the conditional started earlier). Word order is object (سپاسی) + source (ازان) + goal (برسرت) + verb (برنهد), a common poetic arrangement.
segments.s653-g885: بهفتم خردمند = 'the seventh (بهفتم) wise (خردمند)' — بهفتم is an ordinal/adverbial-ordinal ('seventh') used nounally here; خردمند follows as a qualifying noun/adjective. کاید here is a verbal/nominal root meaning 'who plots/contrives' or 'who acts with craft'; in context it functions as part of a relative clause. به خشم = 'into/with anger' (به + noun) indicates the state that triggers the action. The clause describes a particular wise person and his reaction when angered.
segments.s653-g886: به چیز کسان برگمارد دو چشم = 'he fixes/sets (برگمارد) two eyes upon (به) the matters (چیز) of people (کسان).' به + چیز کسان marks the target/objects of attention ('on the affairs of people'); برگمارد is a present/optative poetic verb 'places/appoints/fixes' and دو چشم is the direct object ('two eyes' = both his eyes). The structure is target phrase → verb → object, a common poetic ordering where برگمارد governs دو چشم.
segments.s653-g887: بهشتم به نادان نماینده راه = 'my paradise (بهشتم) to an ignorant (نادان) representative/guide (نماینده راه)'. بهشتم is the possessed noun 'my paradise' (poetic fusion of بهشت + ـم); به here functions as the preposition 'to/for' marking the intended addressee/recipient in the action expressed in the next segment. نماینده راه is a compound 'representative/agent of the way' = 'guide'. Word order places the possessed noun first and the ill-chosen recipient (نادان نماینده راه) after it.
segments.s653-g888: سپردن به کاهل کسی کارگاه = 'to entrust (سپردن) to a sluggard (به کاهل) anyone’s workshop (کسی کارگاه).' سپردن is the infinitive/nominalized verb 'to entrust/hand over'; به + کاهل marks the recipient ('to a lazy one'); کسی کارگاه is a genitival construction 'someone’s workshop' (کسی = anyone/someone modifying کارگاه). The phrase expresses the second parallel thing one would not do, completing the negative idea implied in the previous segment.
segments.s653-g137: دگر باره is an adverbial phrase meaning “once again / anew” placed before the verb for emphasis. بر پیش is a short prepositional phrase meaning “before/in the presence of” and immediately precedes the verb. بگذاشتند is the simple past (they) + b- prefix form of گذاشتن, a transitive past tense meaning “they placed / set.”
segments.s653-g138: همه (everyone) is the subject. خواب را uses the postposition را to mark خواب as the definite/highlighted object. خیره is an adjectival/adverbial modifier here (“astonished / marveling”), and پنداشتند is past tense ‘they thought/considered’; together the clause means they regarded that object as a dream, with خیره qualifying the manner of their supposition.
segments.s653-g139: غلامی is the subject noun (“a young servant / slave”); پدید آمد is a verbal compound meaning “appeared” (پدید + آمد). اندر میان is a prepositional phrase meaning “in the midst / among,” placed after the verb phrase in this poetic word order.
segments.s653-g140: به بالای is a locative/placement expression ‘onto / upon the top of’ (به + بالای). سرو is the noun ‘cypress’ and و links the following descriptive phrase. بچهر is an adjectival phrase meaning ‘having the face/countenance of…’, and کیان is plural ‘kings’ (here used as an epithet); together they describe the appearance of the servant.
segments.s653-g141: تنش is composed of تن (“body”) plus the enclitic ش (his). The reduplicated لرز لرزان intensifies trembling (“trembled, all shaking”); this is followed by به کردار بید, a simile phrase ‘in the manner (کردار) of a bīd (willow)’—so the clause describes vigorous, willow‑like shaking.
segments.s653-g142: This clause centers on دل (heart) as topic. از + جان شیرین (literally ‘from / of sweet life/beloved life’) introduces the cause or relation; شده is the past participial/auxiliary meaning ‘has become’ and نا + امید expresses ‘without hope / hopeless’. Word order places the cause phrase before the predicative state (ناامید).
segments.s653-g143: کنیزک is the noun ‘little maidservant’. بدان is a classical locative/demonstrative (بد- + ان) meaning ‘in/at that’ (i.e. ‘in that chamber’), and حجره هفتاد بود follows with the numeral and the past copula: literally ‘in that chamber were seventy (maidservants)’. The verb بود is simple past ‘was/were.’
segments.s653-g144: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause ‘such that / who’. هر یک means ‘each one’. به before تن سرو indicates a comparison or likeness (‘with respect to the body, like a cypress’). آزاد here modifies سرو (literally ‘free / noble’) and بود is the past copula: ‘each one had a body like a noble cypress.’
segments.s653-g369: دگر گفت is a short narrative clause meaning “another said” (دگر = another; گفت = said). کاندر begins the reported clause (‘that/in which’), so the whole segment sets up the quotation: “another said that, in a wise man…”. Word order: speaker phrase + connective (کاندر) + noun phrase (خردمند مرد).
segments.s653-g370: هنرچیست is the fused rhetorical question ‘هنر چیست’ = “what is the virtue/skill?” (هنر = virtue/skill; چیست = what is). هنگام introduces a time phrase (“at the time of…”). ننگ و نبرد is the time described: ننگ = disgrace/shame, و = and, نبرد = battle/war. The clause asks what skill/virtue a wise man has in such times.
segments.s653-g371: چنین گفت = “thus he said.” کان کس که is a poetic relative/indefinite construction meaning “whoever / someone who” (کان کس = someone; که introduces the subordinate relative). آهوی خویش is a noun phrase ‘his own gazelle’ built from آهوی (gazelle) + خوی (kin/own) + ش (possessive clitic ‘his’).
segments.s653-g372: ببیند is a present/subjunctive-type verb in a relative clause here meaning “who sees” or “on seeing.” بگرداند is a causative/active verb ‘turns/causes to turn’ (used here as ‘turns aside/changes’). آیین و کیش = ‘rites and worship/customs and faith’ (آیین = ritual/custom; و = and; کیش = worship/creed). The verbs are chained: seeing the gazelle is linked to turning away from rites.
segments.s653-g373: بپرسید is a past/formal narrative verb ‘(they/he) asked’ used to introduce a question. دیگر here means ‘further/again’ (they asked further). که introduces the content clause; در زیستن is a locative/verbal noun phrase ‘in living / in one’s life’ (در = in; زیستن = to live).
segments.s653-g374: چه سازی literally ‘what making/what contrivance’ (چه = what; سازی = making/measure) = ‘what course/measure (should one take)’. که introduces the result/goal clause ‘so that…’. کمتر بود is a past-form predicate ‘would be less’ (کمتر = less; بود = was/would be). رنج تن = ‘the suffering of the body’ (رنج = suffering; تن = body). Together: ‘what measure so that bodily suffering would be reduced’.
segments.s653-g375: چنین داد پاسخ = ‘thus he gave an answer’ (چنین = thus; داد = gave; پاسخ = answer). که‌گر is a fused conditional particle (که‌گر ≈ اگر) meaning ‘if’. با خرد = ‘with wisdom’ (با = with; خرد = wisdom). This opens the conditional clause that follows.
segments.s653-g376: دلش = ‘his heart’ (دل = heart; ش = his). بردبارست is a predicate formed from بردبار ‘patient’ + است (here attached as -ست in poetry) = ‘is patient.’ رامش برد is a result clause: رامش = tranquility/peace, برد = ‘gains/takes’ (classical poetic verb). Sequence: if his heart is patient through wisdom, he (thereby) gains tranquility.
segments.s653-g209: زبان ('tongue') is used idiomatically for 'speech' or 'talk' and appears as the subject; برگشادند is a past plural verb (بر + گشاد- + -ند) meaning 'they opened' / 'they began to speak'; برشهریار is a prepositional phrase 'to/ before the king' (بر + شهریار written together), so the whole clause means 'they spoke to the king.'
segments.s653-g210: کجا is an interrogative 'where'; بود is the past copula/existential 'was'; داننده را خواستار is a noun phrase with را marking the definite/focal object (داننده = 'knower') and خواستار used as a noun 'petitioner/one who seeks' — literally 'the petitioner for the knower.' Word order 'کجا بود X' = 'where was X.'
segments.s653-g211: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; بوزرجمهر is the proper name 'Bozorgmehr'; آن سخنها is 'those words' (آن = 'those/the', سخنها = plural 'words/speeches'); شنید is the simple past 'he heard.' Altogether: 'When Bozorgmehr heard those words...'.
segments.s653-g212: بدانش here functions as a fused locative/relational phrase (بدان + -ش) 'toward him / at that (him)'; نگه کردن is the light-verb construction 'to keep a gaze / to gaze' (نگه = 'gaze', کردن = light verb 'to do'); شاه دید is the main clause 'the king saw.' The clause as a whole means 'the king saw that he fixed/kept his gaze on him.'
segments.s653-g213: یکی is an indefinite pronoun 'someone' or 'one of them'. آفرین کرد is the common classical phrasing 'said “Afarin”' (an exclamation meaning 'Bravo!' or 'well done') where آفرین is the exclamation and کرد is the past of کردن used to form the verbal phrase. و links to the next action; بر پای خاست is a fixed phrase 'rose to his feet' (بر + پای + خاست, with خاست the archaic past 'arose').
segments.s653-g214: چنین گفت is 'thus he said' (چنین = 'thus/so', گفت = 'said'), introducing direct speech. کای is the vocative particle 'O' used to address someone. داور here is the addressee noun 'judge/arbiter'. داد is used as the abstract noun 'justice' (not the verb 'gave'), and راست is an adjective 'true/right' qualifying the judge; together the phrase is an honorific address: 'O Judge, (giver of) justice and truth.' Note: گفت in the lemma list is given as 'conversation' but here functions as the past-tense verb 'he said.'
segments.s653-g215: This is an optative wish headed by the final particle باد (“may”). زمین بنده is elliptical for “may the earth be (your) servant” with بنده as a predicative noun; تاج وتخت تو places two nouns in coordination followed by the possessive تو (“your”), i.e. “your crown and throne.” باد at the end marks the whole clause as a prayer/wish.
segments.s653-g216: فلک روشن از روی is another wish-clause: فلک “the heavens/sky” + روشن “bright,” with از here meaning “because of” (a causal use) attaching روی “face.” The conjunction و links the next coordinated wish بخت تو باد (“may your fortune be”), and the optative باد again gives the whole line the sense “may …”. Note the possessive تو appears with بخت but is understood as relevant to the preceding phrase by poetic ellipsis.
segments.s653-g393: وگر is the conditional 'if' introducing a clause. بر + ستاننده marks the beneficiary/target ('to/upon the taker/collector'); آرد is the 3rd-person present/poetic form of 'bring' (آوردن) and takes سپاس ('praise/thanks') as its object, so آرد سپاس = 'brings praise.' Word order is poetic (preposition + noun, then verb + object).
segments.s653-g394: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from'/'out of' indicating source or basis; بخشنده is 'the giver' (the one who gives). بازارگانی functions as a substantive/adjectival qualifier 'merchantry/marketly' (relating to trade), and شناس is used in the sense 'recognize/know' (a root meaning 'one who knows' applied here as an instruction/predicate: 'know/recognize as ...'). The whole segment means roughly 'from the giver, recognize (him) as a merchant.'
segments.s653-g395: دگر 'another' (here: another person/voice) precedes گفت 'said' (poetic past). کز is the relative/connector 'that/of which' linking to the question; مرد پیرایه چیست is a fused clause 'what is the ornament of a man?' with چی 'what' and ست (چیست in archaic form) as the copula 'is.' Note ست is the older/persian poetic copula used instead of است.
segments.s653-g396: وزان is a linking prepositional phrase 'and from that' / 'and from those' continuing the inquiry; نیکوییها is the plural 'goodnesses/virtues/good deeds' (note the plural -ها). گرانمایه is an adjective 'precious/valuable'; چی(ست) again asks 'what is...?' — so the clause asks 'and what is the value/preciousness of those good deeds?'
segments.s653-g397: چنین 'thus/so' modifies the speech act; داد is the past 'gave' and پاسـخ is the object 'answer' (دادِ پاسخ = 'gave an answer'). که introduces the content clause. بخشنده مرد places the adjective 'generous' before 'man' (a common poetic order): 'the generous man.'
segments.s653-g398: کجا is the interrogative/relative 'where' used here in the sense 'where/wherever'; نیکویی 'goodness/benefaction' is the object; با + سزاوار ('with/by deserving') forms a phrase meaning 'in a manner that is deserved' or simply 'where it is deserved/appropriate'; کرد is the past 'did/made' serving as the main verb of the clause. Poetic word order places the object before the verb.
segments.s653-g399: ببالد is an optative/present form meaning 'may he be proud' or a general present 'he takes pride'; به کردار = 'in/with regard to conduct' (به + noun, introducing the domain); سرو بلند is the simile 'lofty/tall cypress' (سرو = cypress, بلند = tall/lofty), so the phrase counsels pride in noble conduct like a tall cypress.
segments.s653-g400: چو here means 'when/as'; بالید is the past form 'grew/rose/flourished' (same root as ببالد but here temporal: 'when he is exalted'); هرگز 'never' negates the following; نباشد is the poetic negative copula/auxiliary 'is not' / 'will not be' (used here to state a general consequence), and نژند is the adjective 'base/worthless' — together: 'when he grows high, he will never be base.'
segments.s653-g273: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. داد (here) is the past tense verb 'gave' (poetic repetition follows). تن خویشتن is a genitival phrase 'his own body' (تن = body, خویشتن = self/own). مرد at the end is the subject 'the man' — Persian poetry often places the subject after the verb phrase (verb–object–subject order here). The doubled داد is a stylistic/poetic repetition emphasizing the act of giving/sacrificing.
segments.s653-g274: چنان دان is an imperative: 'consider/think thus' (دان = 'consider/know' used as a hortative). که introduces a content clause 'that…'. پیروز شد is a two-word predication where پیروز = 'victorious' and شد = 'became' (past), and در نبرد is the locative/circumstantial phrase 'in battle'. Altogether: 'consider him such that he became victorious in battle.'
segments.s653-g275: مگو is the negative imperative 'do not say'. آن سخن = 'that saying/phrase'. کاندرو is an opaque/poetic compound here introducing the source/thing 'from which' (the provided sense list does not cleanly match this usage), and سود نیست = 'there is no profit/benefit'. The whole clause warns against repeating a saying that yields no benefit.
segments.s653-g276: کزان = 'from which' (ک + ز + آن compressed), آتشت = 'your/that fire' (آتشت = fire/ardor, poetic form), بهره = 'share' or 'benefit', جز = 'except', دود = 'smoke', نیست = 'is not/there is not'. Word order is standard: prepositional/source phrase first then predicate 'is nothing but smoke'.
segments.s653-g277: میندیش is an imperative 'consider/think'. ازان = 'from that' / 'of that'. کان is an archaic/poetic relative particle meaning roughly 'which' (closest provided sense is 'as if', but here it functions as a relative), نشاید = 'it is not fitting/should not be' (a stative/impersonal negative form), and بدن = 'the body'. The clause instructs the listener to reject what is unfit for the body.
segments.s653-g278: نداند کس is an inverted negative construction: 'no one would consider/think' (نداند here = 'would not deem/does not deem'). آهن is 'iron' (poetically 'steel/weapon'), به introduces the target/comparison 'against/for', آب = 'water', and آژدن = 'dragon'. The line states that nobody would regard iron as appropriate against a dragon's water (i.e., iron is useless there).
segments.s653-g279: فروتن بود شه is a predicate in past tense: adjective 'humble' (فروتن) + بود 'was' + شه 'the king' (subject placed after predicate, a poetic inversion). که introduces a relative clause 'who/that', and دانا بود = 'was wise'. So: 'The king was humble, who was wise.'
segments.s653-g280: به دانش بزرگ is a prepositional phrase 'in great learning/knowledge' (به = 'in/regarding', دانش = 'knowledge', بزرگ = 'great'). و = 'and' links the next predicate, توانا بود = 'was powerful/capable' (توانا = able/powerful, بود = 'was'). This completes the relative clause describing the king.
segments.s653-g385: دگر کو = 'another who' (دگر 'another') + کو used as an archaic relative/interrogative marker; بسستی نشد is a negated predicate: بسستی (slowness/laziness) + نشد (past of شدن 'become') yields 'did not become slow' or more naturally 'did not slacken'; پیش کار ('before the task') is a prepositional/temporal phrase placed after the verb phrase, typical of Persian poetic word order.
segments.s653-g386: چو دید = temporal clause 'when he saw' (چو = 'when'); او is the third-person pronoun 'he/she'; فزونی بدروزگار is a noun phrase 'the increase/excess of ill-fortune' where فزونی = 'increase/excess' and بدروزگار = 'bad fortune / adversity'; the clause as a whole is 'when he saw the increase of bad fortune.'
segments.s653-g387: دگرگفت is a single verbal phrase 'another replied/said' (دگر = 'another', گفت = 'said'); کزبخشش combines کِ/کَ + بخشش and means 'from/of generosity' or 'which of generosity' as the first item in a comparison; نیک‌خوی ('good-natured' / 'virtuous') is the contrasted noun. The phrase lists the two qualities being compared.
segments.s653-g388: کدامست = 'which is it?' (interrogative copula); نیکوتر is the comparative 'better/more virtuous' (نیکو + -تر); از هر دو سوی literally 'of/between the two sides' = 'of the two' (از + هر دو + سوی), marking the comparison scope. Word order follows a poetic inverted question form.
segments.s653-g389: کجا is the interrogative 'where'. در introduces the locative phrase 'in/within'. دو گیتیش (literally 'the two giti‑sh') means 'the two realms/worlds' (گیتی 'world' + the enclitic/possessive-like -ش fused in the manuscript form), and بارآورد here is a past/preterital verbal form meaning 'bore/produced' (literal 'brought forth'). The word order is locative (کجا در ...) + place + verb, typical of Persian poetry.
segments.s653-g390: بسالی is an adverbial expression 'in a year / within a year'. دو بارش is 'two بار (times/occasions)' with the enclitic ش = 'its' (i.e. 'it twice' or 'two times of it'), so the phrase reads 'in a year, two times of it'. بهارآورد is a compound verb 'bring spring' (here 'caused spring to come' or 'produced a spring'), so the whole segment means 'in one year it brought spring twice.' Note the enclitic ش attaches to بار to indicate 'its two times'.
segments.s653-g391: چنین گفت is a conventional poetic formula 'thus (he) said' (چنین = 'thus/so', گفت = 'said'). کان here is an archaic demonstrative/particle introducing 'that person' (roughly 'that/one who'), followed by کس 'person' and که 'who/that' starting a relative clause. با + خواسته literally 'with desire' = 'willingly / of his own will.' The segment sets up the relative clause 'who, willingly ...'.
segments.s653-g392: ببخشش is the noun/gerundial/inflected form of بخشیدن/بخشش meaning 'granting/forgiveness' or 'giving' and here functions as the object of کند 'does/makes' (کند is the present/future auxiliary/verb 'does'). جانش is جان + -ش 'his soul/life' (enclitic pronoun) and آراسته is participial/adjectival 'adorned, beautified.' The clause means 'grants forgiveness (willingly), his soul becomes adorned' (result/state expressed by آراسته).
segments.s653-g641: اگر begins a conditional clause 'if'. تاو (an abstract noun here, roughly 'valor'/'radiance') is the logical subject and is followed by the present-tense verb دارد 'has/possesses' (present indicative). به‌روز is an adverbial phrase 'in/by the day' (i.e. openly/in daylight) modifying نبرد 'battle/struggle', which follows as the nominal object of having the capacity to do (i.e. 'has the strength to wage battle by day').
segments.s653-g642: سر بدسگال is a noun phrase 'the head of the ill‑fated (man)', with the adjective/epithet بدسگال placed after سر as is common in poetry. اندر is the preposition 'into/in', and آرد is an archaic/polite present/subjunctive form of آوردن 'to bring/place' ('brings' / 'may bring' / 'will bring' depending on context). The final بگرد is an archaic verbal form (here best taken as part of the verbal phrase indicating turning/bringing) — in context it contributes a sense of moving/turning the head into place (imperative/jussive flavor in older usage).
segments.s653-g643: گرامی is an adjective 'dear/esteemed'. It is followed by the copula بود 'was' (past). The prepositional phrase بر دل پادشا 'on/over the king's heart' expresses the experiencer/possessor: 'was dear to the king'. Note the adjective + بود + بر phrase order (adjective first, experiencer after بر).
segments.s653-g644: بود again is the past copula 'was' (or a resumptive copula continuing the description). جاودان 'eternal/everlasting' and شاد 'happy/joyful' are adjectives describing the subject; و connects to فرمانروا 'ruler/sovereign'. The sequence is a predicate list after the copula: 'was eternal, joyful, and a sovereign.'
segments.s653-g645: بدو گفت is literally 'to him (بدو) [he] said' — indirect object before the verb, a common Persian word order for 'he said to him.' گرنیستش is a fused conditional: گر (if) + نیست (is not) + -ش (him/it) meaning 'if it were not' / 'were it not'. بهره زین is a noun phrase 'share/benefit of this' (بهره 'share/benefit' + زین an archaic/genitive-like element 'of this').
segments.s653-g646: ندارد is the negative present 'does not have / would not have' (here used to mean 'would have no need'). پژوهیدن is the infinitive 'to study/investigate' and functions as the verbal noun governed by ندارد. آیین و دین are the objects of that studying: آیین 'rites/customs' and دین 'religion' joined by و 'and'. The overall meaning: 'he would not have the need to study rites and religion.'
segments.s653-g647: چنین داد پاسخ is a formulaic 'thus gave answer' — literally 'thus gave an answer', i.e. 'he replied thus.' The following که introduces the content clause. آن به که is an idiomatic sequence meaning roughly 'it is better that' (آن ... به که = 'better that / rather that'), and مرگ is the subject 'death' of the subordinate clause.
segments.s653-g648: نهد is the verbal form of نهاد/نهادن 'to put/place' (here 'may/should place' or simple present 'places'). بر سر او is a prepositional phrase 'upon his head' (بر = on, سر = head, او = him). یکی تیره ترگ is the object: یکی 'one/a single' + تیره 'dark/ominous' + ترگ 'rag/shred' — 'a single dark rag/shroud placed upon his head.'
segments.s653-g697: بپرسید is an archaic/epic past verb (equivalent to پرسید, 'he asked'); the verb appears before its subject (شاه) — a common poetic word order. زِبن (ازبن) here functions as از + بن ('from the root/origin') and, with و از نژاد, forms a coordinated prepositional phrase meaning 'about/from origin and lineage.'
segments.s653-g698: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/about.' The following nouns (تیزی، آرام، فرهنگ، داد) are listed as qualities being inquired about; تیزی here means 'keenness/sharpness' (mental or martial), آرام 'calm/composure', فرهنگ 'learning/culture', and داد 'justice.' The repeated و simply coordinates the items.
segments.s653-g699: ز ('from') marks the source; شاهی is the abstract noun 'kingship.' وز is an archaic/poetic form (here not the literal 'gust' or 'weight') functioning as a compounded connector 'و + ز' ('and from'), so وز داد reads 'and from justice.' کنداوران is a plural agent noun (کنداور + ان) meaning 'those who search/investigate' or 'investigators.' The verb is omitted but implied, a common poetic compression.
segments.s653-g700: ز 'from' again marks source or extent; آغاز 'beginning' paired with وفرجام ('and the end') gives the span 'from beginning and end' (i.e., throughout). نیک is an adjective 'good/auspicious' modifying the following noun; اختران (here 'stars' in the astronomical/astrological sense) names the auspicious signs. (The lemma senses provided for اختران do not match this usage.)
segments.s653-g701: سخن کرد is a verbal phrase 'he spoke' (سخن = speech, کرد = did/said). زین here functions as 'از این' ('from these' / 'of these') and links to موبدان; موبدان خواستار is a noun phrase 'the mobeds who desired/requested' (خواستار as an adjectival/participle).
segments.s653-g702: به پرسش گرفت is an idiom meaning 'he put (something) to question' or 'he questioned' (گرفت = took up). آنچ آید به کار is a relative phrase 'that which comes to use' = 'what is relevant/necessary'; آید is present-tense 'comes' used here in a general relative sense.
segments.s653-g703: به + proper name marks the addressee ('to Bozorgmehr'). آن زمان is a temporal phrase 'at that time' placed before the subject; شاه گفت is the clause 'the king said' (literally 'the king spoke').
segments.s653-g704: که introduces the content of what was said ('that/asking that'); رخشنده گوهر is a noun phrase 'the radiant jewel' (رخشنده adjectival), and برآر is an imperative/hortative form 'bring forth' (second-person; heroic/poetic command). از نهفت is a prepositional phrase 'from concealment/what is hidden' (نهفت = hidden).
segments.s653-g977: روان (here an adjective) describes being fluent or flowing in speech ('روان در سخن گفتن' = 'fluent in speaking'). 'در سخن گفتن' uses the masdar/gerund 'گفتن' with 'در' to mean 'in speaking'. 'آژیرکن' is an imperative/poetic compound (آژیر + کن) meaning 'sound an alarm' or 'alarm!' directed at the addressee; word order is inverted for poetic force (adjective/phrase then the imperative).
segments.s653-g978: 'کمان کن' is an imperative construction where the noun 'کمان' (bow) comes before the verb 'کن' (do/make), i.e. 'make (or set) a bow' or 'make into a bow'; 'خرد را' is the definite object ('خرد' = wisdom) marked by 'را' — therefore 'make wisdom into a bow'. 'سخن تیرکن' uses 'تیرکن' (here verbal: 'shoot/arrows') applied to 'سخن' so the phrase means roughly 'let speech shoot like arrows' or 'let speech be the arrow'; the line uses concise, poetic imperatives and inverted order.
segments.s653-g979: 'چو' introduces a temporal/conditional clause ('when' or 'if'). 'رزم آیدت' is a verb phrase with enclitic object 'ت' ('battle comes/befalls you') and 'پیش' here means 'before/against you' or 'in your presence'. 'هشیار باش' is an imperative: 'be alert' — typical Persian order here places the temporal clause before the imperative exhortation.
segments.s653-g980: 'تنت را' is the direct object ('تن' body + enclitic 'ت' = your body) marked by 'را'. 'ز دشمن' is the prepositional phrase 'from the enemy' (poetic form 'ز' = از). 'نگهدار باش' combines a verb-like root 'نگهدار' (preserve/protect) with the imperative 'باش' — literally 'be a keeper' but functionally 'guard/keep safe'.
segments.s653-g981: 'چو' again introduces 'when/if'. 'بدخواه' is the agent 'an ill-wisher' or malefactor. 'پیش' means 'before' (in your presence). 'توصف' here means a complaint/description/accusation presented against you; 'برکشید' (literally 'raised' or 'lifted') is used of bringing up or raising that accusation — a past/aorist verbal form used generically ('whenever he raises...').
segments.s653-g982: 'تو را' is a dative/emphatic object ('for you'); Persian often uses 'را' with personal pronouns in such constructions. 'رای و آرام' = 'counsel and calm' (وآرام is 'and calm' written together). 'باید گزید' is an impersonal obligation: 'it is necessary to choose' — practically 'you ought to choose counsel and remain calm.' 'گزید' is the root 'choose' used with 'باید' to express duty.
segments.s653-g983: 'برابر' here means 'equal' or 'an equal/opponent'. The sequence 'برابر چو بینی' is a poetic inversion: 'چو بینی' = 'when you see' (بینی is 2nd-person present), and 'کسی هم‌نبرد' specifies 'someone who is your fellow‑combatant / rival in battle' (هم‌نبرد = rival/comrade‑in‑arms).
segments.s653-g984: 'نباید' is impersonal negative obligation 'one must not / it should not be'. 'که' introduces the subordinate clause. 'گردد' (from گشت) is a subjunctive/passive-style verb here meaning 'become' or 'come to be'. 'تو را' marks the person affected ('your'), and 'روی زرد' = 'a yellow/pale face' so the clause literally says 'it should not be that your face becomes pale' (i.e. do not let yourself turn pale).
segments.s653-g1097: نباید introduces an impersonal prohibition/obligation ('it should not be'); که begins the subordinate clause. سیر آید is literally 'satiety comes' (سیر subject + آید verb), and از کارکرد is a source phrase 'from/out of performance or deeds' (از + noun). The present verb آید is the classical-form present used here after نباید to express what ought not to happen.
segments.s653-g1098: همان ('that same') is the subject/referent; تیز گردد is a predication meaning 'becomes sharp/keen' (گردد is the classical/poetic verb 'become' / 'be'); ز is the poetic form of از ('from' / 'because of'), and گفتار سرد is the cause phrase 'cold/stern speech'. The clause states resultative change caused by speech.
segments.s653-g1099: اگر introduces a condition ('if'). گشن شد is 'became hungry' (گشن adjective/noun + شد 'became'). بنده را uses را to mark the beneficiary/recipient ('for the servant'), and دستگاه is the noun 'provision/retinue' (here a grant or maintenance allocated to the servant). The clause is elliptical in classical style, leaving the copula/predication to the next phrase.
segments.s653-g1100: به repeated before فر and نام functions instrumentally ('by/through' or 'by means of'); فر means the kingly 'farr' or royal glory, and نام here means 'name' in the sense of reputation/fame. جهاندار شاه is a compound epithet ('the world-holding king' = sovereign). The whole phrase supplies the source/instrument by which the servant's دستگاه is provided.
segments.s653-g1101: گر is the conditional 'if'. از ده یکی is a partitive phrase 'one of ten'. باژ 'cry' is the action; خواهد is the classical present/future of خواستن indicating desire/will ('wants/will'); and رواست (روا + ست) is the predicate 'it is fitting/permissible'. The clause expresses that an individual complaint/noise among many is allowable.
segments.s653-g1102: چنان 'thus/so' + رفت 'go/behave' + باید 'must/ought' is a modal statement 'one ought to behave thus'. که introduces the result clause; او را uses را to mark the experiencer/beneficiary; هواست (هوا + ست) means 'it is his whim/desire/mood'. The clause prescribes conduct so that the person's inclination is accommodated.
segments.s653-g1103: گرامی‌تر is the comparative adjective 'more honored/dear' modifying آنکس 'that person'. بود is the verb 'was', and نزد شاه is a prepositional phrase 'in the presence of / with the king' indicating relation or standing before the monarch. Together: 'That person was more honored in the king's eyes/presence.'
segments.s653-g1104: که introduces a relative clause modifying آنکس. چون here functions temporally/conditionally 'when/if'; گشن بیند is 'sees (someone) hungry' (بیند = 'sees'); ورا دستگاه places ورا ('to him') before دستگاه ('provision/establishment'), meaning 'gives him provision'. The clause describes the honored person's charitable action.
segments.s653-g953: نباشد is an older/poetic negative form built on باش (here functioning as a copular/existential verb) and appears before its noun, a common inverted (V‑first) order in classical Persian. خرد جان places خرد ('wisdom') with جان ('life/soul') in apposition: 'wisdom — (there is) life' (i.e. 'wisdom is life' implied), but the initial نباشد negates the existence: 'where there is no wisdom, there is no life.' The sequence نباشد ... نباشد رواست uses روا + است (رواست) at the end: روا is an adjective 'fitting/permissible' and ست/است is the copula contracted into the poetic form ست, giving 'that is not fitting.'
segments.s653-g954: This clause states a positive identity: خرد جان — 'wisdom (is) the soul.' پاکست is the fused poetic form of پاک + است ('is pure'); پاک acts as predicate adjective and -ست is the copula. و links the two predicates. ایزد گو should be read as a compound: ایزد (God) + گو (‑speaking / praising) — an adjectival/epithet form meaning 'God‑speaking' or 'praising God' — followed by است, the straightforward copula 'is.'
segments.s653-g955: چوبنیاد is a compound noun 'wood‑founded / wooden foundation' placed before the following noun. مردی here is the abstract noun 'manliness/courage' (not simply 'a man') and functions as the object/thing learned. بیاموخت is the simple past 3rd‑person 'he learned.' The final مرد is the subject 'the man' (poetic word order: object follows verb and subject can appear after it), so the whole clause reads 'from a wooden foundation (چوبنیاد) the man learned manliness.'
segments.s653-g956: سرافراز is an adjective 'exalted/honored' placed before the verb. گردد (from گشتن/گردیدن) is a present/subjunctive form often used narratively with the sense 'becomes' or 'is made.' به functions here with the instrumental/causal sense 'by/through.' ننگ و نبرد are the instruments/conditions ('shame/disgrace and battle/combat') by which the man becomes exalted — a compact, poetic way to say that suffering and fighting raise a man's status.
segments.s653-g957: ز is the poetic preposition 'from' (here 'from / out of'). دانش نخستین = 'first/primary knowledge' where نخستین modifies دانش. به + یزدان گرای is an instruction/advice: به here marks direction 'toward,' and گرای is an adjectival/verb stem meaning 'incline/turn' (‑oriented / 'be inclined'); together 'turn toward God' or 'be God‑inclined.' The compact phrasing gives the imperative/advisory sense ('from first knowledge be inclined to God').
segments.s653-g958: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause 'that / who.' او هست و باشد combines two copulas: هست (simple present 'is / exists') and باشد (here 'may remain' or 'remain'); the doubling is emphatic/aspirational in poetry — 'that he is and may remain.' همیشه 'always' modifies the following phrase; به جای literally 'in place' or 'in his rightful position.' Overall: 'that he exists and may he always remain in his place.'
segments.s653-g959: "بدو" is a prepositional-like particle meaning 'to that / unto it' and takes the following verb as its complement. "بگروی" is a 2nd-person present/optative form meaning 'you accept/embrace' (classical usage can have an optative/conditional sense). "کام دل یافتی" is a result clause: "کام" (desire) with the possessor "دل" (heart) and "یافتی" (past 2nd-person of 'to obtain') meaning 'you obtain the heart's desire'; together the segment gives a conditional/result sense ('if you accept it, you obtain your heart's desire').
segments.s653-g960: "رسیدی" is the past 2nd-person verb 'you reached/attained'. "به جایی" ('to a place/somewhere') names the destination, and the relative "که بشتافتی" ('that you hastened to') modifies that place; "بشتافتی" is past 2nd-person 'you hastened'. The whole clause means 'you arrived at the place you rushed to.'
segments.s653-g601: چنان دان is a directive phrase: چنان (thus/so) precedes دان (consider/know) giving an imperative or strong assertion 'know/consider thus.' توای is an archaic/poetic second‑person copula/attribute applied to شهریار بلند ('you are a lofty sovereign'), with the noun followed by its adjective (شهریار + بلند) as is normal in Persian.
segments.s653-g602: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause describing the king. از وی = 'from/with him (i.e. from you/him)' and نبیند is a classical negative present ('does not see' / in context 'meets/encounters' — here rendered 'meets') governing کسی ('anyone/no one') with جز گزند giving the exception pattern 'no one (kasi) ... except (jaz) harm (gezand)'. The clause means 'so that no one meets him without harm.'
segments.s653-g603: چو is the temporal 'when.' بر انجمن = 'at/on the assembly' (بر + noun giving location/occasion). مرد خامش بود is a simple past copular clause: مرد (a man) + خامش (silent) + بود (was), so 'a man in the assembly was silent.'
segments.s653-g604: ازان = 'from that' (referring back to the preceding silence). خامشی is the noun 'silence' and دل به رامش بود is 'the heart had/was in tranquility' where به introduces the result/benefit ('to/into') and بود is past copula; literal: 'from that silence, the heart was into tranquillity.'
segments.s653-g605: سپردن به + agent phrase: سپردن 'to entrust' followed by به introduces the recipient. دانای داننده is a stacked modifier (دانا 'wise' + داننده 'one who knows'/'knowing'), forming 'a wise, knowing man.' گوش (ear) is the thing being entrusted (i.e. 'give ear to / listen to'). Word order places the verb-noun infinitive first (typical in classical Persian advisory lines).
segments.s653-g606: This is a twofold result clause: به تن توشه یابد = 'the body finds/obtains provisions' where یابد is present/past-in-general 'obtains' and توشه is 'provisions' for the body (تن). Then به دل رای وهوش = 'and to the heart (come) counsel and understanding' — رای (counsel/plan) and هوش (intelligence) are coordinated; the verb is understood from the first half (elliptical coordination common in poetry).
segments.s653-g607: شنیده سخنها is a noun phrase 'the words/speeches heard' (شنیده = heard, modifying سخنها). فرامش مکن is an instructive negative: فرامش (forgetfulness) + مکن ('do not do') = 'do not forget.' The order places the object (heard words) before the negative imperative 'do not forget.'
segments.s653-g608: که introduces the reason/explanation: تاجست 'it is a crown' (تاج + copular-stylistic -است fused) and برتخت شاهی = 'on the throne of kings' (برتخت = on the throne; شاهی = kingship/kings). سخن (speech/word) is the subject: 'for speech is a crown on the kingly throne.' Note the predicate-before-subject poetic ordering where تاجست sits before the locative phrase.
segments.s653-g793: This is a noun phrase composed of 'یکی' (a certain / one) + 'انجمن' (assembly) followed by a descriptive phrase 'لب پر از آفرین'. 'لب' (lip) is used collectively here and 'پر از' is the adjectival construction 'full of X' with 'از' as the preposition. The whole phrase means 'a gathering whose lips were full of acclamations.'
segments.s653-g794: 'برفتند' is past tense, 3rd person plural (they departed/they went). 'ز' (poetic form of 'از') is the preposition 'from'. 'ایوان شاه' is a genitive: 'the king's iwan/portico'. 'زمین' here names the destination/surface ('the ground'/'floor'). Together: 'they left from the king's portico to the ground.'
segments.s653-g795: 'برین' is the poetic fusion of 'بر' + 'این' meaning roughly 'on/after this' or 'in this respect'; 'نیز' means 'also/too'. 'بگذشت' is past tense meaning 'passed' (of time). 'یک هفته روز' is a dated/poetic way to say 'one week (of days)' — together: 'a week also passed.'
segments.s653-g796: 'بهشتم' here functions as 'به هشتم' (on the eighth); 'چو' is the conjunction 'when'; 'بفروخت' is past 3rd person singular 'sold' (active); 'گیتی‌فروز' is an epithet 'world‑illuminating' used substantively. Grammatically this gives: 'on the eighth (day), when Giti‑froz was sold/put away.' (The exact referent of گیتی‌فروز is semantic/contextual.)
segments.s653-g797: 'بیانداخت' is past tense, 3rd person singular, meaning 'he threw/cast down' (بی‑ is an intensifying/verb-forming prefix + انداختن). 'آن' is the demonstrative 'that' and precedes the noun 'چادر' (here a cloth/veil/covering). 'لاژورد' describes its color/material (lapis/azure). The verb takes 'آن چادر لاژورد' as its object.
segments.s653-g798: 'بیاراست' is past tense 3rd person singular 'he adorned'. 'گیتی' is the direct object 'the world'. The preposition 'به' here introduces the instrument or material ('with/by'), followed by 'دیبای زرد' ('yellow brocade'), so: 'he adorned the world with yellow brocade.'
segments.s653-g799: 'شهنشاه' is the subject 'the emperor'. 'بنشست' is past 3rd person singular 'sat down / took his seat'. 'با' is the preposition 'with' and 'موبدان' is plural of 'mōbed' (Zoroastrian priests). The phrase means 'the emperor sat with the mobeds.'
segments.s653-g800: This is a nominal phrase describing the companions: 'جهاندیده' ('worldly‑wise') linked by 'و' to the compound 'کار کرده' — literally 'work‑done' but used adjectivally as 'experienced/tried'. 'ردان' names the people described; in context it functions as a plural substantive ('men/counsellors/officials'). Grammatically, the adjectives precede the noun they characterize.
segments.s653-g281: هر آنکس که = 'whoever' (هر + آنکس + relative که) introduces a relative clause. او کرده کردگار is a compact, poetic word order: او = 'him/He' (object), کرده = past participle 'made' (literally 'has made'), کردگار = predicate noun 'Creator' (God). In modern prose one would expect او را کردگار کرده, but the object marker را is omitted for metrical/poetic reasons; the phrase means 'whoever has made/considers him the Creator'.
segments.s653-g282: بداند is third-person present(tense/mood) of دانستن used here as 'considers/knows' (i.e. 'deems'). گذشت از بد روزگار follows as the result/clause: گذشت (here: 'passes beyond/escape') + از 'from' + بد روزگار 'ill fate/evil of fortune'. Grammatically the verb بداند governs the following nominal clause as the content of what is considered/known.
segments.s653-g283: پرستیدن داور is a verbal-noun phrase 'worshipping the Judge' (داور here = divine Judge). افزون کند is a third-person present clause 'increases' or 'adds more (to what one has)' — افزون is an adverb/adjective meaning 'more'/'increasing', and کند is the 3rd-person singular of کردن used as 'makes/causes' (so together 'makes greater' / 'increases').
segments.s653-g284: ز دل = 'from/out of the heart' (poetic variant of از). کاوش دیو is a genitival phrase where کاوش ('search/inward restlessness' in poetic use) modifies دیو 'demon' — i.e. 'the demon of anxious or restless thought'. بیرون کند is 'drives out' (بیرون = out, کند = makes/does); word order is verb-final in classical style but here the verb follows the object phrase for rhythm.
segments.s653-g285: بپرهیزد is a 3rd‑person (or jussive/hortative) form meaning 'let him avoid' / 'one should avoid' (the prefix بـ- + verb of پرهیزیدن). از هرچ ناکردنیست = 'from whatever is not doable' (هرچ = هرچه, 'whatever'; ناکردنیست = 'is not possible/to-be-done' formed with negative prefix نا- + کردنی + است). Together: 'One should avoid whatever cannot be done.'
segments.s653-g286: نیازارد is 3rd‑person singular present negative (نیـ + ازارد stem), meaning 'does not harm / spares.' آن را is the definite object (آن + را). که introduces a relative clause: نازردنیست is a negative adjective phrase ('is not to be harmed/does not suffer harm' — formed with نا- + زردنیست in poetic morphology) qualifying 'that.' So: 'Do not harm that which must not be harmed.'
segments.s653-g287: به یزدان گراییم is a first‑person plural hortative/indicative 'let us turn/ incline to God' (گراییم = we turn/are inclined). فرجام کار is a noun phrase 'the end/outcome of affairs' (فرجام = end/outcome, کار = affairs/work) and functions as the object/reason of the verb: 'let us turn to God for the outcome of our affairs.'
segments.s653-g288: که introduces a clause explaining why: روزی‌ده is an epithet 'Giver of provision/sustenance' (روزی‌ده = provider). اویست here is an archaic fused form equivalent to 'او است' ('he is') in older/poetic Persian, and و پروردگار adds 'and (He is) the Lord' — so the clause reads 'for He is the Bestower of sustenance and Lord.'
segments.s653-g537: گهر (subject) + descriptive phrase بی‌هنر modifies it; ناپسندست is a predicative adjective with the copula omitted (classical Persian often drops است). The conjunction و links a second predicate خوار, so the whole clause says ‘a jewel lacking art is unacceptable and low/contemptible.’
segments.s653-g538: برین is the fused prepositional phrase بر این (‘on/against this’). داستان is the object/locution ‘this tale,’ and زد is a poetic use of the verb زدن meaning ‘to sound/perform/utter’ (i.e. to begin or recite). The subject یکی هوشیار follows the verb in inverted order: ‘a certain sensible/wise man.’
segments.s653-g539: که‌گر is the conditional ‘if.’ گل is the subject and نبوید (from بوییدن) means ‘does not emit scent’ (third-person negative). به رنگش مجوی is a negative imperative/advice: مجوی ‘do not seek/prize’ with به رنگش ‘by/for its color’ — ‘do not value it for its color.’
segments.s653-g540: کز is the contracted relative/causal که ز ‘that/from which’; آتش is the source and بروید (from روییدن) means ‘sprang/grew up’ or ‘came forth.’ مگر introduces an exception ‘unless,’ and آب جوی ‘running/stream water’ completes the clause: ‘that grew from fire, unless from a stream.’
segments.s653-g541: توانگر (the rich person) is the subject; به بخشش is a prepositional phrase of means ‘by/through generosity,’ بود is the copula (classical past/formal copula) and شهریار is the predicative noun—‘the rich man is (becomes) a sovereign by giving.’
segments.s653-g542: به گنج نهفته is a locative phrase ‘in hidden treasure’; نه‌ای here expresses existential/negative force in poetic phrasing (literally ‘there is (no) …’) and پایدار is the predicate (‘lasting’). The line means that hidden wealth does not endure. Word order places the locative fronted and the existential remark after it.
segments.s653-g543: به گفتار خوب is an adverbial phrase ‘in/for good speech’; ار is the conditional ‘if’; هنر خواستی is a protasis meaning ‘you wish/desire skill’ — خواستی is the classical conditional/past-subjunctive form used here as ‘if you want.’
segments.s653-g544: به کردار is the means/arena ‘in/by your conduct’; پیدا کند is a verbal phrase where پیدا (apparent/manifest) + کند (‘may make/do’) yields ‘let ... be made manifest’; راستی is the object ‘truth/uprightness.’ Grammatically this is a jussive/result clause: ‘may truth appear in your deeds.’
segments.s653-g945: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from' (variant of از) placed before گیتی 'the world'. یکی (literally 'one') quantifies the verbal-noun بازگشتن 'return'—the infinitive بازگشتن is used as a noun ('a return'). به خاک is a directional phrase ('to dust/earth'), with به governing خاک.
segments.s653-g946: که introduces an explanatory subordinate clause ('for/that'). راهی is the noun 'road/way' (here the subject), followed by the predicative adjective درازست ('is long'—دراز + ست). با بیم و باک is a fixed idiomatic accompaniment meaning 'with fear and dread', where با repeats for emphasis.
segments.s653-g947: خرد 'wisdom' is the wished-for predicate; باشدت is an optative/subjunctive construction 'may it be to you' (may X be yours). زین here functions as 'از این/of this' (poetic 'from/with this') modifying سخن 'speech/these words', and رهنمون is the noun 'guide' that completes the idea 'May wisdom be your guide from these words.'
segments.s653-g948: بدین is the compound 'با + این' meaning 'with/by this' (here given split as ب + دین in the data; treat them together). پرسش 'question' is the means, and اندر 'into' governs چرایی و چون — چرایی 'the reasons/why' and چون 'how' (here interrogative noun 'how'). The whole phrase directs the listener to probe reasons and manner using this question.
segments.s653-g949: The sentence states a predication: خرد 'wisdom' is the subject. مرد را خلعت ایزدیست is the predicate 'a divine robe for man' — خلعت ایزدی ('divine robe') with است fused as ایزدیست. The particle را here links the human beneficiary ('مرد') to the predicate in a possessive/for‑someone sense typical of poetic word order.
segments.s653-g950: سزاوار خلعت is an adjectival phrase 'deserving/fit for the robe'. نگه کن is an imperative 'look/observe/consider' (نگه as 'gaze/watch' + کن imperative 'do'), and که کیست introduces a subordinate clause 'to see who is' — literally 'consider who is deserving of the robe.'
segments.s653-g951: تنومند 'the robust/powerful (one)' is topicalized and followed by را as an emphasis/topic marker. کو is an interrogative/relative 'where/that (there is)', here functioning to introduce the circumstance: '(such) that wisdom is not his companion.' خرد یار نیست literally 'wisdom is not (his) companion' — یار 'companion/ally' governed by خرد.
segments.s653-g952: به گیتی is the locative phrase 'in the world' (به + گیتی). کس here is used in the negative sense 'no one', and او را (object-marked 'him') خریدار نیست states the result: 'there is no buyer for him' — i.e. nobody in the world values/purchases him. را marks او as definite object and نیست negates the existence of such a buyer.
