segments.s653-g489: چنین داد پاسخ که is a fixed literary formula: چنین ('thus') + داد (gave) + پاسخ (answer), followed by که introducing the reported content. نزد پدر is a prepositional phrase 'near/in the presence of the father' (نزد + پدر) that marks the relation or audience for the following claim.
segments.s653-g490: This clause gives the content of the reply. گرامی is a predicate/adjective 'honored, dear'. چوجانست is an archaic fused form (چو جان است) meaning 'as if (it were) life' or 'as dear as life' and functions as the degree-comparison for گرامی; فرخ پسر is the noun phrase 'Farrokh, the son' (name + apposition). Word order is adjective + degree phrase + name.
segments.s653-g491: پس ازمرگ = 'after death' (پس + ازمرگ) sets the temporal frame. نامش بماند به جای is literal ['his name' + clitic ش = 'his' + بماند 'remained/was left' + به جای 'in place/stead'] — a literary way to say 'his name remained' or 'his name was kept in his stead.' بماند here is a 3rd-person verbal form meaning 'remained/was left.'
segments.s653-g492: This is a compact nominal clause listing the name and roles: ازیرا is presented as a proper name (here used as 'Azira'); پسرخواندش is the verb+object fused form 'he called him adopted son' or the noun 'his adopted son' depending on context — the -ش suffix marks 'him/his'; رهنمای is a noun 'guide/adviser' appended as a role. Word order is name + role(s).
segments.s653-g493: بپرسید دیگر is a past-narrative third-person verb 'he asked' (بپرسید) plus دیگر 'further/again' indicating an additional question; که introduces the content of that question. ازخواسته functions as a noun-like phrase 'about what was desired / what he wanted' (from/of + 'desired').
segments.s653-g494: Here که دانی is a purpose/result clause: که + دانی (subjunctive/optative 'that you may know' or 'so that you know') introduces the reason for asking. دارد is the present/continuous auxiliary 'has' (3rd sing.), دل آراسته is the nominal phrase 'a heart adorned/prepared' (دل = heart, آراسته = adorned/ready). Together: 'that you may know that he has an adorned heart.'
segments.s653-g495: Again the reply-opening formula: چنین داد پاسخ که = 'he answered thus that...' followed by the general statement. مردم به چیز places 'people' as subject and 'به چیز' ('by/with things') as the instrumental/causal phrase: 'people are honored by things...' (چنین ... که introduces the clause's content).
segments.s653-g496: گرامیست is the predicate 'is honored/valued.' وز is a poetic fused form of و + ز (i.e. 'and by / and from') used to link the contrasting clause — an archaic connector here meaning roughly 'and by.' چیز خوارست reads as 'things [that make one] خوارست' where خوارست (archaic spelling of خوار است) means 'are despised/lowly'; نیز adds 'also/too.' Overall: 'People are honored by some things, and by some things they are despised as well.'
segments.s653-g729: بدانگه is an adverbial particle meaning “thereupon/at that time.” شد is the past form of شدن ‘become’ placed before its predicate; تاج خسرو is a possessive noun phrase (تاجِ خسرو, “Khosrow’s crown”), and بلند is the predicate adjective “high/lofty.” The clause thus reads literally “Thereupon became Khosrow’s crown high.”
segments.s653-g730: که introduces a subordinate/explanatory clause (“that/for/and”), دانا is a nominal adjective meaning “the wise one,” and بود is the past copula “was.” نزد او = “in his presence / to him,” with نزد used in the sense ‘in the presence of’ or ‘before,’ and ارجمند is a predicate adjective “esteemed/valued.” The clause means “and the wise (man) was esteemed by him.”
segments.s653-g731: نگه‌داشتن is a verbal noun/infinitive ‘to keep/guard,’ here naming the action. کار درگاه is a compound noun phrase ‘the affairs/work of the court’ (کار + درگاه), and را is the definite/object marker attaching to the whole phrase. The phrase functions as the object/topic of the following prepositional phrase.
segments.s653-g732: به here introduces the source or direction of the threat (classical usage; roughly ‘against/from’), زهر is used figuratively as ‘venom/malice,’ آژدن is the dragon/serpent image, کام is ‘desire’ or appetite, بدخواه means ‘ill‑wisher,’ and the final را again marks the definite object. Together the phrase expresses protection of the court’s affairs from the venomous/destructive desire of a malicious (dragon‑like) enemy.
segments.s653-g733: چو is a subordinating particle meaning ‘when/if.’ دارد is the present/habitual third‑person ‘has’ (to possess), ز is the poetic preposition ‘of/from,’ هر دانشی = ‘every kind of knowledge,’ and آگهی means ‘information/awareness’ here. The whole clause reads “when/if he has awareness/information of every knowledge.”
segments.s653-g734: بماند is the present/subjunctive form of ماندن ‘remain/continue’ (here indicating that the sovereign will endure or remain), جهاندار is the sovereign/world‑ruler (subject), با is the preposition ‘with,’ and فرهی denotes the noble/learned or cultured class. Word order (verb before subject) is typical of classical diction; the clause says the sovereign endures accompanied by/among the noble/learned.
segments.s653-g735: نباید is an impersonal modal ‘must not/should not.’ که introduces the subordinate clause; خسبد is the present/subjunctive form of ‘sleep’ used after نباید to express prohibition; کسی دردمند = ‘anyone/someone who is distressed/in pain.’ The structure = ‘No one who is distressed should sleep.’
segments.s653-g736: که again links the explanatory clause. آید is ‘may come / happen’ (here potential), مگر functions in the sense ‘unless/otherwise’ (i.e., ‘lest / otherwise’), شاه را is the object-marked phrase ‘to the king,’ ز + و together render the classical contracted ‘زو’ = ‘from him’ (ز = ‘from’ + the clitic pronoun), and گزند means ‘harm.’ The clause warns that otherwise harm might come to the king from that person.
segments.s653-g681: تن‌آساني و کاهلی is a coordinated object/phrase: تن (body) with the abstract nouns آسانی (ease) and کاهلی (sloth) describes the softness/laxity of the body. دور کن is an imperative composed of دور (make distant/remove) + کن (do) and commands the addressee to remove that bodily ease and laziness.
segments.s653-g682: بکوش is an independent imperative 'strive/endeavor'. وز here is an archaic/prepositional use equivalent to از ('by/through' or 'from'), introducing the means; رنج تنت is a genitival construction 'the toil/hardship of your body' (تنت = تن + enclitic -ت for 'your body'). سور کن is another imperative 'make a feast/banquet' (سور = feast) meaning figuratively 'provide enjoyment/benefit for your body by means of toil'.
segments.s653-g683: که begins a causal/explanatory clause ('for/because'). ایدر is an archaic form of 'here' (اینجا). تو را سود = 'benefit/profit to you' (تو را marks the recipient with the object marker را). بی‌رنج modifies سود ('benefit without hardship'), and نیست is the present negative copula 'does not exist/is not'.
segments.s653-g684: چنان هم که introduces the comparison 'just as/so too that'. بی‌پاسبان is an adjective 'unguarded/unwatched' modifying گنج 'treasure'. نیست negates the clause: 'treasure is not [found] unguarded.'
segments.s653-g685: ازین باره 'از این باره' = 'concerning this matter' (از = concerning; ین = variant of این 'this'; باره = matter/instance). گفتار بسیار گشت is a verbal phrase: گفتار ('speech/talk') + بسیار ('much') + گشت (past of گشت 'became'), i.e. 'much talk arose' or 'there was much said.'
segments.s653-g686: دل مردم is a possessive/genitival phrase 'the hearts of the people'. خفته is a past participle/attribute 'having slept' or 'asleep', and بیدار گشت is 'became awake' (بیدار = awake; گشت = became). The verb expresses a change of state.
segments.s653-g687: جهان is the noun 'the world' and زنده‌باد is an optative/exclamation 'long live!'. به + نوشین‌روان links the wish to someone described by the epithet نوشین‌روان ('sweet‑souled' or 'pleasant‑minded'), i.e. 'Long live the world for the sweet‑souled one.'
segments.s653-g688: همیشه is an adverb 'always/ever'. جهاندار ('world‑holder' = sovereign/ruler) and دولت جوان ('young/renewed prosperity') form a parallel wish: the line implicitly prays that the ruler and the fresh prosperity endure forever. The copula/verb is omitted (elliptical optative).
segments.s653-g297: میان + noun phrase forms a prepositional phrase meaning 'among' or 'amidst'. Here 'میان مهان' = 'among the guests'; the following 'بخت بوزرجمهر' is a poetic noun phrase without an overt ezāfe (the linking -e), reading as an epithet: 'بختِ بوزرجمهر' → 'Bozorgmehr of fortune' or 'the fortunate Bozorgmehr.' Word order is topical (prepositional phrase first) for emphasis.
segments.s653-g298: چو introduces a temporal/simile clause 'when/as', followed by the noun phrase 'خورشید تابنده' ('the sun, shining') which functions as the subject, and the past verb شد ('became')—so literally 'when the sun became shining.' 'بر سپهر' is a prepositional adverbial 'upon the sky/heavens'. The whole clause describes the moment/appearance being compared to the previous nominal phrase.
segments.s653-g299: ز پیش (poetic short form of از پیش) = 'from before' or 'from the presence of'; here followed by شهنشاه 'the shah/king'. برخاستند is the past, 3rd person plural 'they rose/they stood up', so the clause means 'they rose from the king's presence.'
segments.s653-g300: برو here is a contracted/poetic sequence corresponding to بر او ('upon him') rather than the literal verb 'go' — it functions as a prepositional particle. آفرینی is a noun meaning an exclamation/praise ('afarīn' → 'praise/bravo') and نو آراستند is a past transitive: آراستند 'they adorned/arrayed' with modifier نو 'new'. Together: 'upon him they set/newly adorned (him) with praise.'
segments.s653-g301: بپرسش گرفتند is a light‑verb construction: the verbal noun بپرسش (“questioning”) plus گرفتند (3rd‑pl. past of گرفتن) = “they began/held a questioning” or “they questioned.” زو is the poetic contraction of از او (“from him”). آنچ گفت = آن‌چه گفت “that which he said” (آنچ = آن‌چه).
segments.s653-g302: که introduces a reported-content clause “that …”. مغز و دلش lists two faculties (مغز “brain/intellect” and دلش “his heart”), باخرد is an adjective meaning “wise/sensible,” بود is past “was,” and جفت here means “a pair / matched together” — i.e. his intellect and heart were both characterized by wisdom.
segments.s653-g303: زبان تیز is a noun + adjective phrase “sharp tongue / sharp speech.” بگشاد is a past form of گشودن (“opened/spoke”) placed before the subject (poetic verb–subject order): بگشاد (he opened/spoke) + مرد جوان (the young man) as subject. The phrase therefore means the young man spoke with a sharp tongue.
segments.s653-g304: که introduces a descriptive relative clause “who/for he was …”. پاکیزه‌دل is a compound adjective meaning “pure‑hearted,” بود is past “was,” و links the second adjective روشن‌روان, a poetic compound meaning “bright‑souled / cheerful.” Together they describe the young man’s inner qualities.
segments.s653-g361: دگرگفت is a fused poetic phrase meaning “another said / replied” (دگر + گفت). کز introduces a subordinate/relative clause meaning “that/of which”; ما is the clause's referent “we/us.” چه نیکوترست is a comparative clause: چه (which/what) + نیکوتر (comparative “better”) + -ست (copula) => “which of us is better.” Word order is typical Classical Persian: reporting verb + ‘that’-clause with the complement inside.
segments.s653-g362: ز is a poetic preposition here meaning “of/from”; گیتی = “the world.” کرانیکویی (literally ‘of the brim/edge’) qualifies گیتی, giving “a corner/edge of the world.” درخورست is the predicative adjective درخور + -ست, meaning “is fitting/appropriate.” The clause therefore states the result: that the best among us is fitting for a world’s corner.
segments.s653-g363: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed narrative phrase “gave this answer” (چنین = thus, داد = gave, پاسخ = answer). که introduces reported content (“that …”). آهستگی is a nominal abstract (آهست + -گی) here meaning “gentleness / modesty / slowness” and begins an enumerated list of virtues.
segments.s653-g364: This segment is a simple coordinate list of abstract nouns joined by و: کریمی (generosity), وخوبی (goodness), وشایستگی (worthiness). In Classical Persian, adjectival or active-stem words can function as abstract nouns in such lists; the final meaning is the enumeration of desirable qualities mentioned in the preceding clause.
segments.s653-g365: فزونتر is a comparative/adverbial form “more / still more.” بکردن is the infinitive/causative ‘to make / to do,’ taking the following phrase as its object. سرخویش here should be read as سرِ خویش (“his own head”) and پست is the adjective “low / bowed,” so the whole phrase means “to make his head lower” (i.e., to humble himself more). Note the poetic compression of سرِ خویش into one token.
segments.s653-g366: ببخشد is a third-person verbal form here meaning “he gives / bestows” (بخشیدن used as give). نه ... از بهر is the negation pattern “not for the sake of …”; پاداش = “reward” and دست here is literal “hand” but idiomatically part of پاداشِ دست meaning material recompense/payment. Altogether: he gives without seeking reward.
segments.s653-g367: A string of verbs expresses habitual/actional behavior: بکوشد (he strives/attempts), بجوید (he seeks), بگرد (goes about/wanders) followed by جهان (the object/space ‘the world’). These verbal forms (ب‑ prefix + present-stem) are typical in Classical Persian to list repeated or characteristic actions.
segments.s653-g368: خرامد is a verbal form meaning “he strides/proudly walks” (خرامیدن); به هنگام = “at the time / at the proper moment”; با + همرهان = “with his companions” (همرهان is the plural of همره). The clause describes how and with whom he moves at the appointed time.
segments.s653-g521: چنین ('thus') is a fronted adverbial giving the manner of speech. گفت is the past tense verb 'said' (here used to report speech) and takes the indirect object marked by به 'to', so کسری به بوزرجمهر = 'Khosrow to Bozorgmehr' (poetic word order: manner — verb — subject — indirect object).
segments.s653-g522: که introduces the content of the reported speech ('that' / introducing the clause). از + noun (از چادر) = 'from/out of the veil', شرم is 'modesty/shame' specifying the veil, and چهر is the direct object 'face'. بگشای is an older/poetic present/imperative form (بـ prefix + گشای) meaning 'open/uncover' or 'take off' — here an imperative/command within the reported clause: 'Uncover the face from the veil of modesty.'
segments.s653-g523: سخن‌گو دانا is a noun phrase: سخن (speech) + گوی (here forming 'speaker') modified by دانا ('wise'). زبان برگشاد is a poetic expression 'opened (his) tongue' — زبان (tongue/speech) followed by برگشاد (past form 'opened up') where برگشاد functions as the verbal predicate describing what the wise speaker did.
segments.s653-g524: ز هرگونه دانش is a prepositional phrase 'from every kind of knowledge' (ز = from, هرگونه = every kind, دانش = knowledge). همی‌کرد یاد uses the archaic continuous marker همی plus کرد (past/do) with یاد (remembrance/mention) — a periphrastic construction meaning 'he made mention / recalled' (i.e. he spoke of all kinds of knowledge).
segments.s653-g525: نخست is an adverb 'first'. آفرین کرد is a verbal phrase 'made praise / praised' (آفرین as noun/exclamation 'praise' with کرد 'did/made'), and بر marks the beneficiary/target ('upon the king') so بر شهریار = 'upon the sovereign/king'. This is a common poetic formula 'آفرین کردن بر X' = 'to praise X'.
segments.s653-g526: که introduces the following quoted wish. پیروز بادا is an optative formula: پیروز 'victorious' + بادا (classical optative 'may it be') = 'may [he/it] be victorious'. سر تاجدار 'crowned head' is the subject/target of the wish (literally 'head (of) the crowned one' = 'the crowned ruler'). The word order follows older poetic inversions (adjective + optative + nominal subject).
segments.s653-g527: دگر ('again' / 'other') + گفت ('said' — reporting speech; sense not supplied) introduces the next remark. مردم نگردد بلند is an inverted clause: مردم (the people) is the subject, نگردد is the negative present/subjunctive of گشتن/گردیدن 'become' ('will not become' / 'do not become'), and بلند ('lofty/exalted') is the predicate adjective — 'the people will not be exalted.'
segments.s653-g528: مگر introduces an exception/conditional 'unless'. سر بپیچد uses the present subjunctive b-prefix form بپیچد of پیچیدن 'to turn' — 'the head turns' (here 'turn aside/away'). ز راه گزند is a prepositional phrase 'from the way/path of harm' (ز = from, راه = way, گزند = harm). Together: 'unless the head turns away from the path of harm.'
segments.s653-g849: و is the simple coordinating conjunction 'and' linking this clause to the previous line. رایدون is a proper name (here used as the subject). که introduces a relative clause ('who') and حاکم بود is the relative clause 'was ruler' with بود as the past copula. تیزمغز is an adjectival epithet 'sharp‑minded/sharp‑witted' applied to Rāydūn; in poetic Persian such epithets often follow the noun and are understood as predicate/adjectival apposition to the name.
segments.s653-g850: نیاید is the negative present/impersonal form 'does not come / does not arise' (verb first as often in poetry). ز introduces the source 'from', گفتار او is the genitive phrase 'his speech', and کار نغز is the object 'a good/commendable deed/result' with نغز postpositive as an adjective modifying کار. The clause states that no good/result issues from his words.
segments.s653-g851: دگر is the determiner 'other/another' modifying کارزاری (a martial/war‑related person or one of the battlefield). که begins a relative clause 'who/that', and هنگام جنگ is a temporal prepositional phrase 'at the time of war/battle' that sets the scene for the following verbs.
segments.s653-g852: بترسد and نترسد are present (poetic/imperfect) forms of ترسیدن with and without negation: 'he fears' ... 'he does not fear'. ز marks the object/source of the fear ('for/from'), so ز جان = 'for his life' and ز ننگ = 'for shame/disgrace'. The correlative 'و' links the two contrasting attitudes (fearing life but not fearing shame).
segments.s653-g853: توانگر is the head noun 'wealthy person'. که باشد is the relative clause marker plus copula 'who is' (باشَد here indicates existence/quality). دلش تنگ is a fixed expression 'his heart is narrow' meaning stingy/tight‑fisted; و links the next adjective زفت, which here is metaphorical 'foul/nasty' (describing appetite or character).
segments.s653-g854: شکم زمین = 'the belly of the earth' (a possessive/compound), Лучше بهتَر here is the comparative/adverb 'better' used idiomatically to express what would be preferable, او را is the definite object 'him', and نهفت (poetic past/participial form of نهفتن) 'hid/covered'. Together this clause says it would be better if the earth's belly concealed him (i.e., that he be buried/hidden by the earth).
segments.s653-g855: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. بر here means 'to/for' (on/upon), so بر مرد درویش = 'to/for a poor man'. کنداوری in context functions as the noun 'an act of giving/generosity' (though it can also appear as a proper name in other contexts); the whole phrase reads 'when (one) bestows generosity upon a poor man'.
segments.s653-g856: نه ... نه is the correlative negative 'neither ... nor ...'. کهتر means 'lesser/inferior', زیبنده is 'befitting/appropriate', and مهتری refers to 'preeminence/leadership' (one's rank as chief). The clause states that by such generosity one is neither made lesser nor rendered unfit for eminence.
segments.s653-g257: چو here is the conditional 'if' introducing the clause. دانا functions as a substantive 'a wise one' and تو را uses the postposed object/topic marker را to mark 'you' as the one affected ('an enemy to you'). دشمن جان is a possessive/compound sense 'enemy of (your) life/soul', and بود is the past copula 'was/were' completing the conditional clause.
segments.s653-g258: به از is a fixed comparative construction meaning 'better than' (به + از). دوست مردی که نادان بود is a noun phrase with a relative clause: 'a friend (دوست), a man (مردی) who was ignorant (که نادان بود)'; بود is the past copula for that relative clause.
segments.s653-g259: The verb-predicate 'توانگر شد' ('became wealthy') precedes the noun phrase 'آنکس که خشنود گشت' ('the one who became content') — a common poetic word order where the relative phrase identifies the person. شد and گشت are both past copulas/verbs meaning 'became'.
segments.s653-g260: بدو is a prepositional/benefactive 'to him/for him'. آز و تیمار او is a coordinated possessive phrase 'his greed and (his) care', with the pronoun او after تیمار marking possession. سود گشت is 'became benefit/proved profitable', where گشت is past 'became'. The whole clause means these qualities proved advantageous to him.
segments.s653-g261: بآموختن‌گر is the conditional phrase 'if by learning' (the -گر here functions like 'if/when' attached to the verbal noun). فروتر شوی means 'you become lower/more humble'; شوی is 2nd-person present/subjunctive 'you become' used in conditional/advisory contexts.
segments.s653-g262: سخن را is the definite object 'the words/speech' (را marks the object). ز دانندگان is a source phrase 'from the knowledgeable/experts' (z = 'from'), and بشنوی is the 2nd-person subjunctive/imperative 'hear/listen' — an exhortation tied to the preceding conditional.
segments.s653-g263: به گفتار is a causal/agentive prepositional phrase 'by/at words' (here indicating cause or instrument). گرخیره شد is a compound adjectival idea 'became dazed/stunned' with شد as the past copula, and رای مرد is a possessive ordering 'the man's counsel/opinion' (رای before its possessor مرد because of poetic word order).
segments.s653-g264: نگردد here is a negative-form verb 'will not become' (used with an impersonal sense). کسی in this negative context reads 'no one'. خیره modifies همتای مرد ('the equal/peer of the man'), so the clause says that 'no one becomes an equal to the man's dazed/stunned state' (i.e. nobody matches his stupor).
segments.s653-g529: چو here introduces a temporal/conditional clause 'when/if'. باید is used impersonally ('it is necessary' / 'one must') and is followed by که introducing the subordinate clause 'that knowledge increase you'. بیفزایدت is the present/subjunctive-form verb 'to increase/add' with the enclitic ـت marking 'you' as the object ('increase you'). Word order is clause-initial (چو + باید + که + subject + verb+object-enclitic).
segments.s653-g530: سخن یافتن is a verbal-noun phrase 'finding speech' (i.e. finding words or expressing oneself). را is the object marker attaching to that verbal noun. خرد بایدت places the predicate 'wisdom is necessary for you' after the object: خرد 'wisdom' + بایدت (باید with enclitic ـت) = 'you must have wisdom' (inverted, typical of Persian poetic word order).
segments.s653-g531: در نام جستن is a prepositional + verbal-noun phrase 'in seeking a name/reputation'. دلیری بود is a simple past clause 'there was daring/boldness' (بود = 'was'). The word order places the circumstance first ('in seeking fame') and then states the quality present there ('daring existed').
segments.s653-g532: زمانه is the subject 'the age/times'. ز here functions causally 'because of / on account of'. بد دل 'bad-hearted' (بد modifying دل). به سیری بود = 'was toward/into satiety' (به + noun expresses tendency or state; بود = 'was'). The clause says the times, owing to ill-hearted people, inclined to satiation/avarice.
segments.s653-g533: وگر = 'if' (introducing the conditional). تخت جویی is a compound nominal phrase 'seeking the throne' (تخت 'throne' + جویی verbal-noun 'seeking'). هنر بایدت places the predicate after the object: 'skill is necessary for you' (بایدت = باید + enclitic ـت 'for you'). The clause states the requirement for pursuing a throne.
segments.s653-g534: چو سبزی (here fused as چوسبزی) = 'as/like greenness' (a simile/metaphor). بود is the past copula 'was' linking the simile; شاخ و بر is a fixed-collocation meaning 'boughs and branches' (literal 'branches and...'), and بایدت again 'is necessary for you'. The second clause uses the same predicate structure to state another necessary condition under the simile.
segments.s653-g535: چوپرسند is a fused temporal clause 'when they ask' (چو + پرسند). پرسندگان is the explicit agent 'the questioners'. از هنر = 'about/regarding art/skill' (از + noun = concerning). This segment sets the situation: 'when the questioners ask about skill'.
segments.s653-g536: نشاید is an impersonal negative 'it is not fitting/it should not be'. که introduces the subordinate clause 'that ...'. پاسخ دهیم = 'that we give an answer' (دهیم = present/subjunctive 'we give'); از گهر literally 'from/out of a gem' or 'with a gem' (poetic phrasing). The whole means it would be improper to answer with jewels (i.e. to reply in that manner).
segments.s653-g825: همه is the subject 'everyone'; راست here functions adverbially 'truly/straightforwardly' modifying گوید. گوید is classical 3rd-person singular present (habitual) 'says/utters' (used as a general truth). سخن is the object/topic 'words/speech' and کم وبیش ('few and more') is a parenthetical phrase meaning 'few or many' / 'more or less' modifying سخن; the poetic word order places سخن before the verb and appends کم وبیش for emphasis.
segments.s653-g826: نگردد is negated present/optative 3rd-person singular formed with ن‑ + گردد (here meaning 'does not turn/change, does not become different'). بهر کار is a prepositional phrase 'for/regarding an affair' (بهر = 'for, toward' and کار = 'affair/task'). ز آیین خویش ('from his custom/practice') gives the source/standard he will not depart from; خوی + enclitic ش = 'his own way'. The clause means 'he does not deviate from his customary practice in his affairs.'
segments.s653-g827: ششم ('the sixth') introduces the numbered point. بر + پرستنده = 'toward the worshipper' (بر used as 'toward/on'), and تخت خویش = 'his throne' (تخت 'throne' + خوی + ش = 'his own'). The phrase sets up the object/person toward whom the feeling is directed.
segments.s653-g828: چنان مهر دارد is literal 'he has such affection' (چنان = 'so/such', مهر = 'love/affection', دارد = 3rd-sg present 'has'). که introduces the comparison/extent ('that/as') and the clause بر بخت خویش ('toward his fortune') repeats the prepositional phrase, with the verb understood from the previous clause (ellipsis common in Persian poetry). So: 'he has such affection for the throne's worshipper as he has for his fortune.'
segments.s653-g829: به هفتم 'for the seventh' marks the next numbered maxim. سخن here frames the domain 'in speech' (topic). هرک (هر که) = 'whoever/whosoever' is the subject, دانا بود is the copular phrase 'was/is wise/learned' (بود is past copula but used generically here). Word order is topical/poetic: 'in matters of speech, whoever was learned.'
segments.s653-g830: زبانش 'his tongue' (زبان + enclitic ش) is the topic/possessed organ. بگفتن is the infinitive 'to speak/saying' used as the verbal complement, and توانا بود 'was able/capable' is the predicate: together they form 'his tongue was able to speak' (i.e., he was eloquent). Infinitive + توانا بود is a common structure for expressing capability.
segments.s653-g831: نگردد = ن‑ + گردد 'does not become/turn' (negative 3rd-sg). دلش 'his heart' (دل + enclitic ش) is the subject/topic and سیر 'sated/full' is the predicative adjective; ز آموختن 'from/with learning' gives the cause/source. The clause means 'his heart does not grow satisfied from learning' (i.e., he never tires of learning).
segments.s653-g832: از اندیشگان 'from/of the thinkers/the thoughtful' (از = 'from/because of', اندیشگان = 'the thoughtful/thinkers'). مغز را 'the brain' with را as definite/object marker, and سوختن is the infinitive 'to burn' used in a predicative sense: poetry often places the verb-final infinitive to mean simple present/persistent action. The clause conveys that the brain (i.e. the intellect) is 'burned/consumed' by (pondering) the wise — a figurative way to say his mind is consumed by thought.
segments.s653-g1065: ز + گیتی here marks the domain/scene (“in/from the world”); چو is the temporal conjunction “when”; آگه شوند is a third-person plural verb phrase meaning “become aware/known” (شوند = become); این مهان is the postposed subject “these guests/visitors.” Word order is poetic: temporal clause (ز گیتی چو) + verb (آگه شوند) + subject (این مهان).
segments.s653-g1066: شنیده is a past participle/adverbial participle here meaning “being heard” or “it is heard;” بگویند is a third-person plural present/subjunctive-like form meaning “they say” (used for reported speech); با همرهان is a prepositional phrase “with/among their companions.” Together the clause yields the idiom “they are heard to say (with their companions).”
segments.s653-g1067: چنین گفت is the formula “thus said/so spoke.” با شاه marks the addressee (literally “with/to the king” in older usage); بیدار مرد is the agent “the awakened/alert man” (adjective bīdār modifying مرد). Persian allows this order: formula + verb + prepositional addressee + agent.
segments.s653-g1068: که‌ای here functions as the vocative/relative introduction to the direct address (“O you who…”), with که acting like “who/that” plus the vocative ای. برتر از is the comparative “higher/superior than,” where از introduces the standard of comparison; گنبد لاژورد is a noun phrase “the lapis-lazuli dome” (noun + modifier).
segments.s653-g1069: پرستیدن is the infinitive/verbal noun “to worship” used as the verb phrase; شهریار زمین is a genitive compound “sovereign/king of the earth” (شهریار = ruler, زمین = earth/land). Word order is verb (infinitive) + object phrase.
segments.s653-g1070: نجوید is the verb with the negative prefix ن- meaning “does not seek/does not desire” (third-person singular); خردمند is the subject “the wise man”; جز راه دین is the exception phrase “except the way of religion.” The clause literally reads “the wise man seeks not, except the path of religion” (i.e., a wise man seeks nothing but religion).
segments.s653-g1071: نباید is an impersonal modal “it must not / there should not” followed by the prepositional phrase به فرمان شاهان (“with / regarding the commands of kings”); درنگ is a verbal noun “delay/hesitation.” Together the line reads “there should be no delay concerning the kings’ commands.”
segments.s653-g1072: Again نباید is the impersonal “it should not be,” که introduces the subordinate clause (“that…”), باشد is the present/subjunctive third-person “be,” and دل شاه تنگ is the predicate “the king’s heart [be] narrow.” The structure states the desirability: “It should not be that the king’s heart be narrow.”
segments.s653-g249: ز دانش is a prepositional phrase meaning "from/of knowledge" and functions adverbially. چوجان here is an archaic/poetic form (graphically fused in the manuscript) and in this line functions as part of the condition; the clause خویش/تو را مایه نیست is the main predication: تو را مایه نیست = "there is no mâyé/resource for you." نیست is the negative copula 'does not exist'.
segments.s653-g250: به از is an idiomatic comparative meaning "better than/there is nothing better than"; here the sequence reads literally as "there is no (هیچ) پیرایه (ornament) better than (به از) خامشی (silence)." هیچ negates and پیرایه نیست is the predicate 'there is no ornament.'
segments.s653-g251: چو introduces a conditional/temporal clause "when/if." بردانش is an archaic/poetic contraction (written together here) corresponding to بر دانش "toward/on learning"; خوی + ش = خویش = "one's own" (possessive), and مهرآوری is a verbal noun meaning "showing affection/cherishing." The whole segment = "When/if you cherish your own learning..."
segments.s653-g252: خرد را uses را to topicalize/emphasize "wisdom." ز تو is an ablative/agentive phrase "because of/from you" (i.e., by your doing), and بگسلد is a third-person singular verb here meaning "break/tear apart." داوری is the subject that performs the action ("judgment"), so the clause reads literally: "judgment will tear/break wisdom from you."
segments.s653-g253: توانگر بود is the predicate "was/is wealthy." هر کرا is an archaic relative/indefinite "whoever/anyone who," and آز نیست is the negative predicate "is not greedy." Word order is inverted (predicate before relative) as often in poetic diction: "Wealthy is whoever is not greedy."
segments.s653-g254: This line is compact and archaic: خنک functions as an adjective/noun meaning "cool/peaceful (repose)." بنده‌کش is a compound (بنده + کش) meaning "one who kills/masters servants" (i.e., a cruel or oppressive person). آز انباز is an old compound reading as "greed's store/hoard" (انباز is an archaic/uncertain word) and نیست negates the whole. The clause says that the cruel, greedy hoarder has no cool/peaceful repose.
segments.s653-g255: مدارا is the subject "forbearance/tolerance." خرد را uses را to mark "wisdom" as the thing related to مدارا ("forbearance was brother to wisdom"). برادر بود is the predicate "was a brother (to)." The copula بود is used poetically as a timeless statement.
segments.s653-g256: خرد is the subject. بر سر جان is a prepositional phrase literally "on the head of the soul/life" (i.e., protecting one's life), چو is the comparative "as/like," افسر is "helmet," and بود is the copula "was." The image: "wisdom upon the soul's head was like a helmet."
segments.s653-g1169: چو is a temporal conjunction here meaning “when.” با + زه is an instrumental/adjunct phrase “with/using the za (زه)” (here زه is treated as a concrete/onomatopoeic or instrument noun). بگفتی is 2nd‑person past “you said.” زهازه is a compound with ز‑ meaning “from/therefrom” (hence “from it”), and بهم (lit. “to one another / together”) is used adverbially “together.” The whole segment sets a time/setting: “When you, with the za, said (it) from it together….”
segments.s653-g1170: Numeral + noun precedes the copula here: چهل بدره (literally “forty badreh/full‑moons”). بودی is the past copula “there was/were” (past). ز marks source “from the treasure” (ز گنجش) and درم names the unit “dirham(s).” The clause says that forty units (badré) existed in dirhams drawn from the treasure.
segments.s653-g1171: چو again = “when.” گنجور functions as a noun/adjective meaning the treasury/treasure‑house. باشاه is an adjunct meaning “with the king / in the king’s presence” (here a circumstantial phrase). کردی is 2nd‑person past “you made/did,” and شمار is the object “count/number.” Together: “When you made/numbered the treasury, O king….”
segments.s653-g1172: به introduces the beneficiary/measure “for/for each.” هربدره means “each badré (each full‑moon/each unit called badré).” بودی is again the past copula “there was/there were.” درم ده هزار places the amount after the copula: “ten thousand dirhams.” The whole line: “for each badré there were ten thousand dirhams.”
segments.s653-g1173: شهنشاه is the subject “the Shahanshah / emperor.” با زه is an accompanying phrase “with/using the za (زه).” زهازه here functions adverbially/locatively “from it / therefrom.” بگفت is the 3rd‑person past “he said.” Word order is subject + adjuncts + verb, a common classical Persian pattern.
segments.s653-g1174: که introduces a subordinate clause “that.” گفتار او is the noun phrase “his speech / his words.” با + درم means “with/by dirhams,” بود is past copula “was,” and جفت is predicate “paired / matched / a pair.” So the clause states that his words were matched or accompanied by dirhams (i.e. his speech came with money).
segments.s653-g1175: بیاورد is the 3rd‑person past “he brought/produced.” گنجور is used adjectivally or as a compound “treasure‑laden / of the treasury.” خورشید چهر is a two‑word epithet “sun‑faced / having a sunlike face” (a poetic epithet meaning brilliant). Together this is a descriptive noun phrase attached to the verb: “he brought (forth) the treasure‑laden, sun‑faced (riches).”
segments.s653-g1176: درم بدره‌ها lists the items presented: درم (dirhams / coins) and بدره‌ها (the badrés / “full‑moons” — here poetic items/units). پیش + بوزرجمهر means “before / in the presence of Bozorgmehr.” So the phrase says he set/placed dirhams and badrés before Bozorgmehr.
segments.s653-g425: This is a verbless noun phrase functioning as the comparative clause: کدامست (which is) + خوشتر (more pleasant). مرا is the object/experiencer 'to me' (here split orthographically into parts), and روزگار is the topic/subject 'life' or 'the world/fortune' that is judged. Word order places the comparative adjective before the thing being compared and the experiencer after it for emphasis.
segments.s653-g426: ازین introduces the standard of comparison 'than this' (از + این). برشده is a participial adjective 'fallen / separated / cut off' modifying چرخ, and ناپایدار is another adjective 'unstable / transient' further describing چرخ. Together they form 'this fallen, unstable wheel (of fate)'.
segments.s653-g427: سخن گوی (literally 'speech-sayer') is the compound subject 'the speaker'. پاسخ چنین داد is a common formula: پاسخ (an answer) + چنین (thus) + داد (gave) = 'gave such an answer / replied thus'. باز after the verb here means 'again' (he replied again).
segments.s653-g428: که introduces the reported content ('that'). هرکس که گشت is a relative/conditional clause 'whoever became' with گشت as the past-tense verb 'became/turned out'. ایمن و بی‌نیاز are coordinated predicates: 'safe and without need / self-sufficient', describing the state that 'هرکس' attains.
segments.s653-g429: به خوبی زمانه is a noun phrase 'the good(ies) of the age' (the world's bounty/fortune). ورا is the clitic 'to him'. داد is the past verb 'gave' (here poetic repetition داد داد emphasizes the giving or follows a poetic pattern), so the whole means 'time/the age gave him its good.'
segments.s653-g430: سزدِ گر is a conditional phrase 'if it is fitting' (سزد + گر). نگیری is negative subjunctive/imperative 'you should not take' (lit. 'do not take'). جز از = 'except from', and داد یاد = 'remembrance/from justice' — i.e. 'take remembrance only from justice'. The clause gives a moral injunction limiting whose memory/credit one should accept.
segments.s653-g431: بپرسید is a past/perfective 3rd‑person form 'he/they asked' (here used as 'asked' / 'inquired'), and دیگر means 'further / again' (i.e., 'ask again' or 'ask moreover'). که introduces the content clause 'which knowledge...'; دانش کدام inverts the normal order and means 'which knowledge'.
segments.s653-g432: به گیتی = 'about/regarding the world'. که introduces the relative/subordinate clause. باشیم is the subjunctive/present 'we may be / we are' expressing the desired state, زو (ز + و here) = 'from it / because of it' (archaic contraction of از + او), and شادکام is the adjective 'happy/fortunate'. So the clause asks which knowledge of the world makes us happy.
segments.s653-g409: بدو گفت is a common classical sequence meaning “(to) him he said”: بدو is the prepositional pronoun “to him,” followed by گفت (past sing.) “said.” شو باش is an imperative doublet: شو (imperative of شدن) “become / be” + باش (imperative of بودن) reinforcing the command “be.” از introduces the cause or domain “from / away from,” and گناه is the noun “sin.” The whole phrase is an imperative admonition: “to him he said, ‘be distant/keep away from sin.’”
segments.s653-g410: جهان را is the object with the postposition/definite marker را attached to جهان (“the world”); همه is modifying it = “the whole/entire.” چون governs a simile “like / as,” followed by تن خویش “one’s own body” (here خوی + ش form the possessive; ش is the possessive clitic). خواه is an imperative/optative of خواستن used here as a command “consider / wish / treat.” Word order is standard Persian: object marked by را, then comparative phrase introduced by چون, finishing with the imperative verb خواه.
segments.s653-g411: هران چیزکانت is an emphatic/archaic phrasing meaning “those things of yours” (هران = each/that), with the possessive suffix -ت on چیزکان indicating “your things.” نیاید پسند is an impersonal negative construction: نیاید (present/impersonal form of نیامدن) plus پسند (“liking, pleasing”) yields “it is not pleasing / does not please.” The clause states conditionally that those possessions/concerns do not please.
segments.s653-g412: تن here means “self / person,” followed by دوست و دشمن “friend and enemy” as a coordinated noun phrase. دران = در + ان, “in that / within it.” برمبند is an imperative-like verb telling one to close/conceal or keep shut within; it is archaic/poetic and the lemma provided does not match a clear modern sense, so treat it as a command “shut up / keep in.” The sequence thus instructs to keep friend and foe alike enclosed within that self/thing.
segments.s653-g413: دگرگفت is a past verb meaning “he replied / said otherwise.” کوشش is the noun “effort / striving.” ز اندازه بیش is a short phrase “beyond measure” where ز (poetic variant of از) = “from / beyond,” اندازه = “measure / degree,” and بیش = “more / excessive.” The phrase as reported speech gives the idea: “(you say) exertion beyond measure...”
segments.s653-g414: چه introduces an interrogative “what” (here: “what do you say”), followed by گویی which in context is a present-second-person verbal form “you say” (the provided lexical senses list did not include the verb sense, so treat it as the verbal “you say”). کزین = ک + زین “from this / of these,” دوکدامست is an interrogative compound “which of the two,” and پیش here means “before / foremost / superior.” The clause asks which of the two alternatives is preferable.
segments.s653-g415: چنین داد پاسخ is a formulaic clause “thus he gave an answer”: چنین = “thus,” داد = past of دادن “gave,” and پاسخ = “answer.” که introduces the content clause (“that / that in which…”). اندر خرد = “in (the) wisdom / in the intellect” with اندر = “in / within” and خرد = “wisdom / intellect.”
segments.s653-g416: جز اندیشه چیزی = “nothing except thought” (جز = except, اندیشه = thought, چیزی = anything/thing). نه اندر خورد is a negative verbal phrase: نه = not, اندر = into/in, خورد here is used in the sense “to enter / to be taken in / to fall (into)” (an intransitive use often found in classical Persian). So the clause means that nothing enters the intellect except thought.
segments.s653-g1129: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'. بنوازدت is classical third-person present form بنوازد plus the enclitic direct‑object -ت 'you' — 'he/it favours/caresses you'. شاه is the noun 'the king', the object/agent of the verb بنوازدت in context. کشی مکن is a negative imperative formed with the present stem کِشی (from کشتن / کشیدن in older usage) followed by مکن 'do not' — together giving 'do not do X' (here 'do not act violently/against').
segments.s653-g1130: اگر چه is a concessive turn equivalent to 'even if/although'. پرستنده is a noun 'worshipper, devotee'. باشی is the second‑person subjunctive/present form of بودن ('you may be' or 'you are') used here in a concessive clause. کهن is an adjective 'old/long‑standing' modifying پرستنده.
segments.s653-g1131: که introduces the explanatory clause ('for/that'). هرچند is a concessive adverb 'although/however'. گردد is the present/subjunctive form of گشتن/گردیدن here 'becomes' or 'comes to be'. پرستش is the noun 'worship/adoration' and دراز is the adjective 'long' describing پرستش.
segments.s653-g1132: چنان دان is an idiomatic imperative/verb phrase 'consider it so' — دان here means 'consider/think'. که introduces the subordinate clause 'that'. هست is the copula 'is', او the third‑person pronoun 'he' (the king), ز تو is the poetic prepositional phrase 'from you' = 'independent of you', and بی‌نیاز is the adjective 'without need / self‑sufficient'.
segments.s653-g1133: اگر begins a conditional 'if'. با تو گردد literally 'becomes with you' — i.e. 'if he becomes (towards) you', where گردد is 'becomes/happens'. ز چیزی means 'because of/since something' or 'over something' (ز = 'from/because of', چیزی = 'something'). دژم is an adjective 'sullen, grim', describing his mood.
segments.s653-g1134: به پوزش گرای is an instruction 'turn toward apology' — به 'to/toward', پوزش 'apology/excuse', گرای here functions as an imperative/verb‑like form 'turn (yourself) toward' (root meaning 'incline/turn'). و مزن هیچ دم links with 'and do not strike at any moment': مزن is the prohibitive imperative 'do not strike' and هیچ دم is a time phrase 'not for a moment' (هیچ = 'never/none', دم = 'moment').
segments.s653-g1135: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. پرورد here is used as a noun 'nurturer/one who fosters' (classical/paratactic usage). دیگری را is the object 'another (person)' with را the definite/object marker. همان means 'the same' — pointing back to that nurturer/person.
segments.s653-g1136: پرستار باشد is 'will be a worshipper' where باشد is the subjunctive/optative form of بودن used in reported/predictive sense. چو تو 'like you' (چو = as/like), and بی‌گمان is an adverb 'undoubtedly/for certain' closing the statement emphatically.
segments.s653-g721: Two coordinated noun phrases describing the same person: زبان (literally "tongue"/"speech") is modified by the compound attribute راست گوی (literally "true-speaking" = truthful, i.e. someone who speaks the truth). The conjunction و links this to دل آزرم‌جوی, where دل "heart" is modified by آزرم‌جوی ("honor-seeking"). The whole phrase names a person by these qualities.
segments.s653-g722: A short predicative clause: همیشه "always" sets the temporal frame; جهان را uses the object marker را after جهان "the world"; بدو is the dative "to him/for him"; آبروی (standing last) is the noun 'reputation/honor' understood as the thing that the world gives — i.e. "the world's honour is (to) him." Word order is typical Persian: temporal adverb – object (marked) – dative pronoun – predicate noun.
segments.s653-g723: هران کس که باشد is an archaic compound meaning "whoever/anyone who (may be)"; کس "person" plus the relative particle که and the verb باشد (here a subjunctive/present copula) introduce the clause. ورا is the dative "to him/his" and رای‌زن is the noun "counsellor/adviser" — together: "whoever is his counsellor."
segments.s653-g724: سبک باشد is a predication meaning "should be light" — سبک here used metaphorically (light, not heavy or overbearing; modest/unassuming). باشد again has an optative/subjunctive flavor (advisory). اندر دل انجمن is a locative phrase: اندر "in/within", دل "the heart" (figurative centre), انجمن "assembly/council" — "in the heart of the assembly/council."
segments.s653-g725: A string of coordinated adjectival/epithet phrases describing the same person: سخن گوی "speech‑speaker" = eloquent, روشن دل "bright/clear‑hearted" = open/clear of heart, و دادده literally "and giver/one who gives" (here: generous or dispenser of justice). These epithets are linked by و and form a descriptive catalogue of virtues.
segments.s653-g726: کهان را (the world, marked by را) is the definite object placed before the verb; دارد is the present verb 'has/holds/possesses.' بکه is an archaic/obscure item here acting adverbially or as an intensifier in the clause (its exact nuance is rare), so the clause as a whole reads 'he possesses/holds the world.' The following و مه به مه is a repeated adverbial phrase meaning 'and month by month' (مه = moon/month), i.e. continuously or from month to month.
segments.s653-g727: کسی کو (an archaic "کَسِی کَو/کُو") functions as 'any person who'; بود is the copula 'is/was'; شاه را زیر دست literally 'the king (marked by را) under (زیر) hand (دست)' is an idiom meaning 'subject to the king' or 'under the king's authority/control.' The whole phrase: 'anyone who is under the king's authority.'
segments.s653-g728: نباید introduces an impersonal prohibition 'it must not / one must not'; که links the subordinate verb clause; یابد is the present/subjunctive of 'to find/attain' and here means 'to encounter' or 'to suffer'; به جائی is 'in/at any place' (به + جائی), and شکست is 'defeat/failure.' Together: 'he should not (be allowed to) find defeat anywhere.'
segments.s653-g873: سپهبد (spahbed) is the head noun; که begins a relative clause ('who/that') and باشد is the classical copula in subordinate clauses (present/subjunctive-like). نگهبان گنج is the predicate nominal 'guardian of the treasure' with the noun گنج as its object; word order (noun — relative clause — predicate) is a poetic inversion where the relative clause follows the head noun.
segments.s653-g874: سپاهی is another head noun ('soldier/warrior') modified by the relative clause introduced by که. او is the third-person pronoun; سر is the physical 'head'; بپیچد (present/subjunctive/optative form) literally 'turns/twists' and here means 'turns away'; ز is the poetic preposition 'from' and رنج is 'suffering/hardship'. The verb appears at the end, as is normal in classical Persian clauses.
segments.s653-g875: دگر دانشومند = 'another scholar/learned man' (دگر = another). کو is an archaic/classical particle here functioning like a relative 'who' (introducing the clause). از بزه = 'from/of blame, fault, or penalty' — the preposition از links the scholar to what he supposedly does not fear.
segments.s653-g876: نترسد is the negative present ('does not fear'); چو introduces a temporal/conditional subordinate clause ('when/if'); چیزی is the indefinite 'something'; بود is the classical copula ('is/was' used here as simple copula); بامزه is an adjective 'amusing/pleasant/delightful'. The subordinate clause gives the circumstance in which the scholar shows no fear.
segments.s653-g877: پزشکی (here used for a medical man/physician) is the head noun; که introduces the relative clause and باشد is the copula. به تن is a prepositional phrase 'in the body' and دردمند is an adjective 'suffering/in pain', so the clause describes the physician as bodily afflicted.
segments.s653-g878: ز بیمار = 'from the patient' or 'with respect to the sick'; چون here functions as an interrogative 'how' (introducing a rhetorical question). باز دارد گزند is a verbal phrase where باز (part of the compound باز داشتن) contributes the meaning 'to keep back/ward off' and دارد is the present tense auxiliary/lexical verb 'he has/keeps'; گزند means 'harm' — together, 'how can he keep harm away?'.
segments.s653-g879: چو is the comparative/simile 'like/as'; درویش مردم is a noun phrase 'a poor/dervish man of the people' (درویش can mean poor person or ascetic); که introduces the relative clause; نازد is a present verb meaning 'is proud'/'boasts' (classical نازیدن); به چیز = 'about/of a thing' — the phrase describes someone proud of some trivial thing.
segments.s653-g880: که again introduces a subordinate clause; آن چیز is 'that thing'; گفتن is the infinitive/gerund 'saying' (here nominalized: 'the saying of that thing' or 'mentioning it'); نیرزد is the impersonal/present negative 'is not worth' or 'does not merit'; به نیز (نیز as emphatic 'even/also') reinforces that it isn't even worth being said — so the clause means 'which is not even worth saying.'
segments.s653-g345: This noun phrase is the thing being questioned by the following clause. که here introduces the phrase (acting like “what”/“that which”), followed by two coordinated nouns انجام و فرجام (“result/outcome and end”) and the postpositive modifier چونین سخن (“such words/speech”); Persian allows such modifiers after the head noun. The whole phrase functions as the object/topic of the upcoming interrogative قِسم.
segments.s653-g346: چه گونه‌است is an interrogative clause: چه‌گونه (in what manner/how) + است (copula) asking “how is it.” The conjunction و links a second imperative/optative clause: این برچه آید بَبِّن. برچه = "upon what" (بر + چه), آید is the present/simple verb “comes/happens,” and ببن (poetic spelling of ببین) is the imperative “see!” — so: “how is it? and see upon what this falls/happens.”
segments.s653-g347: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed poetic order: چنین “thus” + داد (“gave”) + پاسخ (“answer”) — “thus gave an answer.” The conjunction/quotative که introduces reported content: جوینده مرد = “the seeker (is) a man.” Persian often omits the copula, so مرد is the predicative noun describing جوینده.
segments.s653-g348: This completes the reported clause. دَوان is a participial/preterite-root form meaning “running”/“he runs”; then وشب و روز lists time adverbials “night and day.” با کار کرد puts the means/attitude with کار (“work”) plus کرد (past of کردن) to indicate he labored or “did work.” Word order is typical Persian: verb near the end, modifiers/adjuncts preceding.
segments.s653-g349: بود introduces existence/past (“there was”). روزی here functions adverbially (“one day” / “once”) and is placed before the imperative برو (“go”), which issues the instruction; تارو تنگ are two adjectival words modifying the implied way/path: تارو “dark” and تنگ “narrow.” So the clause reads as an existential set-up plus an imperative: “There was a path; one day go into the dark, narrow [way].”
segments.s653-g350: بجوی is the imperative/optative form of جویدن/جستن “seek/search!” (prefix ب- marks the verbal form). اندرون آب او is a prepositional/genitive string “within the water of it/within its water.” با درنگ is an adverbial phrase “with delay/patience” (درنگ used adverbially). Together: “Seek within its water, with patience.”
segments.s653-g351: یکی (a certain) introduces an indefinite person, modified by the adjective بی‌هنر (unskilled) and the participle/adj خفته (“asleep”), so: “a certain unskilled one, asleep.” بر تخت بخت is a locative phrase “on the throne of fortune” (بر = on, تخت = throne, بخت = fortune/luck).
segments.s653-g352: همی is the habitual/continuous adverb “continually/always.” گل فشاند is a verbal clause “(he) scatters/throws flowers” (فشاند is 3rd-person verb). بر درخت is the directional/locative phrase “onto the tree.” The inserted برو is a poetic directional move (imperative/verb-form) indicating motion toward/upward in this context, so the whole means roughly “he keeps casting flowers up onto the tree.”
segments.s653-g809: کزو is a classical relative/adverbial 'from which / from that'. The noun phrase دین یزدان (literally 'religion of God') is a genitive-like juxtaposition where دین is the thing and یزدان the possessor. به نیرو is an instrumental/adverbial phrase 'with/into strength' (به used here as 'by/with'), and شود is the present/future/resultative verb 'become' — together: 'from which God's religion becomes strong.'
segments.s653-g810: همان is a demonstrative 'that same / the very', preceding the noun تخت شاهی 'royal throne' (تخت = throne, شاهی = royal / kingly). بی‌آهو is an adjectival/qualifying compound 'without a deer' placed before the verb for emphasis, and شود (become) closes the clause — 'that very royal throne will become deer‑less.' The subject is the noun phrase; verb comes at end as in classical Persian.
segments.s653-g811: چو (when) introduces a temporal clause; چوبشنید is an archaic fused/perfective expression 'when he heard' (past event). ز (poetic 'of/from') marks the object/source 'of it', and موبد موبدان is a titled subject/appositive 'the mobed of mobeds' (the high priest). The implied meaning: 'When the chief priest heard of it...'.
segments.s653-g812: زبان برگشاد is a compact verbal phrase where زبان 'speech / tongue' is used metonymically for 'he spoke' and برگشاد (opened) is the verb — literally 'he opened his tongue' = 'he began to speak'. از میان ردان is a prepositional phrase 'from among the ردان' (ردان = those who reject / the rejected) specifying the group to which his speech was addressed or against whom it was directed.
segments.s653-g813: چنین داد پاسخ is an older word-order: چنین (thus) + داد (gave) + پاسخ (answer) — 'he gave this reply'. The conjunction/particle که introduces the content clause; از داد شاه is a genitive construction 'from the justice of the king' (داد here is a noun 'justice' governed by از).
segments.s653-g814: درفشان شود فر places the adjective/epithet درفشان ('shining, glittering') before شود (becomes) and the noun فر ('royal glory') — a poetic inversion meaning 'the royal glory will become shining/manifest'. دیهیم و گاه are added as recipients/locations (dative/locative sense): 'over the rural people (دیهیم) and the (places/times) (گاه)'. The whole is a future/resultive assertion.
segments.s653-g815: چو (when) introduces the temporal clause. با داد is instrumental 'with/by justice' (با used to mark the means), and بگشاید is the verb 'opens/releases' (poetic subjunctive/future sense). از گنج بند is the object phrase 'the bond/lock of the treasure' (از marking the source or the object of opening) — together: 'when, by justice, he opens the treasure's lock.'
segments.s653-g816: بماند is the predicate 'will remain / endure'. پس از مرگ is a temporal phrase 'after death' (پس = after, از = of), and نامش بلند is a nominal predicate 'his name [is] high/lofty' with the possessive enclitic ش attached to نام. The clause states the result: his name remains lofty after his death.
segments.s653-g985: تو (you) is the subject pronoun placed before the conditional clause; پیروزی (victory) sets the circumstance — 'in victory' — and ار is the conditional particle 'if'. پیشدستی کنی is a subjunctive/conditional clause (کنی, present-subjunctive of کردن) meaning 'you take the initiative' (literally 'do precedence'); the verb appears at the clause end as in classical Persian.
segments.s653-g986: سرت پست گردد is a verbless result clause with گردد (become) as the main verb: 'your head becomes low' (i.e. you are humbled). چو سستی کنی is a temporal/conditional 'when/as you become lax' (چو = چو/چون 'when', سستی 'laxness/weakness', کنی subjunctive). The two parts are linked: the first gives the consequence, the second the circumstance under which it happens.
segments.s653-g987: بدانگه 'at that moment' is an adverbial compound introducing a temporal clause; که is the relativizer/’when’. اسب افگنی is a descriptive compound ('horse‑throwing' or a horse that causes throwing) and functions as the event described; هوش دار is an imperative-like formula 'have awareness' / 'be alert' (هوش 'awareness' + دار used here to mean 'possess/be in the state of').
segments.s653-g988: سلیح هم آورد literally 'when weapon(s) also come/is brought' — سلیح 'weapon', هم 'also/then' and آورد (perfect/past-form used in a temporal sense) marks the arrival of arms; را is the object/ topical marker attached to the phrase; گوش دار is an imperative admonition 'keep your ear' = 'pay attention' (گوش 'ear/attention', دار used as a directive 'have/hold').
segments.s653-g989: گرو 'band/group' is the topic; تیز گردد 'becomes keen/fierce' uses گردد 'become' as the copula verb in classical order. تو زو برمگرد is an injunction addressed to 'you' (تو) with زو (poetic 'from it') and برمگرد (imperative/verb 'turn back/withdraw'): 'you turn away from it.' Word order places the condition first and the admonition after.
segments.s653-g990: هشیوار یاران گزین is an imperative sequence: هشیوار (alert) qualifies یاران (companions), and گزین (choose) is the imperative verb 'select'. در نبرد is the adverbial phrase 'in battle' specifying the domain where you should choose such companions.
segments.s653-g991: چودانی که 'as/if you know that' is a concessive/conditional preface (چو دانی = 'since/if you know'); با او نتابی uses نتابی (negative sense of تابیدن/تابیدن 'to endure/withstand') meaning 'you cannot endure/withstand him'; مکوش is an imperative negative 'do not attempt' (مَکوش). The whole segment is a warning: if you know you cannot stand him, do not try.
segments.s653-g992: ببرگشتن از رزم is an infinitival/gerundial phrase 'withdrawal/returning from battle' (ب‑برگشتن = 'to turn back'); باز آر هوش is an admonition 'bring back your awareness' — باز آر here = 'restore/bring back' and هوش 'awareness/wits'. Grammatically it's an imperative addressed to the listener about what to do upon withdrawal.
segments.s653-g473: چنین داد پاسخ او is a verbless-order common in Persian epic: چنین (thus) + داد (gave, past) + پاسخ (answer) + او (he). Literally ‘thus gave an answer he’ — i.e. “he answered thus.” کز introduces a subordinate clause ('that' / 'from which') and here begins the content of that answer: 'کز نخست' = 'that/from the first, from the beginning.'
segments.s653-g474: درپاک یزدان functions as the object/phrase of knowledge: درپاک = 'in purity' qualifying یزدان = 'God'. بدَانست is بد + (انست) past 'he knew it' and وجست should be read as و + جست = 'and sought' (past of جستن), so the two verbs are coordinated: 'knew (him/it) and sought (him)'.
segments.s653-g475: کزویت = 'from whom / from him' governs the following nouns; سپاس و بدویت پناه lists two things coming 'from him': 'سپاس' (praise/thanks) and 'پناه' (refuge). The coordinating و links the two nouns; بدویت is an archaic/poetic word placed before پناه qualifying the kind/source of refuge and is semantically uncertain here (see senses).
segments.s653-g476: خداوند is the appositive/title identifying that 'whom' (i.e. God/the Lord). The following coordinated nouns روز و شب و هور و ماه enumerate His domains ('day and night and sun (splendor) and moon'), linked by repeated و in a simple list.
segments.s653-g477: دل خویش را is the direct object marked by the enclitic را: دل = 'heart', خوی + ش = خویش = 'his own/self', را marks it as the object of the following verb. آشکار و نهان are coordinate adjectives 'manifest and hidden' describing that heart; the order is adjective-adjective after the object, typical in poetry.
segments.s653-g478: سپردن is the infinitive 'to entrust/hand over' and functions as the main verbal noun of this clause. به introduces the indirect object 'to the command' (به فرمان), and شاه جهان is a genitive/title 'king of the world' (شاه modifying جهان). The whole phrase means 'to entrust to the command of the king of the world.'
segments.s653-g479: تن خویشتن پروریدن is an infinitival phrase: تن = 'body', خویشتن = 'oneself / his own self' (reflexive), پروریدن = 'to rear / nurture / pamper'. به ناز ('with nâz') is an adverbial phrase 'with indulgence/softness/pampering', modifying پروریدن. The structure parallels the previous infinitive clause (contrast between heart and body actions).
segments.s653-g480: برو here functions in a connective/epic sense ('and then' / 'and') linking the two infinitives (though its dictionary sense is 'go'); سخت is an adverb 'firmly/harshly', and بستن is the infinitive 'to bind/fasten'. در رنج و آز is a prepositional phrase 'in suffering and trial' qualifying how the binding is done. Grammatically the second infinitive shares the object 'تن خویشتن' from the previous segment.
segments.s653-g417: بکوشی is a second‑person singular exhortative/present form (prefix بـ + verb stem + ـی) meaning “strive” or “make an effort.” چو here means “when,” and the phrase در پیش کار is a prepositional phrase “when/while a task/work is before you”; آیدت is the verb آید plus the enclitic ت (to you) meaning “comes/befalls you.” The clause order is verb (exhortation) → temporal clause → verb phrase that ends with the object enclitic.
segments.s653-g418: چوخواهی is a conditional/optative phrase “if you wish.” که introduces the subordinate content clause. رنجی is an indefinite accusative/subject noun “a suffering/hardship,” and به بار آید is a light verbal idiom (“to be borne/come to pass”); آیدت again is the verb with the 2nd‑person enclitic, “befalls you.” The whole subordinate clause expresses the condition/result framed by چوخواهی.
segments.s653-g419: This is a condensed, inverted question: سزای ستایش = “the recompense/desert of praise.” دگر گفت (“another said”) inserts the speaker, and کیست is the interrogative copula “what/what is it?” Word order is poetic/inverted: topic (سزای ستایش) → comment (دگر گفت) → question (کیست).
segments.s653-g420: اگر is the conditional “if.” برنکوهیده is a past/passive participial adjective meaning “much censured / reviled.” باید is the impersonal modal “one must/ought to,” and گریست is the verbal base (here functioning like an infinitive or impersonal verb) meaning “to weep” — together باید گریست = “one must weep.”
segments.s653-g421: چنین گفت = “thus spoke/said.” کان کو is an archaic relative construction meaning roughly “he who” (classical/poetic usage), and به یزدان پاک is a prepositional phrase “toward/to the pure God” that specifies the object of the following clause. The relative phrase anticipates the predicate that follows.
segments.s653-g422: فزون دارد is a poetic word order (adverb/adjective before the verb) meaning “has much/abundant.” امید is “hope”; و هم links and emphasizes the next items; بیم و باک are two near‑synonymous nouns “fear and dread.” دارد is the present third‑person singular verb “he has.”
segments.s653-g423: دگر گفت “another said” introduces the speaker, then the vocative کای مرد روشن‌خرد addresses someone directly: کای is the archaic vocative “O,” followed by مرد روشن‌خرد “clear‑minded man.” The vocative follows the reporting verb in this poetic word order.
segments.s653-g424: ز گردون is a poetic prepositional phrase “from the sky/heaven (or fate).” چه is the interrogative “what,” بر سر is the locative “upon (the) head,” and همی‌بگذرد is a present/continuous verb form (همی‑ prefix + بگذرد) meaning “passes/happens.” The clause asks what the heavens are bringing upon the addressee’s head.
segments.s653-g1105: ز + بهری begins a causal/relative phrase: ز (poetic 'from'/'regarding') + بهری (بهر as a noun 'benefit/advantage'); که introduces a relative clause 'that/which'. اورا is the clitic object 'to him', and سراید is the 3rd-person singular present (literary) of سراییدن meaning 'gives/affords' here. The final ز گنج ('from the treasure') marks the source; word order is compressed for poetry so verb follows its object phrase.
segments.s653-g1106: نماند is the negative impersonal verb meaning 'there does not remain' or 'there is no longer'. که introduces the subordinate clause 'that ...', باشد is the 3rd-person present-subjunctive/present of بودن here 'that there be' or 'that should be', بدو = 'to him', and درد و رنج are coordinated nouns 'pain and suffering'. Together the segment expresses 'there remains no (thing) that there be to him pain and suffering.'
segments.s653-g1107: ز یزدان ('from/because of God') is a prepositional phrase giving divine source. بود is the copula 'is/was' and آنک ('that/which, he who') introduces the following predicate; ماند is the present/past of ماندن 'remains/endures'. سپاس ('thanks/praise') stands as the object/nominal result: roughly 'What endures is from God; thanks (are due)'.
segments.s653-g1108: کند is the 3rd-person singular present 'does/makes' (light verb); آفرین here is a noun 'acclamation/praise' (what is made). مرد یزدان‌شناس is the subject 'the God‑knowing/pious man' placed after the verb (a common poetic order). The whole segment reads 'a God‑knowing man utters praise / gives thanks.'
segments.s653-g1109: و دیگر 'and another' introduces a second kind/person. که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'; اندر دلش is the locative phrase 'in his heart' (اندر = in, دلش = his heart with enclitic), and راز شاه is the genitive 'the king's secret' (poetically compressed without explicit ezāfe). The whole phrase identifies 'another (one) who keeps the king's secret in his heart.'
segments.s653-g1110: بدارد is the 3rd-person singular present of داشتن used here as 'keeps/possesses' (he keeps the secret). نگوید is the negative present (ن‑ + گوید) meaning 'does not say/tell'; به خورشید و ماه ('to the sun and moon') is an idiomatic indirect object meaning 'to anyone' or 'to the heavens'—i.e. he does not reveal it. The verb follows its object in poetic order.
segments.s653-g1111: به فرمان شاه is a prepositional phrase 'by/at the command of the king'. آنک introduces a relative clause 'who/that', سستی is a noun meaning 'laxity/weakness', and کند is the 3rd-person singular present 'does/acts'. Together: 'by the king's command, he who shows laxity ...'. Note the poetic placement of the relative and verb.
segments.s653-g1112: همی is an adverbial emphasizer 'still/indeed/ever' placed before the clause. از تن خویش literally 'from his own body' (از = from; تن = body; خویش = his own), مستی is a noun 'intoxication/ecstasy', and کند is the light verb 'makes/becomes'. The phrase 'از تن خویش مستی کند' is idiomatic: 'becomes drunk on his own body' = 'indulges in bodily pleasures' or 'is carried away by physical intoxication.'
segments.s653-g913: چنین داد پاسخ is a verb phrase 'thus gave answer' (چنین = 'thus' before the verb, داد = past 'gave', پاسخ = 'answer'). The conjunction/quotative که introduces the reported clause. کمتر خوری places the degree adverb کمتر ('less') before the verb خوری, where خوری is the 2nd-person present/subjunctive/optative form 'you eat' used here as advice/imperative.
segments.s653-g914: تن آسان شوی is literally 'body easy become' — شوی is a 2nd-person subjunctive/optative of شدن ('become'), so the clause reads 'may your body become comfortable'. هم links the next verb as 'also'; روان پروری is a compound of روان ('soul') + پروری, where پروری functions here like a 2nd-person verbal form or hortative 'nurture/tend' (the -ی ending is the classical subjunctive/optative marker).
segments.s653-g915: ز کردار نیکی is a prepositional phrase 'from/of good conduct' (ز = poetic 'from/of'); چو introduces a conditional/temporal clause here ('if/when'). بیشی modifies کنی: بیشی = 'more' and کنی is the 2nd-person present/subjunctive of کردن ('you do'), so the whole phrase means 'if you do more (good deeds)'.
segments.s653-g916: همی is an emphatic/progressive particle often prefixed to verbs in classical Persian ('still/continually' or marking the ongoing action). برهماورد is the verb 'to contend/rival' (بر- prefix + هماورد), and پیشی کنی uses پیشی ('precedence') as an object/nominal complement plus کنی ('you do') meaning 'you gain precedence'. The verb forms remain in the 2nd-person subjunctive/optative register used for resultive/forecasting statements.
segments.s653-g917: چنین گفت is 'thus said' with گفت functioning as the past-tense speech verb introducing direct discourse. پس here functions as 'then' (sequencing), and یزدگرد دبیر places the name and title together (name + appositional title 'the scribe').
segments.s653-g918: که‌ای is an archaic/vocative construction (که ای) addressing someone: 'O ...' or 'who are you, ...'. مرد is the vocative 'man'; گوینده و یاد گیر are two coordinated epithets joined by و: گوینده 'speaker' and یاد گیر literally 'one who takes/holds memory' (i.e., 'learner' or 'one who remembers' — گیر is an agentive 'seizer/taker').
segments.s653-g919: سه آهو کدامند is an inverted question: 'Which are the three gazelles?' کدامند is the predicate 'which are (they)'. با دل به راز is a prepositional/adverbial phrase 'with heart in secret' (با = 'with', دل = 'heart'); به here is used adverbially with راز to mean 'in secret/secretly'.
segments.s653-g920: که is the relative pronoun 'who/that' introducing the relative clause. دارند is the present 'they have' and وهستند is the fused form 'and they are' (و + هستند), so the clause says they 'have (it) and are' — زان ('from/thereby' or 'because of that') links that possession to the state بی‌نیاز 'self-sufficient/without need'.
segments.s653-g609: چوخواهی is a conditional 'if you wish' (چو = اگر). که introduces a subordinate content clause 'that...'; دانسته آید is a passive/perfective verbal phrase 'be known/come to be known' with آید in the classical present/subjunctive sense. به بر is a short prepositional phrase indicating public/external view ('upon/into public sight'). The whole segment = 'if you wish that it be known publicly'.
segments.s653-g610: به گفتار uses به + گفتار to mean 'in/by speech' (instrumental sense). بگشای is an imperative 'open/begin' addressing the speaker; بند here means 'stanza' (a unit of verse) rather than a literal bond. از هنر ('of skill/art') modifies بند: 'an artful stanza'. Word order places the verb before its object (poetic inversion): 'open (begin) a stanza of art in your speech.'
segments.s653-g611: This is an inverted conditional: چوگسترد 'when/if it spreads' combined with خواهی 'you wish' yields 'if you wish it to spread'. به هر جای نام is the goal phrase 'to every place (be) your name' — هر جای = 'every place', نام here = 'reputation/fame'. Poetic word order places the goal before the verb that governs the desire.
segments.s653-g612: زبان برکشی is an idiomatic command: برکشی (second-person present/imperfective) 'draw/raise' governs زبان used figuratively as 'speech/utterance'. همچو تیغ از نیام is a simile 'like a sword from its scabbard' (از = 'from', نیام = 'sheath'). The clause = 'draw your speech forth like drawing a blade from its sheath'.
segments.s653-g613: چو here = 'when'. بامرد دانات is a compound description 'brave (بامرد) and learned/wise (دانات)' describing a person; باشد is the classical copula 'is/are' (here third-person present), and نشست is 'sits/is seated'. The segment states the temporal condition 'when a brave, learned man is seated'.
segments.s653-g614: زبردست گردد is 'becomes powerful/mighty' (گردد = 'become'). سر زیر دست is an idiom: سر = 'head' (metonymically 'person/leader'), زیر دست = 'under the hand' meaning 'subject to his control' (دست here = 'power/control'). The clause means 'the powerful (heads) come under his hand / become subject'.
segments.s653-g615: ز دانش 'from knowledge' (poetic short form ز). بود is the copula 'was/came to be' and places the action in a general/past sense; جان و دل را فروغ = 'radiance/brightness to the soul and heart' with را marking the definite object. The clause literally: 'From knowledge was (the) radiance of soul and heart.'
segments.s653-g616: نگر is an imperative 'look / see / take heed'. تا introduces a purpose/result clause here ('so that / lest'); نگردی is negative subjunctive/optative 'you do not become' (from گردیدن 'become/turn'). به گرد دروغ = 'into the circle/orbit of falsehood' (به گرد = 'into/about', دروغ = 'falsehood'). Together: 'Take heed so that you do not become caught in falsehood.'
segments.s653-g577: چونادان here introduces the condition 'when/as a wise one' (classical phrasing); عادت کند is a verbal phrase meaning 'becomes accustomed' where کند is the present-tense verbal form 'does/makes' attached to عادت (habit). هفت چیز is the object/phrase listing what he becomes accustomed to ('seven things'). Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect: temporal/conditional phrase + verb + object.
segments.s653-g578: وزان is the connective 'and/from that' linking back to the seven items; هفت چیز repeats 'seven things' for emphasis. به here introduces the state/result 'as to' or 'in regard to', and رنج‌ست is a fused predicate meaning 'is suffering/trouble' (ست = است). نیز is the adverb 'also/too', closing the clause. The whole segment says that suffering is among those seven things.
segments.s653-g579: نخست 'first' marks the first item. آنک here is a relative/introducing particle ('that/which'), followed by هرکس که دارد خرد 'whoever has wisdom' — دارد is the present-tense verb 'has/possesses' and خرد is the noun 'wisdom'. The relative clause structure is classical: آنک + هرکس که + verb + noun.
segments.s653-g580: ندارد غم is 'does not have sorrow' (ندارد = does not have, غم = sorrow). آن کزو بگذرد is a restrictive relative phrase 'the [sorrow] which passes from/over him' (کزو = از + او 'from him', بگذرد = pass). Together: 'does not have the grief which would pass/affect him.'
segments.s653-g581: نه negates the clause. شادان کند is 'makes joyful' (شادان = joyful; کند = makes/does). دل بنایافته is a nominal phrase 'the established/settled heart' (بنایافته = established); word order places the predicate before the noun for poetic compression: 'nor does the settled heart make (him) joyful.'
segments.s653-g582: نه‌گر here functions as a correlative negative 'nor/and if not' leading a conditional clause. بگذرد 'passes' with ز + او (written here as ز و) = 'from him' (i.e., 'if it passes from him'). شود تافته is 'becomes tāfta(n)/separate/distressed' — تافته is a predicate meaning 'made/turned (so)', here implying no disturbance. Overall: 'nor, if it departs from him, does he become (undone).' The clause again uses classical, somewhat compressed word order.
segments.s653-g583: چو = 'when'. از رنج وز بد تن is a coordinated phrase: از رنج 'from suffering' + وز (classical 'و ز' = 'and from') بد تن 'ill body' (بد = bad/ill, تن = body). آسان شود means 'becomes easy/relieved' (آسان = easy/relieved, شود = becomes). So: 'When the body is relieved from suffering and ill health ...'.
segments.s653-g584: ز here is the preposition 'from/of', نابودنیها is the plural noun 'things that will perish / perishable things', هراسان 'frightened/alarmed', and شود 'becomes'. The clause states the result: once relieved, the body becomes fearful of mortal/perishable things.
segments.s653-g593: چهارم is the ordinal 'fourth' introducing an item; که begins a relative/conditional clause ('whoever/that') and here links that clause to the ordinal. با هر کسی = 'with/ to any one' (با + هر + کسی), where هر کسی = 'anyone'. راز خویش = 'his own secret' (خوی + ش here functions as the reflexive/possessive construction; the possessive suffix ش marks 'his/her/its').
segments.s653-g594: بگوید is the third-person present (classical/poetic) form 'he tells/one tells' — following که it generalizes ('whoever tells'). برافرازد is a present/optative-like verb 'raises, lifts up' and takes آواز خویش ('his voice') as its object; word order is verb after subject-clause, typical of Persian poetry.
segments.s653-g595: به پنجم = 'as for the fifth' (the ordinal phrase marking the fifth rule). The second به before گفتار functions as a prepositional/adverbial marker meaning 'by/through' or 'in' (indicating manner) + گفتار 'speech/utterance'. ناسودمند is an adjective 'unprofitable/useless' modifying گفتار.
segments.s653-g596: تن خویش = 'his body' (تن = body; خوی + ش again yields the reflexive/possessive). دارد is the present 'has/experiences' (third-person singular). بدرد و گزند is a compound meaning 'pain and harm' — بدرد here functions as 'pain/hurt' (derived from a verbal root but used in the sense of suffering) and گزند = 'harm'.
segments.s653-g597: ششم گردد = 'the sixth becomes' (ششم = sixth; گردد is the third-person present 'becomes/is made'). ایمن ز = 'safe from' (ایمن followed by the poetic preposition ز = 'from'). نا استوار is a negative adjective 'unsteady/unstable' (نا- prefix + استوار). The phrase thus means 'becomes safe from instability'.
segments.s653-g598: همی is an adverb meaning 'continually/still' placed before the verb for emphasis. پرنیان (object) = 'silken robe/soft silk'. جوید is third-person present 'seeks/wants'. از خار بار = 'from a load/burden of thorns' (از = 'from', خار = 'thorn' or metaphorically 'affliction', بار = 'burden/load'), producing the ironic image 'seeks silk from a burden of thorns'.
segments.s653-g599: به هفتم = 'as for the seventh' (ordinal introducer). که again introduces the relative clause ('who/that'). بستیهد is an archaic/poetic third-person singular verb form; according to the provided sense set it carries the sense 'to be hostile/entangled' and here functions as 'is (so) in/engaged in' — followed by اندر دروغ 'in falsehood' (اندر = 'in/within', دروغ = 'lie/falsehood').
segments.s653-g600: به بی‌شرمی = 'with shamelessness' or 'in shamelessness' (به as prepositional particle + بی‌شرمی 'shamelessness'). اندر = 'in', بجوید is third-person present 'seeks', and فروغ = 'radiance/splendor' is the object sought; the clause says such a person seeks outward radiance/glory by shameless means.
