segments.s653-g201: چو at the start is a temporal subordinating conjunction meaning “when.” نان خورده شد is a perfect/passive construction: noun (نان) + participle (خورده) + شد, producing “the bread was eaten” or “when the bread had been eaten.” جام می‌خواستند is the main clause: جام (cup / often idiomatically ‘cup (of wine)’) is the object and می‌خواستند is a past plural verb meaning “they wanted.” The temporal clause introduced by چو sets the time for their wanting the cup.
segments.s653-g202: به marks beneficiary or direction (“for / to”). می‌جان here is a vocative/term of endearment (literally “mi‑jân”); روشن is an adjective qualifying that noun (“bright/clear/enlightened”), placed after the noun in this poetic order. بیاراستند is a past plural transitive verb “they adorned/they dressed/they embellished,” so the phrase means roughly “they adorned (for) the bright mi‑jân.”
segments.s653-g203: Word order here places the addressee/indirect object (بدانندگان, “the knowledgeable ones”) in front for emphasis; شاه بیدار is the subject phrase (“the king, awake/alert”), where بیدار is an epithet. گفت is the past verb “said.” So the sequence reads as “(To) the knowledgeable ones the awake king said.”
segments.s653-g204: که introduces reported speech/that-clause (“that / ‘…’ ”). دانش is the noun “knowledge.” گشاده is an adjective meaning “open/revealed,” and کنید is the 2nd-person plural (or polite) imperative/subjunctive of کردن: here “open/reveal (you [pl.]/you all).” از نهفت is a prepositional phrase meaning “from what is hidden” (از = from; نهفت = hidden/concealed). The clause thus reports the king’s command to make knowledge open rather than hidden.
segments.s653-g205: هران کس که is an archaic/poetic form meaning “whoever” or “anyone who.” دارد is a present/presentive verb “has/possesses.” به دل دانشی is a prepositional phrase where به دل = “in the heart/mind” and دانشی (a noun with the -ی ending) means “a knowledge” or “knowledge” one holds inwardly. Altogether: “whoever has knowledge in his heart…”
segments.s653-g206: بگوید here is a subjunctive/imperative-like present form meaning “let him tell / let him speak” (in classical Persian a present-subjunctive can render exhortation). م + را represents the object marker plus the first-person object (m = مرا, “me”; را = definite object marker placed after the object). ز + و (here written separately) stands for ز او = از او “from him.” بود is the copula “was/would be,” and رامشی is a noun meaning something like “counsel/rest/comfort” in this context. The whole second half means “from him there will be (or would be) counsel.”
segments.s653-g207: از ایشان (written together as ازیشان) means “from them / among them.” هران کس که is again “whoever / any one who.” دانا is an adjective meaning “wise/learned.” بدند is an older/polite past plural/copular form (here describing existence or state: “was/were” or in some contexts a potential/optative “would be”). The clause therefore introduces “among them, whoever was wise…”
segments.s653-g208: بگفتن is a verbal noun/infinitive “to speak / to say” (here describing the field in which they are judged). دلیر و توانا are a pair of adjectives “brave and capable/powerful.” The final بدند repeats the older past/plural/copular form, meaning “they were” or in context “they would be regarded as.” Grammatically this is a nominal/attributive sequence saying that a wise person among them would be deemed brave and able in speaking.
segments.s653-g1185: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of' marking source; here it introduces the source of the tale (ز + دهقان = 'from the dehqan'). کنارِ آن، کنون is an adverb 'now' positioned before the verb for immediate imperative force. بشنو is a second-person singular imperative ('listen') with the subject (تو) omitted, and این داستان is the object 'this tale' (this noun phrase follows the verb, a natural flexible order in classical Persian). The whole segment amounts to a command: 'Now, listen to this tale from the dehqan.'
segments.s653-g1186: که here is a relative pronoun 'which/that' (not the causal sense) introducing a clause that qualifies the preceding noun (the tale or its teller). برخواند is a past third-person form meaning 'he recited/read aloud' (the prefix بر- intensifies the action as reciting/read-aloud). از گفته باستان is a prepositional phrase 'from ancient saying(s)/accounts' where گفته is a noun 'saying/report' and باستان 'ancient' modifies it; together they specify the source of what was recited. The implied subject of برخواند is carried over from the previous noun (the dehqan/he).
segments.s653-g705: 'یکی' here functions adverbially as 'once' (one time). 'آفرین کرد' is a light-verb construction meaning 'uttered praise' or 'praised' (آفرین = acclamation/praise, کرد = did/uttered). 'بوزرجمهر' is the agent placed after the verb in this older/poetic word order (verb + subject).
segments.s653-g706: 'که‌ای' is a vocative/exclamatory address ('که، ای' = 'O' called out to someone); it introduces direct speech. 'شاه' is the addressee 'king'. 'روشن‌دل' and 'خوب‌چهر' are postpositive adjectives describing the king ('clear/bright‑hearted' and 'fair‑faced'), joined by 'و'.
segments.s653-g707: 'چنان دان' is an imperative/hortative phrase meaning 'consider it thus' or 'know this' (چنان = thus; دان = consider/know). 'که' introduces the content clause that follows. 'اندر جهان نیز شاه' begins that clause ('in the world, likewise, a king...'), with 'نیز' adding emphasis ('also/indeed').
segments.s653-g708: 'یکی' here functions as an indefinite 'one/anyone' (part of an expression implying 'no one'). 'چون' = 'like/as'; 'تو' = 'you'. 'ننهاد' is the negative past of نهادن ('did not place/has not been placed'), so in context with 'یکی' it yields the sense 'no one like you has been placed'. 'برسرکلاه' is a fused locative phrase 'upon the head (of the cap/crown)' — i.e., 'to wear the crown'. Word order is older/poetic (indefinite phrase + simile + negative verb + locative).
segments.s653-g709: This is a parallel list introduced by repeated 'به' marking the domain/benefit ('in' or 'with regard to'). 'داد' = 'justice', 'دانش' = 'knowledge', 'تاج' = 'crown', 'تخت' = 'throne'. The repetition of 'به' gives a litany of royal qualities/privileges.
segments.s653-g710: Continuation of the same enumerative pattern: 'به فر' ('in/with royal glory'), 'و به چهر' ('and in appearance/face'), 'برای' is ambiguous in the provided senses and here likely a poetic noun (something like 'renown' or 'prowess') rather than the preposition 'for' — I've marked it accordingly below, and 'به بخت' ('and in fortune').
segments.s653-g711: 'چو' = 'when/as'. 'پرهیزکاری' is the verbal noun meaning 'piety/abstinence' (here an action). 'کند' is the auxiliary/verb 'does/engages in' (present/habitual sense), and 'شهریار' (king) is the subject placed after the verb in poetic order: 'when the king practices piety'.
segments.s653-g712: 'چه' is an exclamatory particle 'how' (introducing an evaluative clause). 'نیکوست' is the predicative adjective 'is good/fitting'. 'پرهیز' (piety/abstinence) is the thing evaluated, and 'با تاجدار' literally 'with the crowned one' — here meaning 'for the crowned ruler' or 'in a crowned ruler it is especially fitting'. The phrase states the commendable nature of a sovereign's piety.
segments.s653-g905: سخنها is the plural subject 'words/speeches'; که introduces a relative clause 'which/that'; جان را uses را as a definite/object/beneficiary marker ('for the soul'); بود is the past copula 'was' (classical word order places the verb after its complements); سودمند is the predicate adjective 'useful/beneficial'. The whole segment means 'words that were useful to the soul.'
segments.s653-g906: همی is an older/poetic adverbial particle (here marking continued or emphatic action); مرد is 'man/person' (the referent of the clause); بی‌ارز is an adjectival compound 'worthless'; گردد is the present/ subjunctive/old-style copular/auxiliary form 'becomes' (here used in the sense 'becomes' or 'turns into'); بلند is an adjective 'high/lofty' (here 'proud, lofty'). The clause says that the man 'becomes proudly worthless' (word order in poetry places the verb before or among adjectives).
segments.s653-g907: ازو 'from him/of him' marks source; گنج 'treasure' is the noun; گویا is parenthetical 'it seems/apparently'; نگیرد is the negative present/subjunctive form 'does not take' or 'would not take' (classical usage can mean 'takes little or does not take'); کمی is an adverb/quantifier 'a little' placed after the verb for emphasis. Together: 'it seems he takes little treasure from him.'
segments.s653-g908: شنودن is the old infinitive/masdar form of 'to hear' (used as a verbal-noun here: 'hearing' or 'listening'); بود is the past copula 'was'; مرد را uses را to mark the person affected ('for the man'); خرمی is a nominal predicate 'joy/delight'. The word order 'شنودن بود مرد را خرمی' yields 'listening was the man's delight.'
segments.s653-g909: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so'; گفت here is the verb 'said' (in the text it appears in verb-before-subject order, common in poetry); موبد is the subject 'mōbed' (Zoroastrian priest); به بوزرجمهر is the indirect object 'to Bozorgmehr' introduced by the preposition به. The phrase reads 'thus the mobed said to Bozorgmehr.'
segments.s653-g910: که‌ای (here read as the opening of direct speech/vocative) introduces the address; نامورتر is the comparative adjective 'more renowned' (تر = comparative suffix); ز is the poetic form of از used in comparisons 'than'; گردان is an adjective 'revolving' (literally 'turning'); سپهر is 'sky/heavens'. The phrase 'نامورتر ز گردان سپهر' means 'more famed than the revolving heavens.'
segments.s653-g911: چه دانی is the classical interrogative idiom 'how do you know' (چه + 2nd-person present of دانستن); که introduces the subordinate clause 'that...'; بیشیش is an emphatic/comparative 'the more' or 'more and more'; بگزایدت is an old-form verb 'بگزاید' ('may choose/appoint/pick') with the enclitic ت (you) as object — the b- prefix marks subjunctive/volitional in classical Persian. Together: 'How do you know that he may prefer/choose you more?'
segments.s653-g912: This clause is elliptical/poetic: بود is the past copula 'would be/will be'; روز 'day' is the subject; بفزایدت is the same volitional/subjunctive verb with enclitic 'ت' 'may increase you' (ب- prefix + افزاید stem), meaning 'may add to you' or 'may advance you.' The first token چوکمی is problematic/rare: in manuscript/poetic usage it can function as an adverbial/measure ('perhaps one day' / 'a single day') or be parsed as چو کمی ('if little'); here it yields the sense 'perhaps/a single' and is treated as a qualification of 'روز'. Altogether the clause says roughly 'perhaps a single day will increase you' or 'a day may make you greater.'
segments.s653-g761: هرانگه که introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'when/whenever'. یازد is a poetic/past intransitive verb meaning 'broke forth/appeared'; ببد is an archaic copula/past-form 'was' linked to کار 'deed/affair'. دست 'hand' is the agent/source (literally 'a deed of the hand'). Word order is temporal particle → relative/connector → verb → copula + noun phrase.
segments.s653-g762: دل شاه بچه is a genitive chain 'the heart of the king's child' (شاه بچه = the king's child/prince). نباید is an impersonal modal 'must not / should not', and شکست here conveys the idea 'be broken' (breakage) — together giving 'must not be broken.'
segments.s653-g763: چو is a temporal 'when'. بر + بد کنش is a prepositional phrase 'upon/toward bad action' (bad action = wrongdoing). دست گردد دراز is a predicate: گردش (here گردد) 'becomes' and دراز 'long/extended' — literally 'the hand becomes long/extends', i.e. 'reaches out'.
segments.s653-g764: جز marks an exception. به خون 'toward/into blood' = 'to bloodshed'. به فرمانِ یزدان is an ablative/agentive phrase 'by the command of God' (به here = 'by'). میاز is an archaic/poetic verb; in context it functions as a prohibitive/jussive meaning roughly 'do not go/seek' — so the clause reads 'do not resort to bloodshed except by God's command.'
segments.s653-g765: وگر is conditional 'if'. دشمنی is the object 'enmity/hostility'. یابی is second-person present 'you find' (conditional sense: 'if you find ...'). اندر دلش is a locative 'in his heart' with the enclitic possession -ش 'his'.
segments.s653-g766: چو here = 'when'. خوباشد is an impersonal/fitting expression 'it is good/it is fitting'. از بوستان 'from the garden'. بگسلش is a verbal form (بَ + گسل + object clitic -ش) meaning 'separate/tear him away' — an instruction to remove him from the garden when appropriate.
segments.s653-g767: که‌گر is a conditional 'if'. دیر ماند is a verb phrase 'remains long / lingers'. بنیرو is an adverbial or circumstantial phrase 'with force / into strength' and شود is the present/future copula 'becomes' — together: 'if it lingers long, it will become strong.'
segments.s653-g768: وزو is an archaic contracted prepositional/ pronominal form meaning essentially 'from it / because of it' (not provided in the sense list). باغ شاهی 'the royal garden' (باغ = garden, شاهی = royal). پرآهو is an adjective 'full of gazelles' and شود 'becomes' — 'the royal garden becomes full of gazelles (as a consequence).'
segments.s653-g401: وگر is a conditional particle ('if' / 'but if') introducing a hypothetical; ناسزا را has the postpositive object marker را, marking 'ناسزا' as the direct object; بسایی is a classical verbal form (present/subjunctive/optative) meaning 'you anoint/cover/ornament' and here takes 'ناسزا را' as its object; به مشک uses به to mark the instrument/means ('with musk').
segments.s653-g402: This clause contains two negated verbs with the negative prefix ن- (نبوید, نروید) expressing 'does not emit/smell' and 'does not grow/come forth' respectively. گل is the subject ('a flower'), از خار خشک is a prepositional phrase 'from (a) dry thorn' where خار (thorn) is followed by its adjective خشک ('dry'). The overall sense is: even when you perfume it, the insult will not scent it and a flower will not spring from a dry thorn.
segments.s653-g403: سخن پرسی is a nominalized verbal phrase meaning 'asking (for) words' or 'questioning'; پرسی here functions as a verbal noun (the act of asking). از introduces the addressee/source ('from'), and گنگ‌گر is an archaic conditional-form compound meaning 'if he is mute' (the -گر suffix in older Persian often forms an 'if' clause); مرد کر is a noun phrase 'a deaf man' (noun followed by adjective).
segments.s653-g404: به بار آید is an idiomatic clause: بار ('burden') plus آید ('comes') meaning 'if it comes as a burden / if the message reaches (him)'. ورای ('to him' / 'there') points to the person; ناید is the negative form 'does not come/reach', and ببر is an imperative 'take (it) away' (not the animal sense). The sequence sets up the practical outcome: if the message arrives, present it; if it does not, withdraw it.
segments.s653-g405: یکی گفت is the conventional opening 'someone said' (یکی = 'a certain one/someone'; گفت here functions as 'said' in the narrative). کاندر is an older fused prepositional form meaning 'that in' or 'in which' (kan + dar), and سرای سپنج is a genitive/compound image 'the house (سرای) of sponge (سپنج)' — a figurative location introduced by کاندر.
segments.s653-g406: نباشد is a negated present/existential verb 'there is not' (third‑person negative of بودن) introducing the predicate. خردمند is the predicate noun 'a wise man'; بی‌درد و رنج is a compound describing absence of 'pain and suffering' (بی‌- prefix = 'without'). So the clause states that no wise person exists free from pain and hardship.
segments.s653-g407: چه سازیم is an interrogative clause 'what shall we do?' with سازیم (first‑person plural subjunctive/optative) meaning 'we shall/may make/do'. تا introduces a purpose clause 'so that / in order that', and نام نیک آوریم is the purpose: 'we bring a good name' (نام = reputation, نیک = good; آوریم = 'we bring/obtain').
segments.s653-g408: درآغاز is an adverbial phrase 'at the beginning / at the outset' modifying the following clause. فرجام نیک آوریم repeats the verb آوریم and expresses the desire for 'a good outcome/end' (فرجام = end/outcome, نیک = good), so the couplet asks for both a good reputation and a good ending.
segments.s653-g1153: اگر introduces a conditional ('if'). پادشا is the subject 'king'. کوه آتش is a poetic noun‑noun phrase 'a mountain of fire' (no ezāfe written because of meter). بدی here is an archaic/poetic verbal form concluding the conditional clause (an old form of دادن meaning 'gives' or 'were to give').
segments.s653-g1154: پرستنده را is the definite/accusative-marked beneficiary 'the worshipper' (را marks definiteness/emphasis). زیستن خوش is a nominalised infinitive + adjective 'pleasant living' (adjective follows noun in poetic word order). The final بدی is the same archaic verb form (gives/would give) that makes this the result of the conditional.
segments.s653-g1155: چو here = 'when'. The subject follows (آتش) with گه 'at times/sometimes' as an adverbial insertion. خشم سوزان is a noun + participial/adjectival phrase 'burning with anger'. بود is the past copula 'was', making this a temporal descriptive clause.
segments.s653-g1156: چوخشنود (poetic fusion of چو + خشنود) = 'when (it) is pleased'; باشد is a present/subjunctive/correlative copula used in classical style ('be/is'), and فروزان بود is the predicate 'was radiant/bright'. The clause contrasts the fire's moods.
segments.s653-g1157: ازو 'from him/of him' sets the perspective. یک زمان 'at one time' is a temporal phrase. شیروشهدست is a compressed poetic predicate (= 'is milk and honey', literally شیر و شهد است) where the copula is fused to the adjective; بهر 'for' marks the beneficiary or relation to others.
segments.s653-g1158: به دیگر زمان = 'at another time' (به used adverbially + دیگر زمان). چون introduces a simile 'like/as'. گزاینده زهر is an agentive phrase 'the biter/stinger of poison' (گزاینده = one that stings/bites), completing the contrast without an explicit copula.
segments.s653-g1159: به کردار = 'in the manner of' (به + noun). دریا is the simile 'sea'. بود is the past copula 'was'. کارشاه (placed after the verb for poetic order) is the subject 'the king's work/affairs.'
segments.s653-g1160: به فرمان او is an instrumental/agentive prepositional phrase 'by his command'. تابد (from تافتن) is a classical present/poetic verb 'shines'. از چرخ ماه = 'the moon from the sky' (from = out of/from the heavens) with inverted noun order for meter.
segments.s653-g969: همان is a demonstrative ('that, the very') pointing to a particular kind of friendship. دوستی is the noun 'friendship' (the thing to be made). باکسی here is orthographic for با کسی ('with someone') and functions as the prepositional complement; کن is the imperative 'make/do' addressing the listener; بلند is a postpositive adjective 'lofty/noble' modifying دوستی (adjectives often follow the noun in Persian poetry).
segments.s653-g970: که introduces a relative clause ('which/that'). باشد is the classical 3rd-person present/subjunctive of 'to be' used here to state what that friendship will be. بسختی is an adverbial phrase 'in hardship / under difficulty'. تو را uses را as the objective/beneficiary marker attached to the pronoun 'you'. سودمند is the predicative adjective 'useful/profitable', completing the relative clause 'that will be useful to you in hardship.'
segments.s653-g971: تو is the emphatic/explicit 2nd-person subject 'you'. در انجمن is a locative prepositional phrase 'in assembly / in gatherings'. خامشی is the noun 'silence' and is the thing being ordered. برگزین is the imperative 'choose' (here: 'select/keep'), placed at the end as is common in Persian imperative constructions.
segments.s653-g972: چوخواهی is a fixed phrase 'as you wish / if you will'. که introduces the ensuing result or consequence. یک سر (literally 'one head') is idiomatic here meaning 'with one voice / all together' (سر used in the sense of unity or single utterance). کنند is the 3rd-person plural verb 'they do/make' (subjunctive/optative sense in classical style). آفرین is an interjection 'bravo / acclamation' that functions as the object of کنند ('make/shout
segments.s653-g973: چو is a temporal/conditional conjunction 'when/if'. گویی here functions as the verb 'you say/utter' (classical present form 'تو گویی' = 'when you say'); in the provided senses list the verbal sense is not given, so the mapping is left undefined. همان modifies گوی: 'that same saying'. کموختی is an adjective/participial compound 'little-learned / poorly learned' modifying گوی—i.e., the saying you have only learned a little of.
segments.s653-g974: به here is the preposition 'by/through'. آموختن is the verbal noun/infinitive 'learning' and is the object of به ('by learning'). درجگر is an adverbial compound 'in the heart' (در + جگر) indicating internal emotion. سوختی is a past tense verb 'you burned/were consumed' used metaphorically ('you were consumed in your heart'), so the clause says one is inwardly consumed by their learning or the urge to learn.
segments.s653-g975: سخن‌سنج is a compound noun 'speech-evaluator' or an instruction to 'weigh/judge speech'. و is the coordinating conjunction 'and'. دینار گنجی is a compound meaning 'a treasure/hoard of dinars' (دینار = coin/money, گنجی here attributive 'treasure-like' or 'treasure of'). مسنج is the imperative 'weigh / measure' (verb at the end), instructing the addressee to compare/judge both speech and money.
segments.s653-g976: که introduces an explanatory or result clause ('for/that'). در دانشی is a locative/qualifying phrase 'in regard to/with respect to knowledge' or 'in the presence of knowledge'. مرد is the subject 'a man' (here 'a learned man' by implication). خوارست is an archaic/persianized predicate meaning 'is held cheap/despised' (the provided senses list does not give this meaning, so the mapping is left undefined). گنج is the noun 'treasure' used predicatively ('treasure is [so-and-so]')—overall: 'for in matters of knowledge treasure is despised (by a man of learning).'
segments.s653-g633: بدو گفت = 'he said to him' (بدو = to him; گفت = said). گرنیستش is a poetic contraction of گر نیستش, a conditional phrase 'if he is not/if he lacks'; بخردی is a noun in the oblique (with a prepositional/relational sense) meaning 'wisdom'. The clause introduces reported speech: 'he said to him: if he has no wisdom...'.
segments.s653-g634: This is the main predication of the reported clause. خرد is the topic/noun 'wisdom'; خلعتی is an attributive noun 'a bestowed robe' (خلعت + -ی), روشنست is the poetic fused copula/adjective 'is bright/clear' (روشن + ـست), and ایزدی is an adjective 'divine'. Word order is noun + attribute + predicate + adjective: 'wisdom is a bright divine robe.'
segments.s653-g635: چنین داد پاسخ is an idiom meaning 'thus gave answer' (چنین = thus; داد = gave; پاسخ = answer). که introduces the content clause. دانش بهست is a short predicate: دانش 'knowledge' and بهست (archaic/poetic) = 'it suffices / is enough'.
segments.s653-g636: چو here is a temporal/conditional 'when/if'; دانا بود = 'one was/is wise' (دانا adjective + بود past/copula). برمهان is a plural/collective of برمه (cloak/shawl) and برمهست is a nominal predicate stating identity/insistence ('is a bermeh' / 'is but a cloak'). The clause means roughly 'when one is wise, the cloak is merely a cloak' (i.e. externals matter less).
segments.s653-g637: بدو گفت‌گر functions as the speech-introducer 'he said to him' (بدو = to him; گفت‌گر here is the particle/phrase introducing reported conditional speech — the provided lexical senses don't match this poetic usage). راه دانش is the object 'the way/path of knowledge' and نجست is a poetic past of جستن used here as 'sought/asked/searched for'.
segments.s653-g638: بدین is the fused locative 'ب + این' = 'to/at this'; آب = 'water/stream'; هرگز = 'never' (negative adverb); روان را is marked with the object/definite marker را, here روان = 'soul/spirit' (poetic: mood/inner self); نشست is past 'sat/settled'. Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect: 'to this water never did the soul sit/settle.'
segments.s653-g639: Again, چنین داد پاسخ 'thus gave answer' introduces the reported clause (که). از is the preposition 'from/of'; مرد = 'man' (here 'a man' or 'one'); گرد is ambiguous in isolation (it can mean 'around/about' or be part of a compound) and likely forms part of a descriptive phrase referring to a kind of man. The phrase begins the clause that will describe which man is to be counted low.
segments.s653-g640: سرخویش here in the manuscript is a fused form (likely سر خویش = 'one's own head/self'); را is the definite/object marker; خوار is a predicative adjective 'despised/contemptible'; باید + شمرد is the necessity/modal construction 'ought to consider' (باید = must/ought, شمرد = 'to count/consider' — here the verb is used in the sense 'one should reckon/think of'). The whole clause: 'one ought to consider such a man contemptible.'
segments.s653-g193: همی is the classical progressive/adverbial marker ('continually/kept on'); دانش آموخت is a past action composed of noun + past verb (آموخت = 'learned'): together 'kept learning'. The conjunction و links this clause to the following verbal phrase; اندر گذشت is an archaic prepositional + past-verb sequence meaning 'passed beyond / surpassed (in that matter),' i.e. he went ahead in it.
segments.s653-g194: و is the simple conjunction 'and'; زان = 'from that / thereby' points back to his learning; فیلسوفان is the plural 'philosophers'; سرش is سر + enclitic ش 'his head' used figuratively; برگذشت is a past-verb meaning 'passed over / passed by' — here figurative 'he rose above / surpassed them.'
segments.s653-g195: چنان ... که is a classical subordinating phrase 'so/so much that / it happened that'; بد here is an archaic/formal usage (historically related to بود) introducing the circumstance; بنشست is past 3rd‑sing 'he sat down'; روزی is a temporal adverb 'one day' (used idiomatically); بخوان here is an infinitive/intentive 'to read/recite' (he sat down to read).
segments.s653-g196: بفرمود is the honorific/polite past 'he commanded / he said'; کاین (که + این) introduces the reported content 'that: these ...'; موبدان را has را as the definite/object marker marking Mobil as the object of the order; بخوان is the reported imperative/infinitive 'call/summon' (i.e. 'Summon these mobeds').
segments.s653-g197: که introduces a relative/purpose clause 'that / so that'; باشند is 3rd‑pl present/jussive ('that they be' / 'let them be'); دانا and دانش‌پذیر are predicative adjectives/compounds: دانا 'wise/learned' and دانش‌پذیر 'receptive to knowledge' (دانش + پذیر 'one who receives knowledge').
segments.s653-g198: A string of role‑nouns joined by و: سراینده 'one who composes/sings (a poet or singer)'; باهش 'with him' (با + ـش enclitic 'with him/her/it'); یادگیر is a compound 'memory‑taker' = 'one who learns/retains (memorizer)'. The list describes the qualities/roles required of those to be summoned.
segments.s653-g199: برفتند is past 3rd‑pl 'they went forth / departed' (بر‑ prefix indicates motion); بیداردل is a compound adjective 'wakeful‑hearted / alert‑hearted' placed before the noun; موبدان is the plural 'mōbeds' (Zoroastrian priests) — verb precedes the noun phrase here, a common epic ordering.
segments.s653-g200: This clause states the action/aim: دانشی راز is a genitive 'the secret of knowledge'; جسته is the perfect/past participle/verb 'having sought / sought'; the initial زهر is poetic/metaphorical here (used idiomatically) and ردان functions as an agent/epithet referring to the wise ones — together: 'the wise ones who sought the secret of knowledge.' Word order omits an explicit ezāfe but the nouns are linked in a genitive relation.
segments.s653-g1137: وگر (if) introduces a conditional. نیستت is نیست (there is not / you do not have) plus the 2nd-person clitic -ت, so the verb expresses lack on your part: “you do not have.” آگهی is a noun meaning “knowledge/information,” and زان (= ز + آن) means “of that / from that.” The phrase as a whole means “if you have no knowledge of that sin.”
segments.s653-g1138: برهنه is an adjective “bare/exposed” placed before the noun it qualifies. دلت is دل + the 2nd-person suffix ت, so “your heart.” را is the definite/object marker attaching to the whole NP. ببر is the 2nd-person imperative “take!” and نزد + شاه is the prepositional phrase “to/into the presence of the king.” Together: “take your heart, laid bare, to the king.”
segments.s653-g1139: وگر نه is an emphatic conditional “and if not / but if not.” هیچ تاب means “any strength/endurance” (هیچ negates تاب). اندر is the locative/into “into/within.” آری here is an archaic verbal form meaning “you bring / you put” (not the common معنی “yes”); it functions as the verb governing the preceding object, so the clause reads roughly “if you bring no endurance into your heart.” به دل marks the goal “into the heart.”
segments.s653-g1140: بدو here is the prepositional/allative “to him” (not the imperative “run”), and روی is “face.” منمای is a classical negative imperative form meaning “do not show / do not reveal” (2nd person, negative). و links the clauses. پی means “after / in pursuit of / following,” and برگسل is a poetic image (a wind that scatters leaves). The clause commands: “do not show your face to him, and do not follow the leaf‑scattering wind.”
segments.s653-g1141: به فرش gives a locative setting “on / at the carpet.” ببیند is a 3rd-person present form “he sees” (classical present/indicative). نهان تو را is an object phrase: نهان “hidden,” تو “your,” and را the object marker; the object marker attaches to the whole phrase so the verb takes “your hidden [self/secret]” as its object.
segments.s653-g1142: This is a noun phrase listed as additional objects of the preceding verb. دل “heart” modified by the adjective کژ “crooked,” then و joins تیره روان “dark spirit/soul.” The final تو را repeats the possessor and object-marker that scope over these nouns — i.e. “(he sees) your crooked heart and your dark soul.” Word order is inverted for poetic effect but را still marks the whole as object.
segments.s653-g1143: ازان پس is a temporal phrase “after that / thereafter.” نیابی is the 2nd-person present/near‑future negative of یافتن “to find” (here “you will not find”). تو is the subject pronoun. ز (زو) is the preposition “from / of” and نیکوی is the noun “goodness / good thing”; together زو نیکوی = “goodness from him.” The clause means “after that you will not find goodness in him.”
segments.s653-g1144: همان is a demonstrative/identifying word “that / the same” modifying گرم گفتار “warm speech/words.” او is the possessive “his,” and نشنوی is the 2nd-person negative present of شنیدن “to hear” (you do not / will not hear). The clause literally: “you will not hear that warm speech of his.”
segments.s653-g1145: در پادشا همچو دریا is a prepositional/nominal phrase literally 'in/with the king like the sea' — here it functions as the thing to be compared (i.e. 'count/regard the king as like a sea'). شمر is an archaic/poetic verb meaning 'count/consider' (imperative or jussive sense) and links this clause to the following description. The copula is omitted as is common in classical Persian.
segments.s653-g1146: پرستنده is an agentive noun 'one who worships/adores/adores of' and here describes the attribute assigned to the king by the previous verb; ملاح and کشتی هنر form the objects: 'the sailor and the ship's art/skill' (کشتی is followed by هنر, so هنر qualifies the ship: 'shipcraft' or 'seamanship'). و simply coordinates the two objects.
segments.s653-g1147: This clause omits the copula 'است' and reads as a verbless nominal sentence: 'سخن (is) لنگر' — 'speech (is) the anchor', and after the conjunction و the phrase 'بادبانش خرد' means 'his sail (is) wisdom'. بادبانش uses the pronominal suffix -ش 'his'.
segments.s653-g1148: به دریا is a prepositional phrase 'into/to the sea'; خردمند is the subject 'the wise (man)'; چون introduces a temporal clause 'when', and بگذرد (3rd sg. present/subjunctive from گذرْدن) means 'he passes / goes'. The whole yields 'when the wise man goes to sea.' Note: چون here functions as 'when' in classical usage.
segments.s653-g1149: همان is an emphatic demonstrative 'that very'; بادبان is 'sail' and را is the definite object marker marking بادبان as the thing acted on; کند is 3rd sg. present of 'کردن' used here as 'makes/do' and سایه‌دار is a compound adjective 'shadow-bearing / sheltering / protective' that functions as the resulting state ('makes that sail sheltering').
segments.s653-g1150: که introduces an explanatory clause 'for/that'; هم ... و هم ... is a correlative pair meaning 'both ... and ...'; سایه‌دارست is the fused predicate 'is shade-bearing / is protective' (سایه‌دار + است) and مایه‌دار means 'rich/bringing resources' or 'a source of wealth'. Together: 'for it is both protective and a source of wealth.'
segments.s653-g1151: کسی 'someone' followed by the archaic relative/particle کو (here 'who/that') introduces the relative clause; ندارد is present negative 'does not have'; روانش is 'his soul' (روان + pronominal -ش) and خرد is the predicate 'wisdom'. The clause means 'someone who does not have wisdom in his soul.'
segments.s653-g1152: سزدگر is an idiomatic/archaic conditional phrase meaning roughly 'if it be fitting / if one deems it proper'; در پادشا 'to/in the king' indicates the target/reciprocal context; نسپرد is negative of سپردن 'to entrust/hand over' (here jussive/imperative/optative sense: 'do not entrust'). The sense is: 'If it is fitting, do not entrust him to the king.'
segments.s653-g97: به دل گفت is an idiom meaning 'he said to his heart' or 'he said to himself' (به = to; دل = inner mind/soul). گفـت is the past tense verb 'said' referring back to the subject of the stanza; کین here is an archaic demonstrative (effectively 'that/this') rather than the usual sense 'vengeance' in this line, and کودک هوشمند is the noun phrase 'clever child' (noun + adjective). Word order follows Persian: indirect object + verb + the clause describing whom he referred to.
segments.s653-g98: بجایی رسد is a verbal phrase where بجایی (بجا) means 'to a fitting/place-worthy' or 'properly' and رسد (3rd sing. present/subjunctive/optative form of رسیدن) means 'will reach/attain'. در بزرگی بلند is a prepositional phrase 'in/into a lofty greatness' (در = in; بزرگی = greatness; بلند = lofty/high). The verb comes before the location phrase, typical of Persian clause structure.
segments.s653-g99: وزان is a connective adverb meaning 'from that/thereupon' or 'after that'. بیشه پویان is the subject phrase 'grove-roamers' or 'those who seek the thicket' (بیشه = grove; پویان = those who roam/seek). به راه آمدند is a verbal phrase 'took to the road / came onto the road' (آمدند = 3rd pl. past of آمدن). Word order places the temporal/locative connective first, then subject, then verb phrase.
segments.s653-g100: خرامان is an adverb/adjectival adverb meaning 'with a proud/stately gait'. به نزدیک شاه آمدند is a prepositional phrase + verb 'they came near the king' (نزدیک = near; شاه = king; آمدند = they came). The adverbial (خرامان) modifies the whole clause; verb is at the end in canonical Persian order.
segments.s653-g101: فرستاده is the subject 'the envoy/messenger'. از پیش کودک برفت is a verb phrase where از پیش = 'from before / out of the presence of' and کودک is the object of that preposition; برفت is an older/periphrastic past form meaning 'went away' or 'departed'. The literal order is subject + prepositional phrase + verb.
segments.s653-g102: برتخت کسری is a locative phrase 'upon Khosrow's throne' (برتخت = on the throne; کسری = Khosrow/Kisra). خرامید is a past-tense verb 'strode proudly' with کسری understood as its subject (Khosrow strode). The final token تفت is anomalous in the provided sense list and likely an interjection or scribal/metrical particle here; its precise lexical sense is unclear in the given inventory.
segments.s653-g103: بدو گفت is 'he said to him' (بدو = to him; گفت = said). کای شاه نوشین‌روان is a vocative address: کای = 'O' (vocative), شاه = 'king', نوشین‌روان = 'sweet‑souled / pleasant‑spirited' — a complimentary epithet. The verb گفت introduces the direct speech that follows.
segments.s653-g104: تویی is the copula 'you are'. خفته بیدار places two adjectives in contrast: خفته = 'asleep' and بیدار = 'awake' (here imperative sense 'wake!'/contrastive exhortation). و links the last phrase دولت جوان, where دولت means 'fortune/sovereignty' and جوان qualifies it as 'young/vigorous' — a poetic exhortation to rouse and recall the fresh prosperity of youth. Word order is subject/copyula + adjectives + conjunction + noun phrase (state of affairs).
segments.s653-g1113: نکوهیده باشد گل آن درخت: نکوهیده is a past-participle/adjective meaning “censured/blamed” used predicatively; باشد is the classical present/subjunctive/optative form of بودن (here functioning as the copula, roughly “is / should be”). Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect: the predicate (نکوهیده باشد) appears before the subject phrase گل آن درخت (“the flower of that tree”).
segments.s653-g1114: که نپراگند بار بر تاج وتخت: که introduces a relative clause (“which/that”). نپراگند is the negative form of a verb meaning “(does) not scatter / (does) not spread” (classical/poetic morphology: ن- + verb stem). بار is the direct noun object (here “load/fruit/produce”) and بر تاج و تخت is a prepositional phrase “upon the crown and the throne.” The clause qualifies the preceding subject (the tree’s flower).
segments.s653-g1115: ز کسهای او پیش او بدمگوی: ز (poetic از) marks the object/topic “about / of”; کسهای او = “his people/men.” پیش او means “in his presence / before him.” بدمگوی is here an admonitive verbal form meaning “speak ill / be an ill-speaker” (used addressingly; in context it functions as a prohibition/advice: “do not speak ill…”). The whole phrase is an instruction about speech in the ruler’s presence.
segments.s653-g1116: که کمتر کنی نزد او آبروی: که introduces the reason/result (“for / because / that”). کمتر is an adverb/adjectival element meaning “less” or “to lessen.” کنی is the 2nd-person present/subjunctive of کردن (here “you make / you cause”); the subject (تو) is omitted. نزد او (“in his presence / in his estimation”) indicates whose opinion is affected, and آبروی is the object “reputation/honor.” The clause explains the bad effect of speaking ill before the ruler.
segments.s653-g1117: و‌گر پرسدت هرچ دانی نگوی: وگر (= اگر) introduces a conditional “if.” پرسدت is a classical/perfect-form verbal phrase meaning “(he/they) asked you” or idiomatically “if someone asks you”; here it serves in a general conditional sense. هرچ دانی = “whatever you know” (هرچ = whatever; دانی second-person present of دانستن). نگوی is a negative imperative/subjunctive form (ن + گو = “do not say / do not tell”), giving the advice not to reveal what is asked.
segments.s653-g1118: به بسیار گفتن مبر آبروی: به marks the instrument/means (“by/through”). بسیار گفتن is a verbal-noun (masdar) phrase “saying much / excessive speaking.” مبر is the negative imperative of برداشتن/گرفتن “do not take / do not remove.” آبروی is the object “reputation/honor.” Put together: “By saying too much, do not take away reputation.”
segments.s653-g1119: هرآنکس که بسیار گوید دروغ: هرآنکس که = “whoever / anyone who.” بسیار modifies the verb and here means “often / much / many [times].” گوید is the present (classical) form of گفتن “says / speaks” (used generally), and دروغ is the direct object “lies.” The clause states a general truth about habitual lying.
segments.s653-g1120: به نزدیک شاهان نگیرد فروغ: به نزدیک شاهان is a prepositional phrase “near / in the presence of kings” (به + نزدیک + شاهان). نگیرد is a negative present/future 3rd-person singular of گرفتن/گرفتن “does not obtain / will not take,” used as a general predictive statement. فروغ is the abstract direct object “radiance / splendor / figuratively: glory/favor.” Together: “will not gain splendor (favor) in the presence of kings.”
segments.s653-g929: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when' introducing a subordinate clause. گوینده‌ای is an indefinite noun phrase 'a speaker' (–ای marks indefiniteness). کو here functions as a relative word ('who/that') linking this noun phrase to the following description 'no seat/rank' (negation appears in the next word in the line). برجایگاه means 'position/seat' and completes the relative clause describing the speaker.
segments.s653-g930: سخن گفت is a verb phrase meaning 'spoke' (سخن as the object/thing spoken and گفت the past-tense verb 'said/spoke'); the conjunction و links to the following clause. ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of', and دور شد is an adjectival predicate + auxiliary ('became distant/was removed') with دور as the predicative adjective and شد as the past auxiliary 'became'. فر و جاه are the two coordinated nouns ('glory and rank/status') that are the syntactic subject/experiencer of 'became distant' (typical Persian word order places the predicate before the auxiliary).
segments.s653-g931: همان is a demonstrative 'that/the one' used to pick out the person. کو again introduces a relative clause 'who/that'. سخن سر به سر is an idiomatic expression: سر به سر ('from head to head') intensifies سخن to mean 'the speech from beginning to end' and نشنود is the present negative form of شنیدن ('does not hear'). Together the clause means 'the one who does not hear the whole speech.'
segments.s653-g932: نداند is the classical present/factual verb 'does not know/understand' referring back to the same person. به گفتار here means 'by the words / in the speech' (به marks the domain or means). و هم نگرود joins a second coordinated predicate: نگرود (from گرویدن) with the negative prefix ن- means 'does not take to/does not accept' or 'will not adopt' — the clause therefore says he neither understands by speech nor accepts it. The conjunction که is not present; this is a coordinate structure.
segments.s653-g933: به چیزی ندارد is a negated existential/possessive construction 'has no regard/eye for anything' where به چیزی marks the object/target ('for anything') and ندارد is 'does not have'. خردمند is the subject/noun 'the wise man' and چشم ('eye') is used metaphorically—literally 'the wise man has no eye for anything.' The order (object phrase before ندارد and noun after ندارد) reflects poetic inversion but parses as '[for] nothing does the wise man have an eye.'
segments.s653-g934: کزو = 'from which / from whom' introduces a relative clause describing the 'anything' just mentioned. بازماند is a present/subjunctive form of بازماندن 'to remain / be left behind' — here meaning 'that might leave (him) behind' or 'from which he would be left behind'. بپیچد is a present-subjunctive/form with the prefix ب- 'to turn away/withdraw' (بپیچد = 'he turns away' / 'he would turn away'). ز is the preposition 'out of / from' and خشم is the noun 'anger', so the clause says he would turn away from it in anger. The sequence shows cause/result: if something would leave him behind, he turns away angrily.
segments.s653-g935: بپرسید here is a past/plural narrative form 'they asked' (classical past narrative—context decides person/number). پس is 'then/after that'. موبد موبدان is a title built by genitive apposition 'mōbed of mōbeds' meaning 'chief of priests' (the head priest). The verb precedes the indirect object/title in typical narrative word order.
segments.s653-g936: که introduces an indirect question 'whether/that'. این is the demonstrative subject 'this'. برتر از is a comparative construction 'superior to / better than' followed by دانش بخردان, where دانش 'knowledge' is governed by بخردان ('of the wise') — بخردان is the plural/genitive use of بخرد (the wise ones). So the subordinate clause asks if 'this' is superior to the knowledge of the wise.
segments.s653-g817: دگر کو is a rhetorical interrogative phrase: دگر (literally 'other') + کو ('where / where is') asks 'where else' or 'where now'. بشوید here is a present/subjunctive/passive-like verb form ('be washed / become cleansed') used with زبان ('tongue / speech') as its patient; از introduces the source ('from/away from') and دروغ is the object 'falsehood / lie'. The verb is in a poetic register and the clause literally says 'where else will the tongue be washed from falsehood'.
segments.s653-g818: نجوید is the present-tense verb 'جوید' with the negative prefix ن- (poetically written together), meaning 'does not seek'. The clause places the verb before its logical subject (ز گیتی فروغ = 'the radiance of the world') for poetic word order: 'the world's radiance does not seek crookedness' (نجوید به کژی = 'does not seek toward crookedness').
segments.s653-g819: This is a temporal clause introduced by چو ('when'). سپهبد is the subject ('the spahbed / commander'). با + nouns (با داد و بخشایش) describes the qualities 'with justice and mercy' — بخشایشست is the fused verblike predicate ('is mercy / is merciful' or 'has mercy'); the clause means 'when the spahbed is [characterized] by justice and mercy'.
segments.s653-g820: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from / because of'; تاجش ('his crown') is the cause or instrument, and زمانه ('the age / era') is the subject being described. پرآسایشست is a predicate adjective 'is full of ease / peace' (پر- prefix = 'full of'). The clause reads 'because of his crown the age is full of tranquility.'
segments.s653-g821: This is a nominal/relative fragment: و دیگر ('and another / another one') followed by a relative clause introduced by که ('who'). از کهتر literally 'of the lower' = 'from among the lowly / of lower rank', and پرگناه modifies that subject as 'full of sin / sinful'. The phrase describes 'another person who is lowly and sinful.'
segments.s653-g822: چو ('when') introduces a subordinate clause: پوزش کند = 'he makes/asks pardon' (پوزش 'pardon; excuse', کند 'does/makes'). In the main clause باز بخشدش شاه the verb بخشدش has the enclitic pronoun -ش ('him') attached and means 'the king forgives him'; باز ('again') modifies the verb. Word order (temporal clause → main clause) is typical of Persian poetic style.
segments.s653-g823: به پنجم here marks an ordinal ('as/for the fifth'). جهاندار 'ruler/sovereign of the world' is the noun being enumerated and نیکوسخن is a compound adjective 'good-speaking' = 'eloquent'. The phrase names the fifth item/person: 'the world-ruler who is eloquent.'
segments.s653-g824: که is the relative pronoun 'whose/that'. نامش is name + possessive clitic ('his name'). نگردد is the present tense with negative prefix ن- plus گرد(دن) 'become/turn', meaning 'does not become / will not be changed' (poetically: 'will not fade or be altered'). به گیتی کهن is a locational/poetic phrase 'in the ancient world' (به used poetically for location). Together: 'whose name will not be changed in the old world.'
segments.s653-g25: بگفت is a past-tense reportive verb ('he said') introducing reported speech. The clause inverts the normal word order for emphasis: کجا دید (‘where did [he] see’) is a question-like embedded clause, and در خواب شاه ('in the king's dream') is a locative modifier placed after the verb. آن here functions as a demonstrative ('that') pointing to the thing just mentioned.
segments.s653-g26: بدان is best read as a dative/locative idea ('to that / to those') — an archaic contraction (به + آن) marking the indirect object; the verb that would normally govern this dative is omitted (elliptical imperative 'send' or 'show'). موبدان is plural 'mōbeds' (Zoroastrian priests) and نماینده راه is a noun phrase 'one who shows the way' (i.e. a guide). Together the phrase functions as an instruction: '(send) a guide to those mobeds.'
segments.s653-g27: گزارنده خواب is a nominal phrase 'the narrator / reporter of the dream' (گزارنده as agent noun with خواب as its object). پاسخ نداد is a simple past negated clause 'gave no answer' (نداد = did not give/offer). The verb is straightforward past tense and negated by the negative lexical verb نداد.
segments.s653-g28: کزان (from + آن) introduces a consequence or source 'from which / of that'. دانش او را places 'his knowledge' (دانش) before the object-marking را for emphasis; نبد is an archaic negative copula meaning 'there was not / did not exist'. هیچ یاد is the noun phrase 'any trace/memory' placed after the copula. Overall: 'from that there was no trace of his knowledge.'
segments.s653-g29: به نادانی here means 'by/through ignorance' (به as a causal preposition). آنکس که خستو شود is a relative clause 'that person who becomes weary' with که introducing the relative and شود (present/optative/poetic form of شدن) meaning 'becomes/is made'. The clause attributes the following action to the person's ignorance.
segments.s653-g30: ز is the poetic/genitive preposition 'because of / from'. فام نکوهنده is a compound notion 'a reproachful hue/expression' (فام = hue/appearance, نکوهنده = reproachful/censuring). یک‌سو شود is a verbal phrase 'becomes one-sided / turns aside' where یک‌سو is adverbial ('to one side') and شود is the verbal copula 'becomes/turns'.
segments.s653-g31: ز داننده ('from the knower') places the source before the clause; چون here functions as 'since/because' introducing the reason clause. شاه is the logical subject, پاسخ is the object 'answer', and نیافت is the past negative of یافتن 'did not obtain / did not get'. The clause means 'since the king received no answer from the expert.'
segments.s653-g32: پراندیشه is a compound adjective 'full of thought / anxious'; دل را uses را to mark 'the heart' as the affected/grammatical object. سوی چاره is a directional prepositional phrase 'toward a remedy/solution'. تافت is an archaic/poetic verb here meaning 'turned / was directed' (poetic usage); its literal senses given in the lexicon ('shone', 'scorched') do not match the contextual meaning, so the precise lexical sense supplied in the list does not correspond.
segments.s653-g233: This is an imperative negative clause: the verb مخور is the negative imperative of خوردن ('do not eat/take'), here used idiomatically 'do not take/seek'. The noun phrase 'هنرجوی و تیمار بیشی' lists two objects joined by و: 'هنرجوی' (pursuit/one who seeks art/skill) and 'تیمار بیشی' ('excessive care' — تیمار modified by بیشی). Word order is noun(s) + imperative, with بیشی postmodifying تیمار to give the sense 'too much/excessive care.'
segments.s653-g234: که introduces the causal clause ('for/because'), followed by the statement about the world. گیتی is the subject 'the world'; سپنجست is a poetic adjective meaning 'uncertain/obscure' attached to گیتی; و connects the following clause 'ما بر گذر' where ما is the subject 'we', بر + گذر ('upon the passage' / 'passing') yields the adverbial sense 'we are passing through it' (transience). The sequence gives cause → explanation.
segments.s653-g235: Nominal sentence with a possessed noun phrase 'همه روشنیهای تو' ('all the lights/brightnesses of you' — روشنیهای + possessive تو) followed by the predicate راستیست, a fused copula 'is truth' (راستی + است contracted). The order is topic (possessed noun) → predicate.
segments.s653-g236: ز introduces the cause/reason ('because of' / 'from'); تاری و کژی are two coordinated nouns 'darkness and crookedness/wrongness' acting as the source of lament. بباید is the impersonal/modal 'one must / it is necessary' and is followed by the verb گریست (archaic form for 'to weep' / 'lament'). The modal precedes the full verb, a common classical order.
segments.s653-g237: 'دل هرکسی' is a genitive construction with the possessor after the possessed noun ('the heart of everyone'). The predicate 'بنده آرزوست' is a nominal predicate: بنده 'a slave/servant' governed by آرزو with the copula -ست attached to آرزو. The whole means 'the heart of every person is enslaved by desire.'
segments.s653-g238: This clause links back to the previous with وزو (here a classical connective meaning 'from that / of it') and states a generalization: 'هر یکی را' ('for each one' with را marking the affected person) + 'دگرگونه خوست' where خوست (archaic form related to 'خاستن'/'خاست') means 'is pleasing/desired' and دگرگونه 'differently/other-wise.' The construction expresses that each person has a different desire.
segments.s653-g239: A nominal identification: 'سر راستی' ('the head/source of truth') as subject, followed by 'دانش ایزدست' where دانش is 'knowledge' and ایزدست is an archaic predicate meaning 'is (from) God' or 'belongs to God.' Classical Persian sometimes fuses copula/predicative expressions (ایزدست here functions to ascribe divine origin to the knowledge).
segments.s653-g240: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'; دانستیش is an archaic verbal form meaning 'you know / you have known it' (2nd-person sense). The following items (ز + و combined as the classical 'زو' = 'of it / from it') mark the object/source, and نترسی is the negative 2nd-person present/future 'you will not fear.' بدست here is adverbial 'by/through it' (i.e., 'you will not be afraid because of it' or 'you will not fear it'). The clause therefore gives the result: once you know it, you will not fear.
segments.s653-g1041: چو here is the temporal subordinating conjunction 'when'. با + این + هنرها is a prepositional phrase 'with these arts/skills' (با before the demonstrative and noun). شود is a classical/neutral present/future-ish verb meaning 'becomes/arrives/happens' (here used like 'when he comes' or 'when he is') and نزد شاه places that action 'in the presence of the king' (نزد = 'in the presence of'). Word order is typical older Persian: temporal 'چو' + circumstantial phrase + verb + locus.
segments.s653-g1042: نشاید is an impersonal/evocative predicative meaning 'it is not fitting/appropriate'. نشستن is the infinitive 'to sit' functioning as the subject/complement of نشاید. مگر = 'except' introduces the sole allowable circumstance; پیش + گاه form the compound 'پیشگاه' meaning 'the royal presence/threshold' (here split into two words). The clause thus gives the prohibition and its single exception.
segments.s653-g1043: This is a temporal clause with inversion: سخنها 'the words' is the topic, چوبشنید is an archaic/poetic fused form meaning 'when (he) heard' or 'on hearing' (چو + بشنید contracted). از + و + شهریار places the agent/subject after the verb in classical order — literally 'when (he) heard the words from, then the king' — i.e. 'when the king heard the words.'
segments.s653-g1044: دلش is 'his heart' (دل + enclitic pronoun ش). تازه شد is a simple past/perfect-like result 'became fresh' (شد as a past-resultive). چون introduces a simile 'like/as', گل 'a flower', اندر بهار 'in spring' — standard simile structure comparing the renewed heart to a flower in spring.
segments.s653-g1045: چنین 'thus/so' sets the manner. گفت here functions as the main verb 'said' (the senses list given is not the lexical 'say', so no exact sense-match). کسری is the speaker (Khosrow); به موبد = 'to the mobad' (to the priest). که introduces reported speech or an object clause (i.e. what he told the mobad to do). Note the classical word order Subject–Verb–indirect-object is compacted in poetic form.
segments.s653-g1046: ورا = 'to him' (ولازمِ فعلِ بعدی: the recipient). پایگاهی is a noun meaning 'a base/place/platform' or figuratively 'rank/status' and acts as the object. بیارای here is an infinitival/imperative-like form meaning 'prepare/array/adorn' (in context an instruction to furnish or assign a place); the provided sense entries do not capture this verbal use, so sense is left unspecified. نو 'new' modifies پایگاهی. Together it is an imperative/command: 'provide him a new platform/place/rank.'
segments.s653-g1047: درم خواه is an imperative-like construction: درم 'a dirham (coin/money)' followed by خواه (the verb 'want/ask'), i.e. 'ask for money'. و is the connective 'and'. خلعت 'robe (of honour)' is the second object; سزاوار اوی is a postpositive adjective phrase 'fitting for him' (سزاوار 'deserving/appropriate' + اوی 'his/to him'). Word order places the request first then the specification of the reward.
segments.s653-g1048: که here gives the reason 'for/because'. در دل 'in the heart' is a locative phrase; نشسته‌ست is a perfect/resultative 'has settled/has taken seat' describing a lasting impression. گفتار اوی 'his speech/utterance' is the subject of نشسته‌ست (literally 'his speech has settled in the heart'), providing the justification for the requested reward.
segments.s653-g545: فروتر بود is a predicative adjective + past copula: فروتر (comparative 'lower/more humble') followed by بود ('was') gives 'was more humble'. هرک دارد خرد is a relative/generalizing clause: هرک ('whoever') + دارد خرد ('has wisdom' — دارد is a present/habitual possession here). The clause modifies the subject of the predicate, so literally 'Lower was whoever has wisdom.'
segments.s653-g546: سپهرش (سپهر + ـش) is the subject ('his/that person's heaven(s)' or 'the heavens for him'), همی is the habitual adverb 'ever/still', درخرد is adjectival/adverbial 'in wisdom / wise', and پرورد is an older poetic perfect/past-form of 'to nurture/raise' (here 'nurtured' or 'kept nurturing'). Word order (subject — adverb — manner — verb) is typical of classical Persian poetry.
segments.s653-g547: چنین هم ('thus also') is a linking adverbial; بود is the past copula 'were'; مردم is the subject 'the people'; شاد دل is a postpositive adjective phrase ('merry‑hearted', literally 'happy of heart') describing مردم. Classical Persian often places descriptive adjectives after the noun.
segments.s653-g548: ز introduces an ablative/causal phrase 'because of / from'; کژیش is کژی + ـش ('his/its crookedness' or 'his perversity') forming the cause. خون گردد is the verb phrase 'blood becomes / is shed' (گردد is the present/subjunctive/poetic form of 'become'/'happen'), and آزاد دل ('free/brave‑hearted') is the affected party (the brave‑hearted); the order is inverted for poetic effect (cause — result — patient).
segments.s653-g549: خرد is the subject 'wisdom'; درجهان here functions adverbially ('in the world' — classical spelling fused) and so locates the subject; چون is the comparative/simile 'like'; درخت وفاست is the simile-predicate 'is a tree of fidelity' where وفا + است is contracted in poetry to وفاست. The clause states a general simile.
segments.s653-g550: وزو is an archaic/poetic form meaning 'from it / of it' (here 'from the tree'); بار جستن is a verbal noun + infinitive pattern where بار ('fruit/load') is the object and جستن ('to seek') is the action 'seeking fruit'; دل پادشاست is a nominal clause: دل ('the heart') + پادشاست (poetic/condensed predicate meaning 'is kingly / belongs to a king / is king‑like'), i.e. 'the kingly heart'. The whole line: 'from it the kingly heart seeks fruit.'
segments.s653-g551: چو is the conditional/temporal 'when/if'; چوخرسند باشی is a protasis with باشی in the subjunctive/present-tense sense 'if you are content' (classical form addressing the addressee). تن (your body) is the experiential/patient noun and آسان شوی ('become easy/at ease') is the apodosis: شوی is the present/subjunctive of 'become' addressing the same person. In classical Persian the verb may agree with the implied person even when a noun like تن appears.
segments.s653-g552: چو is again 'when/if'; آز آوری is 'you bring/produce greed' (آوری is 2nd‑person present/imperative form of آوردن used here in a general conditional sense). ز + و together function as the archaic/poetic 'از او' ('because of it / by it'), and هراسان شوی is the apodosis 'you become terrified' (شوی again the present/subjunctive/optative form). The sequence gives a cause–effect: bring greed and you will be frightened by it.
segments.s653-g1033: ز + noun (ز لفظ) marks selection 'from/among the words'. آن is the demonstrative 'those/that'. گزیند is 3rd-person present tense 'he chooses' (classical present). که introduces a relative clause modifying لفظ: کوتاه‌تر is the comparative adjective 'shorter/briefer' describing the words chosen.
segments.s653-g1034: بخط آن نماید is a verbal phrase meaning 'he renders/writes in that script/hand' (بخط = 'in the script/hand', نماید = 3rd sg. present 'he makes/shows/does'). The following که introduces another relative clause (دلخواه‌تر) describing the script: 'which is more to his liking.'
segments.s653-g1035: خردمند is the subject 'the wise man'. باید که باشد is a necessity construction: باید ('ought/it is necessary') + که + باشد (subjunctive/present 'be'), so 'a wise man should be'. دبیر is the predicate noun 'a scribe/secretary' (role he should hold).
segments.s653-g1036: همان here functions as a clarifying demonstrative 'namely/that (one)', linking the predicate to the following descriptive attributes. بردبار and سخن یادگیر are coordinate descriptive nouns/adjectives: 'patient' (بردبار) and 'a learner of speech/words' (سخن یادگیر).
segments.s653-g1037: Two attributive adjectives precede the noun: هشیوار 'alert' and سازیده (a past-participial adjective) 'well-made/composed', followed by پادشا 'king' (archaic spelling). This is a poetic noun phrase meaning 'an alert, composed king.'
segments.s653-g1038: This is a compact clause: زبان خامش = 'a silent tongue' (زبان + adjective خامش). از بد = 'from/of evil' (expressing abstention from evil talk). به تن پارسا = 'toward the person/body of the pious' (به + تن + پارسا), i.e. 'do not speak ill of the pious.' Note the elliptic/poetic style omits a copula or imperative verb — the sense is prescriptive.
segments.s653-g1039: A series of coordinated adjectival phrases describing the subject: شکیبا 'patient'; با دانش is a prepositional adjective 'with knowledge/learned'; راست‌گوی is a compound adjective 'straight‑speaking/truthful'. The conjunctions ו link the attributes.
segments.s653-g1040: Continuation of the attribute list: وفادار 'faithful', پاکیزه 'pure/clean', and تازه‌روی (a descriptive compound) 'fresh-faced/novice' — all adjectives coordinated by و. In context these are qualities expected of the scribe/king.
segments.s653-g753: A prepositional/infinitival clause: ازو (from him) + دور گشتن (to turn/become distant). The phrase به رغم هوا (with به + noun رغم + هوا) is a fixed prepositional phrase meaning “despite (one's) desire”; گشتن is the infinitive/verb at the end of the clause.
segments.s653-g754: خرد را is the noun 'reason/wisdom' marked by the definite/object marker را. بران (thereon/thereupon) modifies رای کردن (رای + کردن = ‘to give an opinion/advise’ — here an infinitive). گوا is an adverbial/parenthetical classical particle meaning 'seemingly/apparently' or 'as if'.
segments.s653-g755: فزودن is an infinitive meaning 'to increase/add'. It takes a prepositional complement with به marking the target (فرزند). برمهر خویش is a descriptive phrase modifying فرزند: برمهر 'beloved/bound by love' + خویش (خوی + ش) ‘his own’ (possessive suffix).
segments.s653-g756: چو introduces a simile 'as/when'. در آب دیدن is a light clause 'to see in the water' (دیدن infinitive inside the prepositional phrase). بود is the past auxiliary used here to form the comparison ('as when it was to see…'). چهر خویش = 'one's own face' (چهر 'face' + خویش reflexive/possessive).
segments.s653-g757: A purposive/source phrase: ز (from) فرهنگ (culture) وز دانش — here وز is a classical/poetic connector used with دانش (intended as 'and/and from knowledge'), followed by آموختن (the infinitive 'to learn'). The whole phrase means 'to learn from culture and knowledge.'
segments.s653-g758: سزد‌گر is a classical conditional/modal formula meaning 'if it is fitting' (literally 'if fitting occurs'); دلت is دل + ت (your heart). یابد is a present/subjunctive-type verb 'finds/attains' here introducing the subordinate infinitive افروختن ('to kindle/awaken'). The clause reads 'if your heart finds it fitting to kindle (it)'.
segments.s653-g759: A verbal-nominal phrase: گشادن (to open) + برو (go) forms an action cluster 'to go and open' or 'to open (by) going to'. بر در (upon/to the door) specifies location, گنج خویش is the genitive 'his treasure' (گنج 'treasure, vault' + خویش reflexive/possessive).
segments.s653-g760: نباید is the impersonal modal 'one must not/should not'. که introduces the subordinate clause whose verb is یادآورد 'would bring to mind/recall'. رنج خویش is the object 'his suffering' (رنج 'suffering' + خویش 'his own'). The clause gives the reason: 'one should not ... because it will recall his own suffering.'
segments.s653-g513: ازو is the fused prepositional pronoun (از + او) meaning 'of/from him' placed before the nominal phrase; نامداران is a plural attributive noun 'the renowned/renowned ones'; فروماندند is a 3rd-person plural past-tense verb (فروماندن) meaning 'were left/ remained' — together the phrase states that, with respect to him, the renowned ones were left/abandoned.
segments.s653-g514: همه is the quantifier 'all/everyone' modifying همزبان ('those of the same tongue' or 'fellow-speakers'); آفرین is here an exclamation/acclamation ('Afarin' = 'bravo') and precedes the verb خواندند, a 3rd-person plural past 'they called/uttered' — the idiom آفرین خواندند means 'they exclaimed “Afarin.”'
segments.s653-g515: چو is the conjunction 'when'; یک هفته is the temporal phrase 'one week'; بگذشت is the past verb 'passed' (time passed); هشتم پگاه is a temporal adverbial 'the eighth dawn/morning' following the clause to specify the exact day/time.
segments.s653-g516: نشست is the 3rd-person past 'he sat / took his seat'; از بر تخت is a poetic/location phrase equivalent to 'upon/on the throne' (the older double preposition از بر indicates location on); پیروز is an epithet 'victorious' modifying شاه 'king' (noun+epithet order is common in classical Persian).
segments.s653-g517: بخواند is a 3rd-person past form of خواندن here meaning 'he summoned/called'; آنکسی را که is a fused demonstrative+relative phrase 'that person whom'; دانا بدند is a description attributed by others — دانا 'wise' plus بدند (3rd-plural past of بودن) functioning like 'they considered/held (him) to be' — together: 'the person whom they considered wise.'
segments.s653-g518: به introduces the sphere 'in/regarding' (here 'in speech/learning'); گفتار و دانْش is the coordinate phrase 'speech and knowledge' (ودانش is the written fusion of و + دانش); توانا is the adjectival predicate 'capable/able'; بدند (the same 3rd-plural past of بودن) ties back to 'they judged him' — literal order: 'in speech and knowledge capable they were' = 'they deemed him capable in speech and learning.'
segments.s653-g519: بگفتند is the reporting verb 'they said'; هرگونه‌ای is an adverbial/qualifier 'in any manner/whatever kind'; هرکسی is an indefinite pronoun 'whoever/anyone' — together they introduce reported speech with an open/indefinite scope ('they said, in any way, whoever').
segments.s653-g520: همانا is a discourse adverb 'verily/truly' used to emphasize the statement; پسندش is the nominalized verb 'his liking/it pleasing him' (پسند + ضمیر ـش); نیامد is the past negative 'did not come' idiomatically meaning 'did not please'; بسی is an adverbial intensifier 'much/very' — together: 'indeed it did not please him much.'
segments.s653-g649: دگر گفت is a short clause meaning 'another said' (دگر = 'another / other', گفت here is the past 'said'); کزبار is a literary prepositional/time phrase 'from the time that / since' introducing the following noun phrase; آن میوه‌دار is a demonstrative + adjective-noun phrase 'that fruit-bearing [tree]' (میوه‌دار functioning as a noun/adjectival noun). Word order is typical literary Persian: temporal marker (کزبار) precedes the phrase it scopes over.
segments.s653-g650: که introduces a relative clause 'which / that'; دانا is the agent-adjective 'the wise (one)'; بکارد is the literary present/subjunctive form of کاشتن with the prefix ب- ('he plants / may plant') and here functions as the verb of the relative clause; به باغ بهار is a locative/directional prepositional phrase 'into / in the garden of spring' (باغ بهار = 'spring garden').
segments.s653-g651: چه سازیم is a rhetorical interrogative 'what shall we do / what can we do' (سازیم is present-subjunctive/optative of ساختن used here as 'do'); تاهرکسی ('تا هر کسی' contracted) gives the scope 'whenever / whenever we meet anyone'; برخوریم (برخوریم) is the verb 'we encounter / we happen upon', in present/subjunctive form — together: 'what shall we do if/when we encounter anyone.'
segments.s653-g652: وگر is a conditional 'but if / and if'; سایه او literally 'his shadow' but idiomatically 'his protection/shelter'; به پی here is a prepositional phrase 'to / into the following/care of' (به + پی 'following/pursuit' used to indicate being put into someone's charge); بسپریم is first-person-plural present-subjunctive of سپردن 'we entrust/commit (ourselves)'. The whole gives the conditional 'and if we entrust ourselves to his protection.'
segments.s653-g653: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed phrase 'he gave such an answer' (چنین 'thus', داد = past 'gave', پاسخ 'answer'). که begins the reported clause 'that / whoever ...'; هر کو is the literary indefinite 'whoever' (هر = 'each/any', کو here functions as a relative/indefinite marker in older usage); زبان is the noun 'tongue / speech' (object/subject of the next predicate).
segments.s653-g654: ز بد بسته دارد is a compact predication: ز (poetic 'from / against') + بد 'evil' + بسته (past-participle/adjectival 'bound, shut') + دارد 'he has/keeps' — i.e. 'keeps (his tongue) shut from evil'. نرنجد is a negative present/subjunctive (ن- prefix + رنجد) meaning 'will not be pained / will not be hurt'; روان 'soul, spirit' is the patient/undergoer. Note the poetic word order places the predicate before روان.
segments.s653-g655: کسی را is the affected person marked by the object marker را ('anyone'); ندرد is the negated verb (ن- + درد from بریدن/دریدن 'to tear') 'does not tear'; به گفتار is an instrumental/causal phrase 'by speech / with words'; پوست 'skin' is the tangible object referenced — the line means idiomatically 'words do not tear a person's skin' (i.e. words cause no physical harm). The order is topical/poetic rather than strictly SOV.
segments.s653-g656: بود is the past-tense copula 'there was / was'; بر دل is the locative 'on / in the heart'; انجمن is the noun 'assembly / gathering / company' serving as a predicate or existential noun 'an assembly (was) in the heart'; نیز دوست adds 'and also a friend' (نیز = 'also', دوست = 'friend'), a typical poetic inversion placing complements around the verb. The clause says that inwardly there was fellowship and also a friend.
segments.s653-g1: نگر here functions as an interjection/imperative ('note' or 'behold') introducing an admonition; خواب را uses the object marker را to mark 'the dream' as definite; بیهده is a predicative adjective 'futile/idle'; نشمری is a negative jussive/subjunctive/imperative form of شمردن ('to count/consider'), so the clause means 'do not count/consider the dream futile.'
segments.s653-g2: یکی 'one/someone' introduces an indefinite person; بهره is 'a share/portion'; دانی here functions as a present/jussive verb ('has/knows/obtains' in this line's sense of acquiring or possessing knowledge), and ز پیغمبری means 'from prophethood' (ز = from). Together: 'someone has/obtains a share from prophethood.'
segments.s653-g3: به ویژه که is an idiomatic connective meaning 'especially since/that'; شاه جهان is a compound title 'king of the world' (شاه + جهان); بیندش is a classical present/subjunctive/indicative form of دیدن with the enclitic object -ش ('sees him/it'); the clause functions as the subordinate reason/condition introduced by که.
segments.s653-g4: روان درخشنده is a noun phrase 'the radiant/shining soul'; بگزیندش is the classical present/future-ish form of گزیدن with the enclitic object -ش, meaning 'he selects/chooses him/it' (the verb is prefixed with ب- as a classical present marker). The object pronoun attaches to the verb.
segments.s653-g5: ستاره is the subject 'the star'; زند here is a poetic/present verb form (from زدن/زَنَد) meaning 'casts/utters/sets forth' and is used with رای 'counsel/opinion' as its object; با چرخ و ماه is an instrumental/associative phrase 'with the sky/heaven and the moon.' Word order is subject–verb–object with an adjunct.
segments.s653-g6: سخنها (plural 'words/messages') is the object of the second verb; پراگنده کرده is a compound verbal phrase meaning 'has scattered' (پراگنده 'scattered' + کرده 'has made/done'); به راه is an adverbial phrase 'along the way/on the road.' The earlier subject (the star) is understood to govern this verb as well.
segments.s653-g7: روانهای روشن is a plural noun phrase 'bright/enlightened souls'; ببیند is a classical present-subjunctive/indicative form of دیدن used here for 'see' (the verb agrees in sense with the plural subject in context though forms vary in classical usage); به خواب means 'in sleep/in a dream' and marks the modality in which they see.
segments.s653-g8: همه بودنیها is 'all beings/that which exists' (همه = all; بودنیها = existents); چوآتش is a simile 'like fire' (چو = like/as + آتش = fire); برآب literally 'on water' gives the image 'like fire on water,' an idiom for transience/ephemerality. The clause is the content of what the souls see.
segments.s653-g33: فرستاد is a simple past, 3rd-person singular 'he sent.' The phrase بر هر سویی is a prepositional phrase 'toward every direction' (بر + سویی), and مهتری is the object 'chief/elder' placed after the directional phrase — a common poetic word order where the verb precedes its object phrase.
segments.s653-g34: که تا begins a purpose/relative clause: که = 'that'/introducer and تا = 'so that, in order that' (introducing the intended action). باز جوید is a present-subjunctive-type sense 'may seek/find out' (جوید = 'to seek'); ز هر کشوری = 'from every country/realm' (ز = poetic 'from').
segments.s653-g35: یکی بدره = 'one/a certain full‑moon (i.e. a beauty)' (یکی used distributively/indefinitely). با هر یکی یار کرد is 'he made a companion for each one' (با marks accompaniment/association; کرد is the past 'made/appointed'). The overall order (noun phrases before the verb) is typical poetic syntax.
segments.s653-g36: به برگشتن امید بسیار کرد is an idiom meaning 'he put/placed much hope on the returning.' به marks the target/aim ('for/on'), برگشتن is the infinitive/gerund 'return,' امید is 'hope,' بسیار is an adverbial intensifier 'greatly/much,' and کرد is the past verb 'he made/placed.'
segments.s653-g37: به هر بدره‌ای = 'to/for each beauty' (به marks beneficiary; -ای on بدره makes it indefinite). درم ده هزار is the quantity 'ten thousand dirhams.' The short phrase is a beneficiary/amount expression; the main giving-verb is understood from the narrative context (elliptical in poetic style). The item 'بد' here is obscure/archaic and does not match the usual senses in the lemma list.
segments.s653-g38: بدان تاکند is a purpose/result clause: the prefix ب- (here functioning as be-/to‑) plus دان ('consider/ make') combine with تاکند (a subjunctive/purpose form 'that they may') to express 'so that they may be regarded.' در جهان خواستار = 'desired/sought after in the world' (در = 'in', جهان = 'world', خواستار = 'desirous/sought'). تاکند is a subjunctive used to state the intended result.
segments.s653-g39: گزارنده خواب is a compound noun 'reporter/narrator of dreams' = 'dream‑interpreter.' دانا is an adjective 'wise/learned' modifying the noun, and کسی ('someone') is used appositionally/indefinitely to introduce the person. Word order places modifiers after the head in this poetic register.
segments.s653-g40: به هر دانشی راه جسته بسی: به marks 'in/regarding,' هر دانشی = 'every kind of knowledge/learning,' راه جسته is a perfect participle phrase 'having sought/found a way' (جسته from جستن), and بسی is an adverbial intensifier 'much/abundantly' or 'sufficiently.' Together it says he had pursued a method/path through every branch of learning.
segments.s653-g1009: This clause begins with the noun 'پرستش' (worship) as the object/topic, followed by the prepositional/causal marker 'ز' (on account of/because of). 'خورد' here is the adjective 'small' (an older poetic use), modified by 'ایچ' 'not at all' (intensifier), and the negative imperative 'مکن' closes the clause: literally 'do not make (it) less because of small (things) at all.' Word order is topic → cause → qualifier → negative imperative.
segments.s653-g1010: 'تو نو باش' is an imperative addressing 'تو' (you) with 'نو' (new/fresh) and the verb 'باش' (be!—imperative). The next phrase 'گرهست گیتی کهن' is predicative: 'گرهست' (is a knot) + 'گیتی' (the world) + 'کهن' (ancient). The clauses are linked by cause/reason: 'be new, for the world is an ancient knot.'
segments.s653-g1011: 'به نیکی گرای' uses 'به' (towards) + 'نیکی' (goodness) with 'گرای' here conveying inclination/turning toward (a classical poetic formation meaning 'be inclined to' or 'turn to'). 'و غنیمت شناس' is a coordinated imperative/adjunct: 'غنیمت' (advantage, opportunity) + 'شناس' (knower/aware) — literally 'and know/recognize the opportunity.' The structure is two coordinated exhortations: 'Be inclined to goodness, and be mindful of the opportunity.'
segments.s653-g1012: 'همه ز آفریننده دار این سپاس' is a compact clause: 'همه' (all/everyone) + 'ز' (from/of) + 'آفریننده' (the Creator). 'دار' in classical poetry functions as 'holder/possessor' (here 'owe/hold'), followed by the demonstrative 'این' (this) and noun 'سپاس' (praise/thanks). The sense is 'all [should] hold/offer this praise to the Creator.' (If 'دار' is unfamiliar as a possessive verb-form in modern lists, it is a classical usage meaning 'to have/possess/offer'.)
segments.s653-g1013: 'مگرد' is a prohibitive/excluding particle (classically 'do not' / 'except'), here functioning as 'do not.' 'ایچ گونه' ('in no way / like this') strengthens the prohibition. 'به گرد بدی' literally 'to/around evil' uses 'به' (to/toward) + 'گرد' (around/about) + 'بدی' (evil). The whole segment is a negative admonition: 'Do not in any way go about with evil.'
segments.s653-g1014: This is a second admonition/appeal: 'به نیکی‌گر' (to the doer/one inclined to good) + the vocative 'ایی' (O!) addresses the addressee; 'اگر بخردی' is a conditional 'if you are wise' (اگر = if; 'بخردی' here denotes wisdom/prudence as a quality). Together: 'Turn to goodness, O doer of good, if you are wise.'
segments.s653-g1015: 'ستوده‌ترآنکس' is a fused poetic compound meaning 'that one more praised' (comparative adjective + demonstrative/person). 'بود' is the pastcopular 'was', and 'در جهان' places the scene 'in the world.' Word order is emphatic/predicate fronting typical of epic style: 'More praised was that one in the world.'
segments.s653-g1016: 'که' introduces a relative clause 'who/that', 'نیکش' = 'his goodness' (from 'نیک' = good/virtuous), 'بود' again is 'was', and 'آشکار و نهان' are paired adverbial/adjectival complements 'openly and hiddenly' (i.e., 'in public and in private'). So: 'who was good both openly and secretly.'
segments.s653-g185: هرانگه که is a temporal conjunction meaning 'when/whenever' (هرانگه + که). پردخته here is an adjective meaning 'well‑formed, accomplished', and گشتی follows it as a verbal form meaning 'you became / were made' or a poetic perfective of گشتن — together 'پردخته گشتی' = 'you became accomplished'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of' and کار is the noun 'work/deed'; the phrase links the state of being accomplished to the domain ('in your work').
segments.s653-g186: The initial ز marks a list of qualities or sources 'from'. داد is a noun 'justice', و is the coordinating 'and', دهش is a noun referring to 'giving/munificence' (an act or quality). The following وز here functions idiomatically as a poetic sequence marker (equivalent to و ز 'and from'), introducing می‌و میگسار — می‌و (an old/formal form related to 'fruit' or 'wine') and میگسار 'wine‑companion / cupbearer'.
