segments.s653-g383: چنین گفت is a formula 'thus he said.' کان کو is a classical/poetic relative phrase equivalent to 'he who' or 'one who' (کان and کو working together as an archaic relative); پس آرزوی literally 'after (his) desire/wish' sets the temporal/conditional frame for the relative clause. Grammatically this opens a reported clause introduced by 'he who...'.
segments.s653-g384: نرفت is the past negative 'did not go/turn away' (he did not abandon); از is the preposition 'from' marking separation; کریمی is a noun/adjective 'generosity' or 'the generous one' and نیک خوی is the compound 'good disposition/good nature' (وز here appears as a connective in the classical line linking the two nouns). The clause reads 'did not turn away from generosity and good nature.'
segments.s653-g129: This is a compact noun phrase: سمن (jasmine) + بوی (scent) forms 'jasmine-scent'; خوبان is a genitival/attributive noun 'the beauties/beloveds' modifying the scent. با ناز و شرم is an adverbial phrase ('with coquetry and modesty') specifying the manner/attitude. Word order is typical poetic Persian: successive nouns build a compound image, then a 'با' phrase gives manner.
segments.s653-g130: همه is the subject 'all/everyone'; پیش کسری is a prepositional phrase 'in the presence of / before Khosrow'; برفتند is a past 3rd-person plural verb 'they went/advanced' (poetic prefix بر- is attached); نرم is an adverb 'gently/softly' placed after the verb for emphasis.
segments.s653-g131: ندیدند is the negated past 'they did not see' (ن- + دیدند). ازین سان is an adverbial phrase 'of this sort / in this manner' (از + این/ین + سان). کسی is the indefinite 'anyone/someone' and در میان means 'in the midst / among' — together yielding 'they saw no one like this among (them)'.
segments.s653-g132: برآشفت is a past intransitive verb meaning 'he became angry / was enraged' (the prefix بر- is common in classical/poetic forms). کسری is the subject 'Khosrow'; چو is the comparative 'as/like'; شیر ژیان is a simile 'a living (or life-like) lion' — together: 'Khosrow grew angry like a living lion.'
segments.s653-g133: گزارنده is the speaker 'the narrator/reporter'. گفت here functions as 'said' (past), introducing reported speech. این نه اندر خورست is an idiomatic clause: این نه = 'this is not', اندر خورست (literally 'is among edible') functions idiomatically as 'is not fit to be eaten / not for eating' — اندر + خورست is an archaic copular/locative construction.
segments.s653-g134: غلامی is the noun 'a youth/servant/slave' (here indicating a boy among the women); میان زنان is a locative phrase 'in the midst of the women / among the women'; اندرست is an archaic present/predicative form 'is within / is among' (copular/locative). The phrase states that such a youth is among the women.
segments.s653-g135: شمن گفت introduces speech: شمن 'the sorcerer/evil spirit' + گفت 'said'. رفتن is the infinitive 'to go' used as the object/goal of the command; بافزون is an adverb/adjective 'increased / more' and together بافزون کنید is an imperative (کنید = 2nd-plural/formal) meaning 'do more / let them go further'. The order places the verbal noun/infinitive before the imperative phrase.
segments.s653-g136: رخ از چادر is a prepositional/partitive phrase 'the face from the veil' (رخ = face; از = from; چادر = veil). شرم here qualifies the action as 'with modesty/shame' (an adverbial sense). بیرون کنید is an imperative 'bring out / put out' (بیرون + کنید), commanding that the face be brought out from the veil.
segments.s653-g313: 'هنرهاش' is the plural noun 'هنر' with the possessive suffix -ش ('his'), so 'his arts/skills.' 'گسترده' is a predicate adjective meaning 'wide/extensive' and 'اندرجهان' is an adverbial compound 'in the world.' The copula ('is/are') is omitted (typical in epic Persian), so the phrase reads 'his skills were widespread in the world.'
segments.s653-g314: This clause has subject 'همه' ('everyone/all') followed by the object 'راز او' ('his secret(s)') and the verb 'داشتن' ('to have'). The final fused form 'درنهان' corresponds to classical 'در نهان' = 'in secret/secretly' (an adverbial). Literally: 'all had his secrets in secret' → 'everyone kept his secrets hidden.'
segments.s653-g315: 'مشو' is the negative imperative 'do not become.' The phrase 'با گرامیش کردن' uses 'با' + verbal noun 'کردن' to mark the means ('by honoring him' — گرامی/گرام + enclitic ش = 'honoring him'). 'دلیر' is the predicate adjective 'brave.' So: 'Do not become/let yourself be made brave by honoring him.'
segments.s653-g316: 'کزآتش' is a fused poetic prepositional phrase (که + از) meaning 'from/because of the fire.' 'بترسد' is the present/subjunctive form of 'ترسیدن' ('to fear'); here it describes an attributed fear. 'دل نره شیر' is a genitive phrase 'the heart of the male lion' (نره شیر = male lion). The clause is relative/explanatory: 'for whose lion‑heart would fear the fire.'
segments.s653-g317: 'اگر' introduces a conditional 'if.' 'کوه فرمانش دارد' literally 'the mountain has his command'—i.e. 'the mountain obeys his command.' 'سبک' at the end is an adjective 'light/frivolous' that in the poem links with the following 'دلش' to form 'سبک‌دل' (light‑hearted/fickle); the copula is omitted as is common in poetry.
segments.s653-g318: 'دلش' = 'his heart' (دل + enclitic ش 'his'). 'خیره' here is a predicative adjective meaning 'dazed/astonished' or 'foolish' in context. 'خوانیم' is 1st‑person plural present ('we call/say') used proverbially to pronounce judgment, and 'مغزش تنک' is a verbless predication 'his brain (is) narrow' (تنک = narrow/small‑minded). The conjunction 'و' simply links the judgments.
segments.s653-g319: 'همه' ('all') is the general subject. The pattern 'X ز شاهست' uses the poetic preposition 'ز' ('from/of') plus 'شاهست' ('is of/comes from the king') to state origin: 'all bad is from the king.' The second half 'و نیکی زشاه' repeats the same elliptical structure: 'and goodness (is) from the king.' The verb/copula is left implicit in the second clause (elliptical).
segments.s653-g320: 'کزو' ('from whom/which') refers back to the king as source. 'بند' here means 'fetter/bond' and 'چاهست' asserts 'is a pit/abyss' (چاه = pit). The clause then adds 'و هم تاج و گاه' — 'and also crown and occasion/time' (تاج = crown; گاه = time/occasion, here implying rank/seat). The line contrasts that the king is the source both of harm (bonds, pits) and of honor (crown, authority).
segments.s653-g41: که introduces a relative/complement clause ('who/that') referring back to the dream-interpreter in the preceding line. بگزارد is a literary third-person singular present/future form (بـ + گذارد) meaning 'causes/puts/relates' (here: 'explains' or 'conveys'). این خواب شاه جهان is the noun phrase object: demonstrative + noun + possessor (this dream of the king of the world); Persian often omits the object marker را in poetry.
segments.s653-g42: نهفته is an adjective/participle ('the hidden' or 'what is hidden') used substantively. بر آرد is the compound verb (بر + آوردن) in third-person singular 'he brings forth/produces' — بر here is a preverb meaning 'forth/up'. ز بند نهان is a prepositional phrase 'from the secret fetter/bond' (ز = poetic از 'from', بند = 'bond/fetter', نهان = 'hidden').
segments.s653-g43: یکی = 'one' or 'a certain one' (indefinite person). بدره آگنده is a descriptive phrase 'full-moon–filled' i.e. 'radiant as the full moon' (بدره = full moon, آگنده = filled/radiant). او را دهند is verb phrase 'they give him' with او + object marker را and دهند (3rd pl. of دادن). In poetic narrative the present form denotes a past or narrative action.
segments.s653-g44: سپاسی is the noun 'praise/thanks' (object). به شاه جهان is the beneficiary/goal phrase 'to/ before the king of the world' (به = to/for, شاه جهان = 'king of the world'). برنهند is 3rd plural of برنهادن/برنهد 'place upon/bestow/bring before' — so the clause means 'they present/bestow praise before the king.'
segments.s653-g45: به هر سو is a prepositional phrase 'to every side/toward every direction' (به = to, هر = every, سو = side/direction). بشد is the literary past/narrative form of شدن ('it happened/there came/he went'); here it introduces the action 'there went/came' or 'was sent'. موبدی کاردان is a noun + adjective 'a skilled/learned mobad' (موبدی = mobed/priest, کاردان = skilled).
segments.s653-g46: سواری is 'riders/horsemen' (here plural, nominative/absolute). هشیوار is an adjective 'alert/alert‑minded', بسیار is a quantifier 'many' (here modifying the following attribute), and دان is adjectival/participial 'knowing/wise' (from دانستن) — together they describe 'many alert, knowledgeable riders.' Word order places modifiers after the noun, typical in Persian poetry.
segments.s653-g47: یکی از ... = 'one of ...' (یکی = one/a certain one; از = of/from). ردان is the group from which he comes (here 'riders'/'men of the retinue' in context), and نامش آزادسرو = 'his name (was) Azadsarv' (نامش = name + possessive). The structure is: 'one of the X, his name Azadsarv...' — a common appositive pattern in Persian.
segments.s653-g48: ز درگاه کسری = 'from the court of Khosrow' (ز = poetic از 'from', درگاه = court/royal threshold, کسری = Khosrow/Kisra). بیامد is the past/narrative form 'he came' (third-person singular), and به مرو = 'to Merv' (به = to, مرو = the city Merv).
segments.s653-g89: The prepositional span 'ز سر تا به پایش' = 'from head as far as his foot' sets the extent (literally 'from head to his feet'). 'ببویید' here names the emanation/odor (a verbal/nominal use of the verb 'to smell/emit scent') and is followed by the adverbial adjective 'سخت' = 'strongly/intensely.' Together this phrase says the smell ran over the whole body and became intense.
segments.s653-g90: 'شد' is the main perfective/copular verb 'became.' 'ازپیش او' = 'from in front of him' (az + pīsh + pronoun) indicates source/location. 'نرم' functions adverbially here ('gently/softly'), 'سوی' = 'toward,' and 'درخت' = 'the tree' is the goal. The clause therefore says that (the thing/snake) from before him gently went toward the tree; 'شد' links this result/state to the preceding description.
segments.s653-g91: 'چو' is the temporal conjunction 'when.' The nominal group 'مار سیه' = 'the black snake' is the subject, 'بر' is the preposition 'on/upon,' and 'سر دار' = 'the top/head of the tree' (here 'دار' = 'tree'). 'شد' is the past/copular 'became/was' used to indicate the snake's coming to rest on the tree: 'when the black snake came upon the tree.'
segments.s653-g92: 'سر کودک' literally 'the child's head' is a common metonymy for the child himself. 'از خواب' = 'out of sleep' (from sleep) and 'بیدار شد' = 'became awake' (i.e., 'woke up'). The verb 'شد' completes the predication in the simple past/resultative sense.
segments.s653-g93: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. آن اژدها is the noun phrase 'that dragon' (demonstrative + noun). شورش او places the possessed noun 'shourish (commotion/uproar)' before the verb; او is the 3rd-person pronoun 'his/its' referring back to someone mentioned earlier. شنید is a simple past/perfect verb 'heard'. Word order is conjunction → subject → object → verb, a normal classical Persian sequence.
segments.s653-g94: بران is an archaic/poetic particle meaning 'thereupon' or 'upon that'. شاخ is the subject 'horn' (here belonging to the dragon). باریک شد is a compound predication: باریک (adjective 'thin/slender') + شد (past of شدن 'to become') = 'became thin'. ناپدید is a predicate/adverb meaning 'vanished' or 'became unseen' that follows the verbal clause; together the clause states a sequence: 'its horn became thin and vanished.'
segments.s653-g95: فرستاده is the subject 'the messenger' (a noun meaning 'one sent' or 'envoy'). اندر شگفتی is a prepositional/adverbial phrase 'in astonishment' (اندر = in, شگفتی = astonishment). بماند is a past narrative use of the verb 'ماندن' (here translated 'remained' or 'stood'), a classical form where the present-stem with ب often functions as a past narrative.
segments.s653-g96: فراوان is an adverb 'much/repeatedly' modifying the following action. برو here is used in the sense 'go/went about' (he moved around or went repeatedly). نام یزدان بخواند is a verb phrase 'he invoked/called the name of God' — نام = 'name', یزدان = 'God', and بخواند (present-stem with ب) here denotes the past action 'called/uttered' (in classical narrative). The whole segment describes repeated movement and invocation.
segments.s653-g81: به زیر درختی is a prepositional phrase (به + زیر + درختی) meaning 'into/under a tree' with درختی indefinite (a tree). فرود آمدند is a past-tense, 3rd person plural verbal compound (فرود آمدن) 'they alighted/descended'; the verb follows the locative phrase here, a normal classical Persian word order.
segments.s653-g82: چوچیزی is an archaic fused clause meaning 'when something / as soon as something happened'. بخوردند is past 3pl of خوردن used here in the intransitive/experiential sense 'befell them' rather than literal 'ate'. و links clauses; دم بر زدند is an idiomatic verbal phrase: دم (breath/moment) + بر زدن (زدند, past 3pl) here means 'they uttered a sound / stirred (gave a cry)'.
segments.s653-g83: بخفت is an archaic past form meaning 'he slept' (past, 3rd singular). اندرَان here functions adverbially 'in/within that' referring back to the immediately preceding place, followed by سایه 'shade' and the name بوزرجمهر in apposition or genitive: 'in that shade Bozorgmehr (was)'.
segments.s653-g84: یکی is an indefinite 'a certain' or 'one (of them)' introducing a human subject. چادر اندرکشیده is a noun + participial adjective: چادر 'veil/tent' + اندرکشیده 'drawn over/ pulled in', i.e. 'a veil drawn over'. به چهر is a prepositional phrase 'upon/over the face' (به + چهر), completing the idea 'a veil drawn over the face.'
segments.s653-g85: هنوز is an adverb 'still/yet' preceding the nominal phrase. این گرانمایه is a demonstrative + epithet 'this esteemed/illustrious one'; بیدار بود is the predication 'was awake' in past tense (بود as past copula). The overall order places the adverb first, then the subject phrase, then the past copula.
segments.s653-g86: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause 'who/for that'. با او 'with him' is the prepositional phrase, به راه اندرون 'to/along the inner way' marks direction/location (به + راه + اندرون), and یار بود is the predicate 'was a companion' (یار = companion, بود = was). Grammatically the clause explains the relationship between the awake person and 'him'.
segments.s653-g87: نگه کرد is a compound verb (noun نگه 'gaze/watch' + کرد 'did') meaning 'he looked'. و links clauses. پیسه appears here as a brief adverbial/insert (the manuscript word is archaic/ambiguous); followed by یکی مار دید 'he saw a certain snake' where یکی modifies مار and دید is the past 3sg 'he saw'.
segments.s653-g88: که introduces a relative clause describing the snake. آن چادر is the direct object 'that veil/tent'. از خفته is a prepositional phrase 'from the sleeper' (از + خفته). اندر here reinforces location/coming-out (classical adverbial use), and کشید is the past 3rd person singular of کشیدن 'to pull/draw' — so 'which drew the veil away from the sleeper.' (Note: the lemma senses list supplied is not the pulling sense; here کشید is used in the normal 'to pull/draw' sense.)
segments.s653-g657: همه کار دشوارش (literally “all work his-difficult”) places the noun phrase (determiner + noun + adjective with possessive suffix) before the verb. دشوارش is دشوار + -ش (his/her/its), so the phrase means “all his difficult work.” شود is the third-person present/subjunctive/copular verb 'become' used here as a general statement: “becomes/ease.”
segments.s653-g658: ورا is the prepositional pronoun meaning “to/for him” placed before the coordinated nouns. دشمن و دوست is a coordinated subject (enemy and friend) with و‑ attached to دوست as the conjunction. یکسان is the predicate adjective “equal/identical,” and شود is again the copular/optative verb “becomes/are made.” The word order is typical Persian: indirect object/topic (ورا) + subject + predicate + verb.
segments.s653-g659: دگر گفت here means “another saying” / “another proverb says” (دگر = another). گفت functions as a verbal noun/heading “(a) saying” introducing the reported clause. کان کو is an archaic relative construction meaning roughly “he who / that one who,” and ز راه گزند = “from/the path of harm” (ز = from, راه = path, گزند = harm). Grammatically this segment introduces the conditional/relative subject of the following predicate.
segments.s653-g660: بگردد is a third‑person verb (ب‑ prefix + گردد) meaning “turns / becomes” or “will turn” in narrative; here it links the relative subject to its predicates. بزرگست is an archaic fused form of بزرگ + است (the copula -ست instead of است) meaning “is great.” و هم ارجمند adds a second predicate (“and also esteemed”), where و (and) + هم (also) precede the adjective ارجمند. The clause structure is: (he who ...) بگردد (= is/turns) بزرگست و هم ارجمند.
segments.s653-g661: چنین داد پاسخ is an idiomatic sequence: چنين (thus) + داد (gave, past of دادن) + پاسخ (answer) = “thus he gave an answer.” که introduces the subordinate clause: کردار بد = “bad deed / bad conduct” (کردار = deed, بد = bad). Word order is subject+verb+object for the reporting clause, then که + clause with the topic (کردار بد).
segments.s653-g662: بسان is a comparative particle meaning “like / as.” درختیست is درخت + ی + ست (an older 3rd‑person copula -ست) giving “is a tree / is tree‑like.” با بار بد is a prepositional phrase “with a (bad) load/fruit”: با = with, بار = load/fruit/burden, بد = bad. Together: “(bad conduct) is like a tree with bad fruit.”
segments.s653-g663: اگر introduces a conditional “if.” نرم precedes the verb and functions adverbially/adjectivally as “gently/soft.” گوید is third‑person present/subjunctive of گفتن (“says” / “speaks”), and زبان کسی = “the speech of someone” (زبان = speech/utterance, کسی = someone). The phrase means “if someone speaks gently.”
segments.s653-g664: درشتی = rudeness/roughness (the thing that would offend). به گوشش نیاید is an idiom meaning “does not come to his ear / does not reach his hearing” (نیاید is negative present/subjunctive/optative “does not come/shall not come”), with ش = his attached to گوش. بسی at the end is an adverb meaning “much / sufficiently.” Together: “rudeness will not much reach his ear.”
segments.s653-g9: شبی is a temporal adverbial placed frontally ('one night'). خفته is a past participle/adjectival predicate 'lying asleep' with no copula (typical of poetic Persian). بد here functions as an adverb/adjectival modifier of خفته ('sleeping ill/uneasily' or 'sleeping poorly'). شاه is the (understood) subject 'the king', and نوشین روان is a two-word epithet (post‑nominal adjective + noun) meaning 'sweet‑souled' that modifies شاه.
segments.s653-g10: This is a string of epithets coordinated with و that belong to the previously mentioned شاه: خردمند (wise) و بیدار (awake/alert) و دولت جوان (a prosperity/fortune that is young = 'youthful fortune'). In poetry adjectives and descriptive phrases commonly follow or attach to the noun; here they are simply listed to qualify the king.
segments.s653-g11: چنان دید is 'he saw thus/so' with دید the simple past ('saw'). درخواب is an adverbial phrase 'in a dream' modifying دید. کز is the archaic contracted relative 'که از/که ز' introducing the following locative phrase; پیش تخت = 'before the throne' or 'in the presence of the throne' (پیش as locative/prepositional).
segments.s653-g12: برستی is an older/poetic past tense verb meaning 'rose/appeared/grew up' (past action). یکی is an indefinite determiner 'a certain' and precedes the noun phrase; خسروانی is an adjective 'royal' modifying درخت 'tree'. The overall order places the verb before the indefinite noun phrase (a stylistic inversion typical in epic poetry).
segments.s653-g13: شهنشاه را uses را in a benefactive/affective sense ('for the king' / 'the king's ...'); دل is the thing affected ('heart'). بیاراستی is an archaic past-form of بیاراستن 'to adorn/beautify/make glad' (here meaning 'was made glad' or 'was adorned'). The construction places the beneficiary (شهنشاه را) before the affected noun (دل) and then the verb.
segments.s653-g14: This clause lists the objects of خواستی (past of خواستن 'to want/desire'). می‌و and رود and رامشگران are coordinated by و as the things desired; خواستی is the simple past 'he desired' (subject omitted/understood). In poetic diction می‌و often stands for wine/fruit and رود here functions as a musical/melodic term (instrument/song), while رامشگران are entertainers/comforters (minstrels).
segments.s653-g15: بر او بران is a locative/prepositional cluster meaning roughly 'upon it / on that' (بر + او + بران for emphasis/location). گاه is an adverb 'at times', and آرام و ناز are coordinated adjectives/adverbial qualities ('calm/gentle and caressing/pampering') describing the manner or state. The whole phrase sets the scene/position.
segments.s653-g16: نشستی here is an archaic/poetic past of 'نشستن' meaning 'sat' (the provided lemma/senses list corresponds to a different root, so the exact sense is supplied as -1 below). After the verb comes an indefinite noun phrase یکی تیزدندان گراز = 'a certain sharp‑toothed boar' (تیزدندان modifies گراز). The verb‑first order with the following indefinite subject is common in verse.
segments.s653-g65: This clause reports a sudden utterance: 'یکی بانگ برزد' = 'someone raised a cry/uttered a shout' (برزد is an archaic past-form here). 'برو' is an imperative interjection addressed to someone ('Go!' / 'be off'). The clause closes with the copula 'مرد است' = 'he is a man/is the man' (predicate after the imperative and report).
segments.s653-g66: Relative clause beginning with 'که' = 'that/who', giving the identity of 'the man': 'تو دفتر خویش کردی درست' = 'you have made/set (کردی) his دفتر (office/book) درست (proper/right)'. Word order is subject ('تو') + object ('دفتر خویش') + verb ('کردی درست'). The phrase 'دفتر خویش' functions as 'his office/position' here.
segments.s653-g67: 'فرستاده گفت' is a reporting clause: 'the messenger said'. 'ای' is the vocative marker ('O!'), and 'خردمند مرد' is the vocative phrase 'wise man' (adjective + noun). Together they introduce the direct address.
segments.s653-g68: This is an admonition in two parts: 'مگر داند او' = 'unless/perhaps he knows' (مگر here has an exceptive/conditional sense) + 'گرد دانا مگرد' = 'do not regard/turn him into a wise one' (مگرد is a negative/forbidding form; دانا = 'wise'). The sequence gives a conditional warning: do not call him wise unless he truly knows.
segments.s653-g69: 'غمی شد' is a simple past/resultative: 'sorrow arose / he became sorrowful'. The preposition 'ز' (= 'because of / from') marks the cause 'بوزرجمهر', and 'اوستاد' identifies the affected person as 'his master' (the master/teacher who is grieved because of Bozorgmehr).
segments.s653-g70: 'بگوی' is an exhortative/imperfect-subjunctive form meaning 'tell' (imperative/request), followed by the object phrase 'آنچ داری' = 'that which you have' (i.e. whatever knowledge/possession you have). 'بدو' = 'to him'. The sequence 'گفت یاد' reports that he said this and adds 'یاد' as a reminder/marker ('remember' / 'take heed'), so the whole amounts to 'he said to him: tell him what you have — remember to do so.'
segments.s653-g71: 'نگویم' is a first-person negative/future/ subjunctive form meaning 'I will not say / I shall not tell'. 'من' is the explicit subject; 'این گفت' = 'these words/this saying' is the object. 'جز پیش شاه' = 'except in the presence of the king' (جز = except, پیش شاه = before the king).
segments.s653-g72: 'بدانگه' = 'on that occasion / thereupon' introduces a temporal clause 'که بنشاندم پیش گاه' = 'when I seated/placed (him) before the court/royal presence' (بنشاندم is the speaker's past action 'I seated/caused to sit', پیش گاه = 'before the court/at the royal presence'). The clause specifies the particular occasion when the speaker would speak.
segments.s653-g49: بیامد is a simple past/perfect 'came' (3rd singular) describing arrival. همه گرد مرو is an adverbial phrase: همه 'everyone/all' + گرد 'around' + مرو the place name 'Merv' (word order: determiner/quantifier before the local phrase). او بجست follows as a separate clause: او 'he' (3rd person) + بجست, past of بجستن 'to seek/search' (here 'he searched' or 'he looked about'), so the clause means 'he searched among them / looked around.'
segments.s653-g50: یکی introduces an indefinite noun 'one / a certain'. موبدی is the noun 'mobed' (a Zoroastrian priest). دید is the past tense 'saw / beheld' (3rd singular). بازند and است form a small clause stating existence/presence: است is the copula 'is/was', while بازند here is archaic/poetic and not well represented by the provided sense list; together they convey 'was present / was there' (so the whole segment states 'he saw a certain mobed, who was there').
segments.s653-g51: همی is an iterative/continuous marker 'kept on / was continually'; کودکان را is the direct object with the Persian object marker را ('the children'). بیاموخت here functions as a transitive past form of آموختن 'to teach' (i.e. 'he taught / taught to'), and زند is the noun 'Zand' (religious commentary) functioning as the object/content being taught (so syntactically: 'he repeatedly taught the children the Zand').
segments.s653-g52: به introduces manner ('with / in'), followed by a series of adverbial nouns in parallel. تندی 'haste / swiftness' and خشم 'anger' describe the mood/speed; ببانگ is a compound adverb 'with a cry / by a cry' and بلند 'loud' qualifies it. The whole phrase gives the manner of the teaching: 'in haste and anger and with a loud cry.'
segments.s653-g53: یکی is an indefinite 'one / a certain'. کودکی here is used as 'a child' (noun) and مهتر is an adjective/noun meaning 'older / the elder' (so 'an elder child'). ایدر is the locative 'here' placed after the noun phrase. برش is a compact form (بر + ـش) where بر is a directional/prepositional element 'upon/at' and -ش is the third-person clitic 'him' — syntactically it indicates direction toward him ('at him' / 'upon him').
segments.s653-g54: پژوهنده is a present-participial form 'searching / inquiring' (it describes the child's action). زند in this context is a verb-form (poetic present/past of زدن 'to strike') meaning 'struck' or an action verb attached to the participle; the provided sense-list entries for زند do not reflect this verbal sense, so the mapping is uncertain. وا is an interjection/prefix often used in older/poetic Persian (here part of the verbal sequence), ستا likely contains a verbal element (but the listed senses 'to praise' don't fit well); سر 'head' and ش the possessive clitic 'his' mark the object 'his head'. Overall the clause describes the searching child acting upon the man's head ('(he) struck at his head / came upon his head').
segments.s653-g55: همی‌خواندندیش is a compound iterative verb: همی- 'kept on' + خواندند 'they called/sang' with the enclitic یش 'to him' — meaning 'they repeatedly called/sang to him' or 'they kept calling his name'. بوزرجمهر is the proper name 'Bozorgmehr' and is the direct object/name being uttered.
segments.s653-g56: نهاده is a perfect/past participle 'placed / put' (someone had placed). بران is a locative/adverbial compound 'upon that / on it' (the demonstrative 'that' + preposition). دفتر is the noun 'book / notebook' (the thing on/into which something was placed). از introduces a source/partitive 'from / of', followed by مهر چهر: مهر here is best read as 'seal / impression' (or figuratively 'likeness') and چهر 'face' — so از مهر چهر means 'of the seal/likeness of the face.' Grammatically the phrase says '(he) had placed, in that book, an impression/likeness of (his) face.'
segments.s653-g737: کسی کو = an archaic relative phrase: کسی (someone) + کو (classical/poetic relative particle here meaning 'who' or 'that'). به + بادافره is an adverbial/prepositional phrase: به here has a by/‑ly sense ('in a ... way' or 'with ...ness') and بادافره is a poetic compound 'wind‑borne / wind‑like, fickle'. اندرخورست is the predicate (copula + adjective): اندرخور + -ست 'is of that sort / is fitting to be described as such'.
segments.s653-g738: کجا here is an archaic/discourse particle introducing the following characterisation (not literal 'where'). بدنژادست is a compound adjective + copula: بد‑نژاد + -ست 'is ill‑born'. و is the simple coordinating conjunction 'and'. بد گوهرست is a second predication: بد (bad, base) + گوهر + -ست, i.e. 'is of bad essence / base in character' (گوهر used in a predicate meaning 'essence / nature').
segments.s653-g739: کند is a classical third‑person verb meaning 'does/makes/causes' (here glossed 'has [him] put'); Persian allows V–S order in older poetry, so the verb appears first. شاه is the subject 'the king'. دور از میان گروه is a locative/prepositional phrase 'away from among the group/company' (دور + از + میان + گروه).
segments.s653-g740: بی‌آزار is an adverbial/qualifying adjective 'harmless, inoffensive' describing how the king acts. تا introduces a purposive/result clause 'so that'; it governs the following negative subjunctive. ز + و here represent the poetic contraction زِ او (= زـ او → زو) 'from him / of him' (the list split it into two tokens). نگردد is the negative present/subjunctive of گشت/گردیدن 'become' (i.e. 'does not become / shall not turn into') and ستوه is the predicate 'weary / vexed'.
segments.s653-g741: هران is the fused classical form of هر آن 'every/each' and begins a free relative 'whoever'. کس که باشد = 'person who is' (که introduces the relative clause). باشد is the classical subjunctive/present form of بودن used in relative clauses to mean 'is / may be' rather than an imperative. به زندان شاه is the locative phrase 'in the king's prison' (به used with place).
segments.s653-g742: This segment is elliptical/condensed: گنهکار (guilty/sinner) is stated and the particle گر (classical 'if' / concessive) is fused or implied after it, setting up a contrast; the rest مردم بیگناه 'the people [are] innocent' is a terse nominal clause with the predicate بیگناه. In older Persian such conditionals and contrasts are often left without an explicit copula (بودن is implied).
segments.s653-g743: به فرمان یزدان is an agentive/ablative phrase 'by the command of God' (به = by/under). بباید is the classical impersonal modal 'it is necessary / one must', used here to introduce the required action. گشاد is an adjective 'wide / open' used predicatively — in context the sense is passive/necessitative: '[the prison] must be opened wide' (the verb 'to open' is implied in classical compressions).
segments.s653-g744: بزند is a subjunctive/optative third‑person form of زدن 'to strike / to open' meaning 'he should strike/open'. و is the coordinating 'and'. باست is an archaic/exclamatory verb meaning roughly 'let it suffice / let it stand / cease' (here 'let it remain' or 'let that be enough'). آنچ = آن‌چ 'that which' (relative/neutral noun) and کرده‌ست is the perfect 'has done'; یاد 'remembrance' functions as the object/goal: 'let that which he has done be remembered'. The clause follows classical Persian terseness where verbs and subjects can be elliptical.
segments.s653-g497: نخست is an adverbial introducer meaning "first." آنکه here introduces a subordinate clause (literary/archaic "that/what" or "he who"). یابی is a jussive/subjunctive form of یابیدن — literally "you should find/learn" — and takes بدو (dative "to him") plus آرزوی (the object "wish/desire"); the verb appears before its object due to poetic inversion.
segments.s653-g498: ز is the poetic preposition "from/through." هستیش is a possessive/nominalized form referring to "his being/existence/essence." پیدا + کنی is a periphrastic expression: پیدا is adjectival "apparent/visible" and کنی (subjunctive of کردن) is "you make/bring about," so together "you make apparent/from which you show." نیک‌خوی is the predicate adjective "good-natured/kind-natured."
segments.s653-g499: وگر is the conditional "but if." چون here links temporal/conditional sense roughly "when/as/if," and بباید is the impersonal/obligatory verb "it is necessary/ought to be." نیاری is the noun "companion/ally" (dative/target) and به کار is a prepositional phrase "for the work/task." The clause thus states a conditional: "if (when) it is necessary and you have no companion for the task..." (poetic word order and ellipsis of an expected verb follow).
segments.s653-g500: همان is the demonstrative "that/the same." سنگ وهم گوهر is a compact noun phrase with poetic coordination: سنگ (stone) followed by وهم (illusion) and گوهر (gem/jewel); شاهوار is a postpositive adjective qualifying the phrase, meaning "regal/lofty." The whole phrase stands as the thing presented/offered in the conditional clause (the verb is elliptical).
segments.s653-g501: دگر here means "another/other" or "again" in a narrative sense. گفت is the past/action word "said/spoke" (classical style). با introduces the instrumental phrase "with" and تاج و نام بلند is a coordinated noun phrase "crown and lofty name/reputation" where بلند is a postpositive adjective "lofty." The whole phrase sets the setting/stance of the speech: "Another said, with crown and lofty name..."
segments.s653-g502: کرا is an archaic interrogative/relative meaning "who/whom." خوانی is the second-person verbal form (here functioning as an interrogative verb: "will you call/summon/choose?"). از introduces the source/selection "from among." خسروان is the plural "kings/rulers," and سودمند is a postpositive adjective "useful/beneficial." The phrase asks, in effect, "Whom will you call from among the kings as someone useful?"
segments.s653-g503: چنین is the adverb "thus/so." داد is the past verb "gave" and پاسـخ is the object "answer/reply." کزان is a literary/archaic particle linking the reply to the addressee (literally "to/from which" in classical usage) and شهریار is the addressee/title "sovereign/king." The clause therefore means "He gave such an answer to that king..."
segments.s653-g504: که introduces a relative clause "who/that." ایمن is an adjective "safe/secure/protected." بود is the past copula "was." مرد is the noun "man/person," and پرهیزکار is a postpositive adjective meaning "pious/abstinent." Together they form the relative clause describing the king: "who was a secure, pious man."
segments.s653-g665: بدان is an imperative 'know' formed from دانستن with the prefixed ب- (ب + دان). کز is the subordinating conjunction 'that/from which' introducing the clause. زبانست is a fused poetic form meaning 'is (from) the tongue' (زبان + است). گوشش is 'his ear' (گوش + enclitic ش for 'his'). به رنج here means 'to/into pain' or 'hurt' (i.e., his ear is made sore/offended).
segments.s653-g666: چو here works as 'if' (conditional). رنجش is 'his resentment/being offended'. نجویی is jویی (seeking) with a prefixed ن- giving a negative sense here ('do not seek' / 'do not provoke') — the clause therefore reads 'if you do not seek his offense'. سخن را is the direct object 'the speech/your words' (را marks definiteness/object). بسنج is an imperative 'weigh/measure/consider' (addressing the speaker/reader).
segments.s653-g667: همان is 'that/the same' pointing back to something just mentioned. کم سخن is a tight noun phrase 'scant/brief speech' (کم modifying سخن). مرد here is simply 'man/person' and خسروپرست is a compound epithet 'one who worships the king' i.e. a flatterer or courtier — it describes the man who utters the brief speech.
segments.s653-g668: جز ... نشاید ... نشست is an idiomatic clause: جز 'except', از پیش گاهش 'at/from its beforehand time' (پیش + گاه + enclitic ش 'its' = 'its proper moment'). نشاید is an impersonal/formal negative 'it is not fitting' and نشست here denotes 'a sitting/occasion/utterance' (i.e. the event of speaking). Together: 'it is not proper that the utterance take place except at its appointed time.'
segments.s653-g669: دگر (here 'another' or 'one other') begins a new class/example. از + بدیهای is the prepositional phrase 'from the evils' (بدیهای is plural with the Persian plural -های). نا + آمده = 'not yet come/unarrived' — a poetic way to say 'unforeseen' or 'not-yet-arrived'. The whole phrase = 'one who avoids evils that have not yet come.'
segments.s653-g670: گریزد is a 3rd-person present/future-form 'he flees/escapes'. چو = 'as/like' introduces a simile. از دام مرغ و دده literally 'from the trap a bird and dede' — here دام is 'trap', مرغ is 'bird' and و is 'and'. دده is an older/poetic word (literally 'elder' in the listed senses) but in context is part of a stock simile; overall the clause says 'he flees as a bird flees from a snare (and its wielder/decoy)'.
segments.s653-g671: This nominal clause names 'three others' (سه دیگر). که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'. بر بد توانا بود = a poetic ordering meaning 'was strong/capable toward/at evil' (بر here 'against/toward', بد = 'evil', توانا = 'capable/powerful', بود = past/copula 'was'). So: 'three others who, if they are powerful in evil...' (poetic word order).
segments.s653-g672: بپرهیزد is a jussive/3rd‑person verb 'he should beware/avoid' (classical Persian often uses this conjunctive form). ار = 'if'. ویژه دانا بود = 'he were/was especially/particularly wise' (ویژه as 'especially/specially', دانا 'wise', بود past copula). The whole conditional: 'He should be avoided, if he were especially wise' — i.e. 'if one of them is able at evil, even a discerning man should shun him.'
